Title: Data scientist Business Banking NL
Description:
Would you like to shape how ING Business Banking understands and influences customer behaviour across the end‑to‑end customer journey? Are you energized by complex data, advanced analytics, and turning insights into real business impact? Then this role is for you!

The team

The Center of Expertise Pricing Business Banking is part of Business Development, a central BBNL department working for all business banking products and segments. With the strategic product-price positioning, we impact the results of the Domestic Bank Business Banking Netherlands, and customer behaviour, credit and market risk, and perception of our customers and society. As CoE Pricing we have a holistic view on Business Banking products and segments covering savings, payments and lending for small to midsized corporates.

Customer behavioural modelling and Insights Chapter

You will be part of the Customer behavioural modelling and Insights Chapter. This is a newly established squad designed to understand our customers’ behaviour. The focus areas are on behaviour modelling, customer segmentation and price elasticity. You will have the opportunity to build analytical solutions, shape frameworks and best practices from the ground up.

Role And Responsibilities



Develop advanced analytics solutions and models to drive data-informed commercial decisions across Business Banking.
Collaborate with subject matter experts to bring models into production.
Support business in understanding customer behaviour through data.
Actively engage with stakeholders.




How To Succeed



Proactive and result driven.
Highly motivated to solve complex problems with a continuous learning mindset.
Someone who enjoys working cooperatively with others as part of a team.
Comfortable in dynamic multi-matrix environments.




Required Skills



At least 3 years of experience developing advanced analytics solutions, ideally in the financial sector.
Strong experience writing clean, readable, well documented Python and SQL code suitable for production environments.
Affinity with panel data / time series models, linear and non-linear regressions, time series analysis, clustering, and hypothesis testing.
Able to tell a clear story with data and to explain findings to business stakeholders.
Hands-on experience with PySpark, BigQuery, SAS, MS Azure DevOps and GCP is a plus.
Knowledge of pricing domain is a strong advantage.
Solid educational background in mathematics, statistics or econometrics (Master’s or PhD degree level preferred).
Excellent written and oral communication skills in both technical and business contexts. Fluency in spoken and written English. Dutch is a plus.




Rewards And Benefits

We want to make sure that it’s possible for you to strike the right balance between your career and your private life. Find out more about our employment conditions.

The Benefits Of Working With Us At ING Include



25-28 vacation days depending on contract
Pension scheme
13th month salary
8% Holiday payment
Hybrid working
Personal growth and challenging work with endless possibilities
An informal working environment with innovative colleagues




About Us

Curious about how ING empowers people and businesses to move forward?

Discover what we do and what we can offer you.

Questions?

Please visit our Frequently Asked Questions section to find some answers on questions you might have. If you're questions are not answered, you can also contact the recruiter attached to the advertisement. Want to apply directly? Please upload your CV and motivation letter by clicking the ‘Apply’ button

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
About The Role And The Xomnia Culture

At Xomnia, machine learning is more than model building - it’s about engineering intelligent systems that deliver real value. As a Machine Learning Engineer, you’ll work alongside passionate consultants to help our clients unlock the full potential of their data.

From advancing cancer research to real-time fraud detection and electricity grid optimization, we work on projects that make a difference. Our culture thrives on curiosity, shared ownership, and the courage to challenge assumptions. We value people who enjoy experimenting, sharing knowledge, and working together to create AI solutions that last.

Are you ready to take on this challenge?

In this role, you’ll develop and deploy machine learning models across diverse domains - from healthcare to logistics and finance. Whether you’re designing recommender systems, deploying models to the cloud, or helping clients adopt MLOps practices, your consultancy skills will bring together technical excellence and business impact.

Your role


Design and implement robust ML models that solve real-world problems.
Build end-to-end ML pipelines - from feature engineering to deployment and monitoring.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to turn business needs into scalable solutions.
Apply MLOps best practices such as CI/CD, versioning, testing, and automation.
Deploy models on cloud platforms like Azure, AWS, or GCP.
Work with tools like MLflow, Airflow, Docker, Kubernetes, and Spark.
Stay up to date with the latest techniques, including Generative AI, and explore their application.
Guide clients in building internal ML capabilities and following engineering best practices.
Share your expertise through mentorship, demos, and internal learning sessions.


What You Bring To The Table


Preferable +/- 2-5 years of relevant experience in ML engineering, data science, or similar roles.
A degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or a related technical field.
Strong coding skills in Python and experience with cloud environments (Azure, AWS, or GCP).
Solid understanding of statistics and machine learning fundamentals.
Experience building and deploying production-grade ML systems.
Familiarity with Docker, CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure-as-code, and orchestration tools.
A consulting mindset: you enjoy listening, collaborating, and turning needs into solutions.
Fluent in English and Dutch.
A drive to learn, improve, and share knowledge - both internally and with the broader tech community.


What’s in it for you?

We offer more than a job - we offer a platform for growth:


A monthly salary between €3.800 - €6.200 per month (based on 40 hours and excluding vacation allowance)
€5.000 annual development budget
25 vacation days to fully recharge
Secondary benefits such as pension, flexible working options (in terms working location) and the possibility to take unpaid leave
Memorable company trips (Berlin this year!)
The opportunity to grow along with our success through Stock Appreciation Rights


At Xomnia, we believe that collaboration, knowledge sharing, and personal development are the keys to our success. That’s why we offer various trainings through our ‘Learning Labs’ and organize a yearly Health Month. We also regularly enjoy boat trips through the Amsterdam canals with our own boat, and there is always a healthy (free) lunch and a bar at the office.

Become a Xomnian!

At Xomnia, we believe in using data and AI to tackle real-world challenges. With a team of passionate experts from 20+ nationalities and over 30% female representation, we celebrate diversity and welcome different perspectives.

We Are Proud Of Our Core Values

We are challengers, we embrace agility, and we always share.

Want to be part of this and take the next step in your career?

Apply now or reach out to ezra.lammers@xomnia.com. We look forward to meeting you.

Title: Software Engineer Frontend (React)
Description:
We’re looking for an enthusiastic and experienced Software Engineer Frontend. In this role, you’ll play a key part in developing the next generation of our TraXwise platform, the online platform in the AgXeed ecosystem that helps farmers embrace the future of agriculture with smart, autonomous technology. TraXwise is the application where our customers manage, plan and monitor their machines, fields and tasks.




What you’ll do as Software Engineer Frontend at AgXeed

As Software Engineer Frontend, you build fast, user-friendly, reliable web applications using modern techniques (React) and best practices.

You’ll be part of a young and growing dev team. TraXwise is actively being built: you’ll have the opportunity to build from the ground up and make real architectural decisions. You’ll also think along on tools and processes, ensuring the platform is not just functional, but future-proof. You keep a close eye on performance, analyze bottlenecks with your team, and continuously improve stability, speed and user experience.




You’ll be working with the following tools, technologies and processes:

React, Vite
Typescript / Javascript
HTML / CSS (SASS/SCSS/CSS-in-JS)
API-driven development (HTTP, REST, optionally Websockets, GraphQL)
Unit tests and end-to-end-tests (Playwright)
Storybook
Mapbox
Git
CI/CD (Github Actions)
Figma




What we’re looking for:

You have at least 3 years of experience in modern frontend development with React in a professional environment
You have experience with state management, API integrations and working with real-time data
You care about code quality and show that in your structure, tests and reviews
You use AI (LLMs) effectively, responsibly and sustainably in your work (no vibecoding)
You have solid communication skills in English;
You have knowledge of UX/UI patterns and accessibility
You’re available for at least 32 hours per week
Experience with map integrations and visualisations (such as Google Maps, Mapbox), GeoJSON etc. is a plus




What AgXeed offers?

A competitive salary
27 vacation days and 13 roster-free days (based on a 40-hour work week)
Travel allowance
An enthusiastic team in a a scale-up environment with a lot of freedom and responsibility
Laptop of your choice (Mac/Windows/Linux)
Pension plan participation via PME
A workspace at our office in Grubbenvorst (Limburg, the Netherlands), with the option to work from home 1–2 days per week




Interested?

We’d love to hear from you! Send your CV and a short motivation to career@agxeed.com




Acquisition in response to this vacancy is not appreciated.

We’re looking for an enthusiastic and experienced Software Engineer Frontend. In this role, you’ll play a key part in developing the next generation of our TraXwise platform, the online platform in the AgXeed ecosystem that helps farmers embrace the future of agriculture with smart, autonomous technology. TraXwise is the application where our customers manage, plan and monitor their machines, fields and tasks.


What you’ll do as Software Engineer Frontend at AgXeed

As Software Engineer Frontend, you build fast, user-friendly, reliable web applications using modern techniques (React) and best practices.

You’ll be part of a young and growing dev team. TraXwise is actively being built: you’ll have the opportunity to build from the ground up and make real architectural decisions. You’ll also think along on tools and processes, ensuring the platform is not just functional, but future-proof. You keep a close eye on performance, analyze bottlenecks with your team, and continuously improve stability, speed and user experience.


You’ll be working with the following tools, technologies and processes:

  • React, Vite
  • Typescript / Javascript
  • HTML / CSS (SASS/SCSS/CSS-in-JS)
  • API-driven development (HTTP, REST, optionally Websockets, GraphQL)
  • Unit tests and end-to-end-tests (Playwright)
  • Storybook
  • Mapbox
  • Git
  • CI/CD (Github Actions)
  • Figma


What we’re looking for:

  • You have at least 3 years of experience in modern frontend development with React in a professional environment
  • You have experience with state management, API integrations and working with real-time data
  • You care about code quality and show that in your structure, tests and reviews
  • You use AI (LLMs) effectively, responsibly and sustainably in your work (no vibecoding)
  • You have solid communication skills in English;
  • You have knowledge of UX/UI patterns and accessibility
  • You’re available for at least 32 hours per week
  • Experience with map integrations and visualisations (such as Google Maps, Mapbox), GeoJSON etc. is a plus


What AgXeed offers?

  • A competitive salary
  • 27 vacation days and 13 roster-free days (based on a 40-hour work week)
  • Travel allowance
  • An enthusiastic team in a a scale-up environment with a lot of freedom and responsibility
  • Laptop of your choice (Mac/Windows/Linux)
  • Pension plan participation via PME
  • A workspace at our office in Grubbenvorst (Limburg, the Netherlands), with the option to work from home 1–2 days per week


Interested?

We’d love to hear from you! Send your CV and a short motivation to career@agxeed.com


Acquisition in response to this vacancy is not appreciated.

Title: Data Scientist AI
HTML:
Company Description

Varrlyn is a consulting firm which was founded in 2010 and is based in The Netherlands. We help financial institutions with system implementations and provide some serious application support, concentrating on the way they process transactions and deploy reporting applications.

Varrlyn has a proven track record: we have worked for financial institutions in Amsterdam, Brussels, London, Paris, New York, Frankfurt, and Sydney. We help improve cost efficiency, simplify application architecture, optimize time to market, enhance straight-through processing, and ensure compliance with financial regulations. In other words: You do the deal, we’ll do the project.

Job Description

The Responsible GenAI squad drives the development and implementation of Generative AI solutions that enables the client to apply AI effectively in practice. These solutions enhance the customer journey, streamline data processing, answer internal staff queries and generate insights and reports to facilitate workflows. To accelerate innovation, the team also develops APIs that help other teams build GenAI solutions faster and more efficiently. 

As Data Scientist, you will contribute to building, monitoring and optimizing the performance of GenAI solutions across diverse use cases within our client. You’ll be at the forefront of creating tools that tackle complex challenges, improve efficiency and unlock new opportunities. Your work will involve exploring topics such as responsible GenAI, performance monitoring, red teaming and evaluating GenAI models for fairness and bias. 

Practical examples

  • Work directly with (internal) customers to learn how processes and systems work;
  • Be accountable for building responsible and cost effective GenAI solutions for millions of customers;
  • Enabling the performance and monitoring of the reusable GenAI building blocks can be used by 100+ tech teams;
  • Design and build responsible and cost effective GenAI solutions;
  • Collaborate with team members to solve critical or complex problems.

Top Responsibilities

  • Design and build responsible GenAI via performance monitoring, red teaming and evaluating GenAI models for fairness and bias;
  • Contribute to building, monitoring and optimizing the performance of GenAI solutions;
  • Join-forces with your peers, experts and stakeholders to provide business value to our clients customers.

Qualifications

Technical skills and experience:

  • You have at least 5 or more years of experience working as a Data Scientist;
  • You have strong programming skills (software engineering experience is a plus) and are proficient in Python;
  • You have a solid understanding of NLP and LLMs and experience in building Generative AI applications;

Additional Information

Acquisition in response to this vacancy is not appreciated.

Title: AI Engineer
HTML:
BAS Group is een AWS Enterprise-organisatie die volop inzet op AI-gedreven automatisering. We hebben inmiddels drie AI-agents succesvol gebouwd en geïmplementeerd binnen kritieke bedrijfsprocessen, en staan aan het begin van een bredere transformatie waarbij AI-agents een sleutelrol gaan spelen in efficiency, klantbediening en interne automatisering.

Als AI Engineer richt je je primair op het bouwen, uitbreiden en operationaliseren van AI-agents. Je werkt op het snijvlak van engineering, LLM-technologie, MLOps/LLMOps en data. Je ontwikkelt systemen die zelfstandig taken uitvoeren, processen automatiseren en geïntegreerd samenwerken met interne platformen zoals CRM, ERP, orderverwerking, digitale diensten en supportprocessen. Je experimenteert niet alleen, maar zorgt ervoor dat AI-oplossingen productie-klaar, schaalbaar en betrouwbaar worden uitgerold en onderhouden.

Wat je precies gaat doen bij BAS Group

In deze rol bouw je nieuwe AI-agents die bedrijfsprocessen automatiseren en teams ondersteunen — van sales en operations tot support en marketing. Je verbetert en onderhoudt de bestaande drie agents, maakt ze schaalbaar en slimmer, en zorgt dat ze naadloos aansluiten op interne workflows.

Je ontwikkelt robuuste agent-architecturen voor taakautomatisering, documentverwerking, decision-assistants en interne workflow-optimalisatie, waarbij je moderne agent-frameworks zoals LangGraph inzet om multi-step reasoning, tools en bedrijfslogica te orkestreren.

Daarnaast bouw je integraties met onze kernsystemen (CRM, ERP, orderplatformen, supporttools) en zorg je dat agenten volgens best-practice LLMOps-principes — zoals monitoring, evaluation, versioning, routing en safety-mechanismen — betrouwbaar draaien in productie.

Je werkt nauw samen met Data Engineers, Analytics Engineers en het AI-team in een AWS-first omgeving, en deployt alles schaalbaar en herhaalbaar met Terraform, Git en CI/CD.

Wij bieden jou

  • Een aantrekkelijk salaris op basis van ervaring en motivatie;
  • 25 vakantiedagen plus de mogelijkheid om extra dagen te kopen;
  • Spaar- en pensioenregeling, reiskostenvergoeding;
  • Deelname aan het BAS Fit sportprogramma, vers fruit op kantoor en diverse sportactiviteiten;
  • Een prettige werkomgeving met fijne collega's en een platte organisatiestructuur;
  • Doorgroei- en trainingsmogelijkheden voor een lange termijn carrière;
  • Teamuitjes, borrels en het jaarlijkse BAS Jaarfeest.


Jouw specificaties

  • 1+ jaar ervaring als AI Engineer of 2+ jaar als Data Scientist/Data Engineer met AI-focus;
  • Sterke Python-ervaring;
  • Terraform (must) en Git (must);
  • Ervaring met AI-agents of LLM-workflows;
  • Kennis van/affiniteit met frameworks zoals LangGraph;
  • Begrip van LLMOps (evaluatie, prompts, monitoring, versioning, routing);
  • Ervaring met integraties tussen AI-modellen en operationele systemen;
  • Ervaring met AWS-deployments (Lambda, ECS, Bedrock, Sagemaker);
  • Affiniteit met automation, reliability en productie-AI;
  • In staat om je eigen data beschikbaar te stellen;
  • Basiskennis van API's en dbt.


Wij zijn BAS!

We zijn actief sinds 1962. Sindsdien vestigden wij onze naam als een betrouwbare kracht in de Automotive. Als familiebedrijf zijn wij wereldwijd actief in kopen, onderhouden en verkopen van vrachtwagens, trekkers, trailers, bouwmachines, bedrijfswagens en landbouwmachines. Al onze kennis en ervaring verwerkten we daarnaast in de ontwikkeling van basworld.com. Hier verbinden we kopers en verkopers met elkaar en helpen we hen om snel, gemakkelijk en veilig te handelen.

Onze ambitie voor BAS kent geen grenzen. Rijd jij voorop om BAS naar het volgende niveau te brengen?

Samengevat: bij BAS gaan we altijd vol gas en is geen dag hetzelfde. Met meer dan 1.000 collega's proberen wij onszelf iedere dag een stukje beter te maken voor onze klanten. Daarbij staan Brabantse nuchterheid, passie, teamwork en ondernemerschap voorop.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
This Is What We Offer You


Salary: Gross monthly salary between EUR 3,516 and EUR 5,021 (scale 07) for a 36-hour work week.
Extras: a thirteenth month, 8% holiday allowance, and a 10% Employee Benefit Budget.
Development budget: EUR 1,400 development budget per year for your growth and development.
Hybrid working: a balance between home and office work (possible for most roles).
Pension: decide for yourself the amount of your personal contribution.


Or view all our benefits.

Junior Data Scientist - Data Analytics Early Career Program

Ready to turn your programming skills into models that help protect millions of financial transactions?

You & your role

As Junior Data Scientist, you know the secrets of transforming complex data into reliable insights and solutions. You will work on a key part of Rabobank’s transaction monitoring models and help improve the quality and effectiveness of these solutions.

You will spend much of your time programming, testing and improving components of these models. At the same time, you will work closely with business stakeholders to understand requirements, explain outcomes and help deliver valuable features. Because the teams operate in a highly collaborative environment, communication and teamwork are essential parts of your role.

"We are looking for people who enjoy working together, are enthusiastic about data science and are proactive in taking ownership of their work and development."


Joppe Boekestijn, Hiring Manager.


In short


Improve and test parts of our Transaction Monitoring models using Python (PySpark) and existing code bases.
Present model outcomes and technical solutions to stakeholders and explain the impact of your work.
Collaborate with colleagues across squads to develop and implement new features.


You & your future

You will work on your career by developing and improving transaction monitoring models together with experienced colleagues. You will join one of our data science squads, work in a highly collaborative environment and contribute to solutions that support the bank in managing financial risks. You will combine technical development with stakeholder interaction and learn how data science creates impact within a large organisation.

And then? After successfully completing the programme, you will continue to develop yourself as a Data Scientist within Rabobank. You will have built a strong professional network, gained practical experience and developed the skills needed to grow your career further.

You will join a team of around 10 colleagues, including Data Scientists, Product Owners, Scrum Masters and other specialists. Depending on your strengths and interests, you may work in a squad with a stronger programming focus or in a squad with more stakeholder interaction.

Practical


Length Data Analytics Early Career Program: 18 months.
After which you will continue to work with us within this role.
Hours per week: 40
Salary: €4007,80


Your growth in the Data Analytics Early Career Program

At Rabobank, we believe real growth comes from doing. From celebrating successes and learning from the moments that could have gone differently. That is why we offer you the perfect balance between freedom and guidance.

The Data Analytics Early Career Program is all about your development. You will intentionally invest in discovering who you are, what drives you, and how you want to grow as a professional. You will be challenged to step outside your comfort zone and explore where your strengths truly lie, sometimes in places you did not expect yourself. Through these experiences, you will learn where you can make the greatest impact.

And you will not do it alone. Every Friday, we come together at the office with the Data Analytics community. In an open and informal environment, we share knowledge, experiences, insights, and learn with and from each other.

From day one, you will build a strong and valuable network that will benefit you throughout your career. Throughout the entire programme, you can count on intensive support from experienced colleagues. Whether you want to specialise in a specific field or explore what energises you most, your growth always comes first.

Learning From Each Other

Development is our top priority. Because the best way to learn is by doing, through mistakes and successes. At Rabobank we offer you the right balance between freedom and supervision. To develop talents you never thought you possessed. To specialize in a professional field or explore the things you are good at and enjoy doing. During your Data Analytics Early Career Program you can count on us for excellent supervision. You will work with experienced colleagues and have lots of contact with young professionals.

Work on your own development & shape the world around you

For us, your development and that of society go hand in hand. That is why we want to invest in you and work together towards a better world. We summarize this in one sentence: “At Rabobank, you work on yourself and the world around you at the same time.”

This is reflected in your personal development budget, our hybrid work environment, and a good balance between your work and home life. You can work on banking matters for our private and business clients, but also on societal issues. Together we work towards our mission: Growing a better world together.

We believe everyone should feel welcome, valued and able to be themselves. Diversity, equity and inclusion are important parts of our culture, because different perspectives help us make better decisions and create more impact.

You and your talent


A Masters’ university degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science or another technical field.
Experience with Python programming through studies, internships, projects or work experience.
Affinity with software development and building technical solutions. Strong collaboration skills.
A proactive mindset and willingness to take initiative.
Enthusiasm for learning, developing and working with others.


You and the job application process

We believe in the power of difference. Bringing together people's differences is what makes us an even better bank. What can you bring to Rabobank? We cannot wait to hear from you.

So get in touch straightaway (and certainly before the closing date on 16-09-2026.


Questions about the role? Contact Joppe Boekestijn, Hiring Manager, via Joppe.Boekestijn@rabobank.nl.
Any questions about working at Rabobank and the process? Contact Jelle Nelemans, Junior Corporate Recruiter, via Jelle.Nelemans@rabobank.nl.
Interviews will be held via MS Teams and at our offices in Utrecht.
Assessments are part of the application process.
We look forward to meeting you in person during the selection days.
If you are selected for an interview, Bo, our virtual assistant, will reach out to you via SMS and email to schedule the interview.
A security check is part of the process.
We respect your privacy.

We believe in the power of difference. Bringing together people's differences is what makes us an even better bank. What can you bring to Rabobank? We cannot wait to hear from you.

  • Questions about the role? Contact Joppe Boekestijn, Hiring Manager, via Joppe.Boekestijn@rabobank.nl.

Title: AI/Software Internship
Description:
What To Expect
Start date: September 2026 

Location: Tilburg

Duration: 6 months

Time type: Full-time (40 hours a week)

NOTE: YOU MUST BE ENROLLED IN EDUCATION DURING THE ENTIRE INTERNSHIP TO BE CONSIDERED.

The AI / Software Intern is responsible for helping move the GOBI team's data and application stack — which today runs the dashboards, web applications, and data pipelines behind Tesla's remanufacturing operations in Lathrop, Tilburg, and Buffalo — toward a modern agentic data platform. Through this role we will get some help maintaining our data pipelines and increasing their reliability, as well as integrating both existing and new dashboards and visualizations with AI agents to extend them and generate automatic insights and reports.

The intern will build a semantic / metrics catalog over the existing cache schemas, develop an agent loop on inference endpoints, wrap operational APIs as MCP tools so agents can both read and act, integrate the result into the existing frontend, and put evals and observability in place before any agent reaches production. They will work on the GOBI ETL, visualizations, backend and frontend, and collaborate with other existing stakeholders and teams.

What You'll Do


Build a unified semantic / metrics catalog over the existing cache schemas by walking current dashboards, ETLs and ELTs
Evolve the team's text-to-SQL design into an agent loop with tool calling, multi-step planning, self-correcting SQL and citations back to source tables. Wrap operational APIs as MCP tools so agents can both read and act
Integrate the resulting agent and data products into the existing React + Vite + Tesla Design System frontend
Build a fixed eval set and unit tests, and wire up traces / accuracy / latency dashboards before anything is shipped to production
Contribute to the underlying FastAPI / SQLAlchemy services, Airflow DAGs and CI/CD pipelines as needed to support the above
Document the semantic layer, agent tools and evals so the rest of the GOBI team can extend them



What You'll Bring


Currently enrolled in Computer Science, AI Engineering, ML Engineering, Data Engineering or a similar field
Strong Python and SQL — able to read a non-trivial FastAPI service or a long ETL script and refactor across them
Hands-on experience with at least one modern data warehouse / query engine (Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Trino/Presto, Vertica)
Has built something with LLMs beyond a chat wrapper — RAG, function/tool calling, an agent loop, or an MCP server
Working knowledge of CI/CD, Git, Docker and Kubernetes
Good written and spoken English, with the ability to communicate technical trade-offs clearly
Nice to have: dbt / Cube / Malloy, LangGraph / OpenAI Agents SDK / MCP, React + Vite, LLM evals & observability (Langfuse, Phoenix, Braintrust), or a time-series data analysis background



, Tesla

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Als data scientist bij Trading & Optimization werk je op het snijvlak van energie en AI, op een plek waar je werk direct effect heeft op het energienet. Jouw forecast om 13:00:00 stuurt om 13:00:01 een handelsbeslissing aan, en de impact zie je realtime terug op onze dashboards. Je bouwt niet één geïsoleerd model, maar een samenhangend geheel waarin probabilistische forecasting, optimalisatie en aansturing elkaar versterken. De feedbackloop is kort en meetbaar, wat je vandaag bouwt heeft snel impact.

Je werkt in een team waar data scientists, engineers en traders dicht op elkaar zitten. Er is weinig hiërarchie en veel ruimte voor eigen initiatief, maar ook duidelijke verwachtingen over eigenaarschap: jouw modellen draaien live en je bent zelf de eerste die ingrijpt als er iets misgaat. AI-tooling zoals Claude Code is volledig ingeburgerd en versnelt je werk. Zo draagt het team direct bij aan plan Zero: elke MWh die we slimmer verhandelen of efficiënter in een batterij laden of ontladen, is een MWh die niet uit een gascentrale hoeft te komen.

Jouw impact bij Zonneplan


Jij ontwerpt en bouwt de modellen die onze energiehandel aansturen. Je werkt met meerdere gekoppelde systemen tegelijk: weersvoorspellingen, marktprijzen (day-ahead, intraday, onbalans), klantgedrag en het fysieke gedrag van ons VPP (Virtual Power Plant) van batterijen, laadpalen en zonnepanelen. Je denkt in samenhang en bouwt modellen die elkaar versterken.
Jij brengt modellen naar productie en houdt ze scherp. Samen met engineers zorg je voor een vlekkeloze deployment. Je pakt eigenaarschap over je modellen: je monitort performance, waarborgt betrouwbaarheid en optimaliseert continu.
Jij vertaalt modelkwaliteit naar commercieel resultaat. Jouw werk verlaagt direct de tradingkosten binnen ons energieportfolio en verhoogt het rendement op de Nexus-thuisbatterij. Door nauwkeurige voorspellingen kunnen we klanten een zonnebonus geven in plaats van een terugleverboete, omdat we weten wanneer hun stroom waardevol is.
Jij bereidt onze apparaten voor op een toekomst zonder salderen. Door slim in te spelen op marktontwikkelingen en eigen verbruik zorg je ervoor dat klanten maximaal profiteren. Je vertaalt veranderende marktregels naar concrete modelaanpassingen die direct waarde opleveren voor onze klanten.
Jij brengt nieuwe ontwikkelingen naar de praktijk. Je volgt de nieuwste ontwikkelingen in AI en machine learning en vertaalt die proactief naar concrete experimenten. Denk aan nieuwe benaderingen voor probabilistische forecasting. Liever een werkend prototype dan een slide.


Dit krijg je van ons


Een salaris tussen €4.000 en €6.000 bruto per maand, afhankelijk van je ervaring en senioriteit.
25 vakantiedagen (met de mogelijkheid om er meer bij te kopen) en reiskostenvergoeding.
Een Claude Code Max 20x abonnement als secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarde. Je zet het in voor je dagelijkse werk en mag het ook gebruiken voor side projects en persoonlijke ontwikkeling.
Een persoonlijk keuzebudget voor sport, boodschappen of iets anders dat bij je past.
Vanaf dag één investeren we in jouw groei met de Zonneplan Academy, goede tools en ruimte om te blijven leren. Je kunt doorgroeien naar senior data scientist met specialisatie in energiehandel, batterij-optimalisatie of forecasting.
Een pragmatische, no-nonsense werksfeer waarin korte lijnen de norm zijn. Je zit dicht op traders en engineers.


Dit neem jij mee


Je hebt minimaal 3 jaar ervaring als data scientist en sterke fundamenten in statistiek en machine learning, met aantoonbare ervaring in forecasting of time series.
Je schrijft Python op productieniveau en hebt ervaring met het deployen en monitoren van modellen in een live omgeving.
Je hebt ervaring met of affiniteit voor AI-ondersteund ontwikkelen, bijvoorbeeld met tools als Claude Code, GitHub Copilot of vergelijkbare AI-assistenten.
Je denkt vanuit commerciële impact: je kunt de stap maken van een goed model naar meetbare euro's en onderbouwt je keuzes met data.
Je neemt eigenaarschap over wat je bouwt. Jouw modellen draaien live en je bent de eerste die ingrijpt, optimaliseert of bijstuurt wanneer dat nodig is.
Je bent nieuwsgierig en proactief: je volgt ontwikkelingen in AI en machine learning op de voet en vertaalt die naar concrete experimenten en kansen.
Je spreekt de Nederlandse taal op professioneel niveau.


Zo ziet het proces eruit


1. Solliciteer op deze vacature.
2. Telefonisch kennismakingsgesprek met Alice (recruiter).
3. Eerste gesprek met Dennis (direct leidinggevende van de functie) en iemand van het team.
4. Persoonlijkheids- en capaciteitentest, gevolgd door een technische casus met iemand van het team waarin je laat zien hoe je van data naar een forecastingmodel en businessimpact komt.
5. Is er een match? Dan doen we je snel een aanbod.


Heb je vragen? Bel of mail 088 020 3012 / alice.kalle@zonneplan.nl

HTML:
Als data scientist bij Trading & Optimization werk je op het snijvlak van energie en AI, op een plek waar je werk direct effect heeft op het energienet. Jouw forecast om 13:00:00 stuurt om 13:00:01 een handelsbeslissing aan, en de impact zie je realtime terug op onze dashboards. Je bouwt niet één geïsoleerd model, maar een samenhangend geheel waarin probabilistische forecasting, optimalisatie en aansturing elkaar versterken. De feedbackloop is kort en meetbaar, wat je vandaag bouwt heeft snel impact.

Je werkt in een team waar data scientists, engineers en traders dicht op elkaar zitten. Er is weinig hiërarchie en veel ruimte voor eigen initiatief, maar ook duidelijke verwachtingen over eigenaarschap: jouw modellen draaien live en je bent zelf de eerste die ingrijpt als er iets misgaat. AI-tooling zoals Claude Code is volledig ingeburgerd en versnelt je werk. Zo draagt het team direct bij aan plan Zero: elke MWh die we slimmer verhandelen of efficiënter in een batterij laden of ontladen, is een MWh die niet uit een gascentrale hoeft te komen.

Jouw impact bij Zonneplan

  • Jij ontwerpt en bouwt de modellen die onze energiehandel aansturen. Je werkt met meerdere gekoppelde systemen tegelijk: weersvoorspellingen, marktprijzen (day-ahead, intraday, onbalans), klantgedrag en het fysieke gedrag van ons VPP (Virtual Power Plant) van batterijen, laadpalen en zonnepanelen. Je denkt in samenhang en bouwt modellen die elkaar versterken.
  • Jij brengt modellen naar productie en houdt ze scherp. Samen met engineers zorg je voor een vlekkeloze deployment. Je pakt eigenaarschap over je modellen: je monitort performance, waarborgt betrouwbaarheid en optimaliseert continu.
  • Jij vertaalt modelkwaliteit naar commercieel resultaat. Jouw werk verlaagt direct de tradingkosten binnen ons energieportfolio en verhoogt het rendement op de Nexus-thuisbatterij. Door nauwkeurige voorspellingen kunnen we klanten een zonnebonus geven in plaats van een terugleverboete, omdat we weten wanneer hun stroom waardevol is.
  • Jij bereidt onze apparaten voor op een toekomst zonder salderen. Door slim in te spelen op marktontwikkelingen en eigen verbruik zorg je ervoor dat klanten maximaal profiteren. Je vertaalt veranderende marktregels naar concrete modelaanpassingen die direct waarde opleveren voor onze klanten.
  • Jij brengt nieuwe ontwikkelingen naar de praktijk. Je volgt de nieuwste ontwikkelingen in AI en machine learning en vertaalt die proactief naar concrete experimenten. Denk aan nieuwe benaderingen voor probabilistische forecasting. Liever een werkend prototype dan een slide.

Dit krijg je van ons

  • Een salaris tussen €4.000 en €6.000 bruto per maand, afhankelijk van je ervaring en senioriteit.
  • 25 vakantiedagen (met de mogelijkheid om er meer bij te kopen) en reiskostenvergoeding.
  • Een Claude Code Max 20x abonnement als secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarde. Je zet het in voor je dagelijkse werk en mag het ook gebruiken voor side projects en persoonlijke ontwikkeling.
  • Een persoonlijk keuzebudget voor sport, boodschappen of iets anders dat bij je past.
  • Vanaf dag één investeren we in jouw groei met de Zonneplan Academy, goede tools en ruimte om te blijven leren. Je kunt doorgroeien naar senior data scientist met specialisatie in energiehandel, batterij-optimalisatie of forecasting.
  • Een pragmatische, no-nonsense werksfeer waarin korte lijnen de norm zijn. Je zit dicht op traders en engineers.

Dit neem jij mee

  • Je hebt minimaal 3 jaar ervaring als data scientist en sterke fundamenten in statistiek en machine learning, met aantoonbare ervaring in forecasting of time series.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data scientist

Fulltime, 38 hours per week

Are you passionate about transforming large volumes of data into predictive models that directly shape business value? As a Data Scientist at Action, you develop solutions that drive smarter decisions, operational excellence, and measurable impact across one of Europe’s fastest-growing retailers. In this role, you help teams embrace data-driven decision-making and deliver solutions that positively impact daily operations.

This is your daily action

As Data Scientist, you will:


Translate business questions into testable analytical objectives
Collect, clean, and prepare structured and unstructured data to enable modelling
Build descriptive and diagnostic insights through exploratory data analysis
Design production-ready, integrated data science solutions together with solution architects
Develop and improve solutions using statistical methods, machine learning, deep learning or GenAI when appropriate
Productionise solutions following MLOps/LLMOps principles (scheduled workflow jobs, containerised APIs or interactive apps)
Set up monitoring for accuracy, drift, and system performance
Collaborate with Technology and business teams (Supply Chain, Commerce, Store Operations and Real Estate) to embed solutions into daily operations


This is what your day could look like: you kick off a new AI project with Commercial Operations, align on scope and responsibilities, brainstorm solution design with peers, and later fix a bug in a Supply Chain data product. After automated CI tests pass, your update is deployed via the CD pipeline, delivering immediate impact to operations.

Meet your team

You will join the central Data Analytics team and work closely with your peers. While you will have your own tasks and responsibilities, being part of a team also involves supporting one another, learning together, and growing both individually and collectively. Besides working with your peers, you work closely with Technology, the Process team, and with business teams in Supply Chain, Commerce, Store Operations, and Real Estate. You report to the Manager Data Science & Analytics, who supports innovation and personal development.

This is you

You are an enthusiastic and proactive Data Scientist who enjoys working end-to-end: from scoping and data exploration to modelling, deploying and monitoring. You communicate clearly with technical and non-technical colleagues and enjoy building solutions that improve forecasting accuracy, optimize operations and support strategic decisions.

In addition:


Master’s degree in Econometrics, Data Science, AI, Operations Research or a similar quantitative field
5+ years of experience as a Data Scientist, preferably in a retail environment
Experience with machine learning models (e.g. clustering, regression, time-series, classification)
Experience with modern data platforms, specifically Azure (e.g. workflows, container registries) and Databricks (e.g. MLflow)
Experience with MLOps practices (e.g. model versioning, monitoring, CI/CD)
Strong Python, PySpark and SQL skills
Familiarity with Data Engineering and ML Engineering principles
Familiarity with LLM concepts and practical applications (e.g. RAG, agentic workflows, tool calling, fine-tuning) is a plus


What we offer

Surprisingly much! From our international headquarters in the Netherlands, you have an impact on what happens in our stores across Europe. We welcome more than 15 million customers every week in more than 3,000 stores in 14 European countries. We have great growth ambitions. For Action and for you. Because our growth means that you can always take new steps. A different position, an interesting innovation project, new skills: you can continue to develop as a professional. And all this in a culture where our shared values are truly lived: customer focus, teamwork, simplicity, discipline, cost conscious and respect.

Did you know that you can also count on us for:


A market-based salary based on a 38-hour work week
24 days of holiday and the option to buy 5 extra days (based on 38 hours per week)
An annual bonus between 10 and 20% based on company and personal performance
Flexible working hours and the possibility of working up to 40% from home (in coordination with your team and manager)
15% staff discount on your purchases at Action
A good pension scheme, collective health insurance and travel expenses allowance
A culture focused on collaboration, ownership and continuous improvement


How we contribute to society

If you only know Action from the stores, a world will open up for you when you come and work with us. We work hard to make a positive impact on society and the environment. Did you know, for example, that we sponsor more than 2,500 children worldwide who grow up in an SOS Children's Village? That our team reflects the diversity of our customers, including 155 different nationalities? That we purchase 94% of our wood products and 100% of our cotton sustainably? And that almost all our stores only use energy-efficient LED lighting? Yes, that is also Action. Curious about what other initiatives we’re facilitating? Join our team and find out for yourself.

Interested?

We are proud to be an inclusive employer, and we warmly invite anyone who recognises themselves in the vacancy to apply. So take action and respond to this vacancy now!

What happens next:


Response to your application within 14 days (and usually earlier)
First meeting with the manager and a colleague
A business case for you to complete
Second interview with your recruiter, among others
Welcome to Action…


Throughout the process, your recruiter is your permanent point of contact and is available to answer any questions you may have.

Integrity is very important to us. Pre-employment screening is therefore part of the process for this vacancy. Ask your recruiter for more information.

Title: AI engineer
Description:
Wil jij werken aan AI-oplossingen die écht gebruikt worden in plaats van te eindigen in een proof of concept? Ben jij een Data & AI Engineer of ervaren data scientist die energie krijgt van bouwen, verbeteren en kennis delen? Dan pas je waarschijnlijk goed binnen deze rol.




Over de functie

Je komt terecht bij een snelgroeiende technologieorganisatie die actief is binnen een sterk gereguleerde markt. Vanuit een compact en ambitieus Data & Analytics-team werk je aan oplossingen die direct impact hebben op de dienstverlening en bedrijfsvoering van klanten.




Binnen het team krijg je veel vrijheid en verantwoordelijkheid. Je werkt niet alleen aan technische implementaties, maar denkt ook mee over de technische koers van het team en de verdere professionalisering van AI- en ML-oplossingen. Geen dag is hetzelfde: de ene dag train je een Machine Learning-model, terwijl je de volgende dag werkt aan een productieomgeving voor een nieuwe AI-toepassing of een MLOps-pipeline optimaliseert.




Je speelt een belangrijke rol binnen het team door technische richting te geven, kennis te delen en minder ervaren collega's te begeleiden in hun ontwikkeling.




Wat ga je doen

Ontwikkelen van AI-applicaties van prototype tot productieomgeving.
Trainen, valideren en operationaliseren van Machine Learning-modellen.
Ontwerpen en implementeren van technische en operationele AI-strategieën.
Opzetten, beheren en optimaliseren van ML- en AI-Ops pipelines.
Ontwikkelen van schaalbare architecturen en infrastructuren voor AI-oplossingen.
Samenwerken met stakeholders uit verschillende afdelingen om businessvraagstukken te vertalen naar technische oplossingen.
Coachen en begeleiden van junior engineers op het gebied van engineering best practices.




Wie ben jij

Je beschikt over minimaal 4 jaar ervaring binnen een analytische of technische functie.
Je hebt aantoonbare ervaring met het zelfstandig uitvoeren en opleveren van projecten.
Je hebt sterke programmeervaardigheden in Python en SQL.
Je hebt ervaring met CI/CD, testing en versiebeheer met Git.
Je bent bekend met containerisatie en tools zoals Docker.
Je hebt ervaring met Machine Learning, Generative AI, RAG-oplossingen of frameworks voor AI-agents is een grote pré.
Je beheerst de Nederlandse taal uitstekend in woord en geschrift.




Over de organisatie

Deze organisatie ontwikkelt technologieoplossingen die complexe processen voor klanten eenvoudiger en efficiënter maken. Innovatie, data en automatisering staan centraal in de strategie van het bedrijf.

De cultuur kenmerkt zich door ondernemerschap, samenwerking en een sterke focus op persoonlijke ontwikkeling. Medewerkers krijgen veel vrijheid en verantwoordelijkheid om initiatieven te nemen en nieuwe ideeën uit te proberen. Er wordt gewerkt in een informele en professionele omgeving waarin kennisdeling en samenwerking vanzelfsprekend zijn.

Daarnaast wordt veel aandacht besteed aan de onderlinge verbinding door gezamenlijke activiteiten, informele bijeenkomsten en regelmatige teamuitjes. De organisatie investeert actief in zowel professionele als persoonlijke groei van medewerkers.




Wat bieden we je

Een salaris tot €7.000 o.b.v. 40 uur;
8% vakantiegeld, reiskostenvergoeding én thuiswerkvergoeding;
Flexibele werktijden + hybride werktijden;
Thuiswerkvergoeding om je plek volledig in te richten;
Diverse opleidingsmogelijkheden;
Coaching en persoonlijk financieel advies;




Interesse?

Kan jij je vinden in de bovenstaande beschrijving en wil je een stap maken in je carrière? Neem dan contact op met ons. Stuur je een recent CV mee? Dan kunnen we meteen kijken of jouw ervaring aansluit bij het opgestelde profiel.

Title: Data Scientist (AI/ML)
Description:
For one of our clients active within the Retail sector, we are currently looking for a Senior Data Scientist focusing on Machine Learning, AI, MLOps and GenAI solutions.

As the Senior Data Scientist, you will support the team in developing and deploying data-driven solutions that improve operational efficiency, forecasting accuracy, and business decision-making across the organization.

Responsibilities include (but are not limited to):

Translating business challenges into analytical objectives and impactful AI/ML solutions.
Building, training, deploying, and monitoring machine learning, deep learning, and GenAI models.
Performing data engineering activities including collecting, cleaning, and preparing structured and unstructured datasets.
Collaborating with business stakeholders, architects, and technology teams to deliver production-ready data science solutions.
Implementing MLOps/LLMOps best practices including CI/CD, model monitoring, versioning, and deployment.
Driving adoption of data science solutions within Supply Chain, Commerce, Store Operations, and Real Estate teams.


Profile


Background as a Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, AI Engineer, or similar.
Minimum 5 years of experience within a Data Science or Machine Learning environment.
Experience with Python, PySpark, SQL, Azure, Databricks (MLflow), Docker, and MLOps practices.
Strong experience with Machine Learning techniques such as classification, regression, clustering, and time-series forecasting.
Experience within a Retail environment would be a strong plus.
Familiarity with LLMs, RAG, agentic workflows, tool calling, and fine-tuning is advantageous.
Fluent in English.


Project



Start: ASAP

Duration: 6 months (+)

Hours per week: full-time

Salary indication: (based on experience)

Does this sound like you and are you available on short notice? Please let me know as soon as possible by applying with your most recent CV and cover letter highlighting your most relevant experience.

Please note that this role is only applicable for candidates based in the Netherlands.





Let op: vacaturefraude




Helaas komt vacaturefraude steeds vaker voor. We waarschuwen je voor mogelijke misleiding:
* Wij zullen nooit via WhatsApp of in een videogesprek vragen om jouw persoonlijke gegevens (zoals een kopie van je ID, bankgegevens of BSN).
* Twijfel je over de echtheid van een vacature of contactpersoon? Neem dan altijd rechtstreeks contact met ons op via de officiële contactgegevens op onze website.

Important: job fraud

Unfortunately, job fraud is becoming more common. Beware of such scams:
* We will never ask for personal information (such as a copy of your ID, bank details, or social security number) via WhatsApp or during a video call.
* If you're unsure whether a vacancy or contact person is legitimate, please reach out to us directly using the official contact details on our website.

Title: Applied Research Scientist [Machine Visibility]
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for an Applied Research Scientist (Machine Visibility) based in Netherlands.

This is a unique opportunity to pioneer research in the emerging field of machine visibility, exploring how AI systems, search crawlers, and autonomous agents interact with the modern web. As the first dedicated research specialist in this domain, you will build the research function from the ground up while working with one of the industry's largest datasets of web crawling behavior. You will design original studies, develop scalable research infrastructure, and generate insights that influence both product strategy and the broader technology ecosystem. The role combines large-scale data science, applied research, and strategic collaboration in a highly autonomous, remote-first environment. Your work will be published under your name, helping establish thought leadership in a rapidly evolving area of AI and web technologies. This position is ideal for researchers who enjoy transforming complex datasets into impactful discoveries with real-world applications.

Accountabilities


Build and maintain the research infrastructure required to organize, clean, and analyze large-scale web crawling and machine interaction datasets.
Design and execute original research projects from hypothesis development through methodology, statistical analysis, and publication.
Develop new data collection processes to expand research capabilities and answer emerging questions related to AI crawlers and machine visibility.
Analyze the behavior of search crawlers, AI bots, and autonomous agents while identifying trends across evolving machine ecosystems.
Produce research outputs ranging from short data-driven reports to comprehensive flagship studies published under your own authorship.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate research findings into actionable insights that support product development, business strategy, and market positioning.
Continuously monitor developments in AI search, web standards, structured data, and machine-readable technologies to inform future research priorities.
Leverage modern AI tools to accelerate data analysis, synthesis, experimentation, and research workflows.


Requirements


Proven experience conducting and publishing original research in either academic or industry settings.
Strong expertise in large-scale data analysis using SQL and Python.
Demonstrated ability to build research infrastructure and data pipelines rather than working solely with existing datasets.
Solid background in experimental design, hypothesis testing, statistical analysis, and evidence-based research methodologies.
Experience independently managing research projects from concept through publication.
Comfortable incorporating AI tools into research and analytical workflows.
Excellent written communication skills with the ability to present complex technical findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Strong curiosity, critical thinking, and the ability to work autonomously in an unstructured research environment.
Experience with AI crawler behavior, technical SEO, Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), information retrieval, or natural language processing is considered a strong advantage.
Public research publications, conference presentations, or recognized thought leadership in related fields are a plus.


Benefits


100% remote position with preference for European time zone collaboration.
Opportunity to build and lead a brand-new research function with significant autonomy.
Access to a unique, web-scale dataset unavailable elsewhere in the industry.
Direct collaboration with executive leadership to shape long-term research strategy.
Publication of original research under your own name, supporting professional visibility and career growth.
Structured onboarding roadmap with clearly defined research objectives and milestones.
Learning and development budget for conferences, courses, tools, and professional resources.
International, collaborative, and remote-first working environment.
Transparent, market-aligned compensation shared before the interview process.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Title: AI Enablement Lead [gn] Data Intelligence
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for an AI Enablement Lead [gn] Data Intelligence based in Netherlands.

This role is designed for a hands-on AI leader who will drive the adoption and scaling of artificial intelligence capabilities across an innovative data technology environment.

You will define the strategy, architecture, and operational frameworks that enable teams to build and deploy AI-powered solutions efficiently and responsibly.

The position combines software engineering expertise, AI infrastructure knowledge, and organizational influence to accelerate product innovation.

You will create reusable AI platforms, establish LLMOps and governance practices, and help engineering teams integrate advanced AI capabilities into production systems.

Working across product, engineering, and business teams, you will transform AI from an emerging technology into a core organizational capability.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of intelligent data platforms while building scalable solutions with measurable business impact.

Accountabilities

As an AI Enablement Lead, you will own the development and execution of AI enablement initiatives, creating the foundations that allow teams to safely and effectively leverage AI technologies. You will combine technical leadership with strategic vision to accelerate AI adoption, improve engineering workflows, and deliver next-generation intelligent capabilities.


Design and maintain internal AI platforms, orchestration layers, API gateways, and reusable frameworks including advanced RAG architectures and agentic AI solutions.
Partner with product and engineering teams to integrate production-ready generative AI and machine learning capabilities into data platform solutions.
Establish LLMOps and MLOps practices, including governance frameworks, model evaluation processes, monitoring systems, security controls, and privacy standards.
Optimize AI infrastructure performance by managing model costs, token usage, latency, and technology choices across commercial and open-source solutions.
Lead AI enablement initiatives through workshops, technical documentation, architecture guidance, and knowledge-sharing programs.
Drive rapid AI prototyping from proof of concept through production deployment, ensuring solutions are scalable, reliable, and maintainable.
Define standards for AI tooling, including vector databases, embedding strategies, semantic search approaches, and caching mechanisms.
Evaluate emerging AI technologies, vendors, and open-source models to maintain a forward-looking AI strategy.
Establish measurable success metrics for AI adoption, including developer productivity, delivery acceleration, and business impact.


Requirements

The ideal candidate is a highly technical AI professional with experience building enterprise-grade AI solutions and the ability to influence teams through strong communication and technical leadership. You should combine software engineering discipline with deep knowledge of modern AI architectures and operational practices.


Strong experience as a Senior AI/ML Engineer, LLMOps Engineer, Machine Learning Engineer, or Software Architect building and scaling AI-powered applications.
Proven expertise in enterprise SaaS environments, complex data platforms, or large-scale software ecosystems.
Advanced knowledge of Python or Go, semantic search, vector databases such as Pinecone, Milvus, or pgvector, and AI orchestration frameworks including LangChain or LlamaIndex.
Experience with large language models, prompt engineering, fine-tuning approaches, and building reliable AI-powered workflows.
Strong software engineering practices, including CI/CD, automated testing, evaluation datasets, Docker, Kubernetes, and clean architecture principles.
Experience designing AI governance frameworks focused on security, privacy, performance, and responsible AI adoption.
Ability to proactively identify opportunities and build solutions that address organizational challenges before they become blockers.
Excellent leadership and communication skills, with the ability to align cross-functional teams without direct management responsibility.
Strong written and verbal English communication skills, with the ability to translate complex AI concepts into clear business value.


Benefits


Opportunity to lead AI transformation initiatives within an innovative and fast-growing technology environment.
Competitive salary and benefits package.
Flexible work arrangements, including remote or hybrid options.
Collaboration with a diverse and highly skilled team of technology professionals.
Opportunities for professional growth, continuous learning, and career development.
The chance to shape AI strategy, tooling, and product innovation at scale.

Title: Machine Learning Software Engineer
Description:
About Applied Intuition

Applied Intuition, Inc. is powering the future of physical AI. Founded in 2017 and now valued at $15 billion, the Silicon Valley company is creating the digital infrastructure needed to bring intelligence to every moving machine on the planet. Applied Intuition services the automotive, defense, trucking, construction, mining and agriculture industries in three core areas: tools and infrastructure, operating systems, and autonomy. Eighteen of the top 20 global automakers, as well as the United States military and its allies, trust the company’s solutions to deliver physical intelligence. Applied Intuition is headquartered in Sunnyvale, California, with offices in Washington, D.C.; San Diego; Ft. Walton Beach, Florida; Ann Arbor, Michigan; London; Stuttgart; Munich; Stockholm; Bangalore; Seoul; and Tokyo. Learn more at applied.co.

We are an in-office company, and our expectation is that employees primarily work from their Applied Intuition office 5 days a week. However, we also recognize the importance of flexibility and trust our employees to manage their schedules responsibly. This may include occasional remote work, starting the day with morning meetings from home before heading to the office, or leaving earlier when needed to accommodate family commitments.

About The Role

We are looking for software engineers with expertise in ML-first perception, prediction or planning for autonomous vehicles or mobile robots. Your contributions will focus on building out key ML capabilities of an autonomous vehicle stack.

In addition to your engineering contributions, by working in our dynamic and customer-focused team culture, you will contribute to and learn from best practices in the autonomy industry. We move fast and focus on excellence, for our products and for our business. If you are hands-on and looking for a place to have a multiplying effect on making autonomous systems a reality, Applied Intuition is the place for you!

At Applied Intuition, You Will


Design and implement capabilities and workflows for cutting-edge real-world perception or planning/prediction systems
Leverage established products at Applied Intuition to build the software and infrastructure foundation for our ML developments


We're Looking For Someone Who Has


Experience with the end-to-end development cycle of deep learning models
Expertise in subdomains such as modeling, input pipelines, evaluation, deployment, and model optimization
3+ years of experience building production software using modern software practices
Fluency in C++, or fluency in Python with intermediate experience in C++
Deep understanding of the concepts and methods behind any frameworks or libraries that they worked with
Experience working with production level ML and DL perception algorithms for autonomous vehicles


Nice To Have


MSc or PhD in machine learning, ideally applied to perception, prediction,planning or closely related field
Experience building and shipping software frameworks or tools
Experience with driver assistance or autonomous driving systems
Experience in evaluating and improving system-in-the-loop model performance
Deep hands-on expertise in relevant algorithms or methods, such as non-linear optimization, computational geometry, numerical analysis, or distributed systems


Benefits


30 days of paid vacation per year
Daily meal benefits: €19 lunch budget and €19 dinner budget, free breakfast, snacks, and drinks in the office
You’ll receive €965 annual learning & development and €580 annual fitness budget
Subsidized Wellpass membership, giving you access to thousands of gyms, studios, and wellness facilities across Germany
€25 monthly contribution to your Deutschlandticket for regional public transport across Germany
Generous parental leave: on top of statutory parental leave


Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

Applied Intuition is an equal opportunity employer and federal contractor or subcontractor. Consequently, the parties agree that, as applicable, they will abide by the requirements of 41 CFR 60-1.4(a), 41 CFR 60-300.5(a) and 41 CFR 60-741.5(a) and that these laws are incorporated herein by reference. These regulations prohibit discrimination against qualified individuals based on their status as protected veterans or individuals with disabilities, and prohibit discrimination against all individuals based on their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or national origin. These regulations require that covered prime contractors and subcontractors take affirmative action to employ and advance in employment individuals without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status or disability. The parties also agree that, as applicable, they will abide by the requirements of Executive Order 13496 (29 CFR Part 471, Appendix A to Subpart A), relating to the notice of employee rights under federal labor laws.

Compensation Range: €100K - €150K

Description:
MLOps Engineer | Data Engineer Automotive (m/w/d) in Stuttgart Du brennst für die Mobilität von morgen? Dann bist Du bei uns genau richtig! Bei PROGNUM sind wir als etablierter Ingenieur- und Beratungsdienstleister darauf spezialisiert, in vielseitigen Automotive-Projekten neue Maßstäbe zu setzen. Wir arbeiten an den wegweisenden Technologien von morgen – sei es im autonomen Fahren, Elektromobilität, Connectivity, Infotainment oder darüber hinaus. Sichere Dir die Pole-Position und starte mit uns durch! Was Dich erwartet


Aufbau und Pflege von Daten- und KI-Workflows in Cloud- oder On-Prem-Umgebungen
Unterstützung bei der Automatisierung von Trainings- und Deploymentschritten
Analyse und Aufbereitung von Fahrzeug- und Prozessdaten
Zusammenarbeit mit Data Scientists und Softwareentwicklern
Weiterentwicklung bestehender Datenplattformen und Analysewerkzeuge Das bringst Du mit
Erfahrung in Datenverarbeitung, Cloud- oder Softwareprojekten
Kenntnisse in einer Programmiersprache (z. B. Python, Java, C# oder vergleichbar)
Interesse an KI, Machine Learning und modernen DevOps-Methoden
Analytisches Denken, Lernbereitschaft und Freude an interdisziplinären Teams
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse Was wir bieten
Attraktive Vergütung: Ausgezeichnetes Gehaltspaket & täglicher Kantinenzuschuss
Moderne Arbeitskultur: New Work ist für uns Normalität (z.B. Homeoffice)
PROGNUM-DNA: Familiär, offen und wertschätzend mit flachen Hierarchien sowie legendären Events & After Work Partys
Spitzen-Karriereperspektive: Möglichkeit zur individuellen Weiterentwicklung durch berufsspezifische Trainings
High-End Equipment: Notebook, Firmenhandy, Headset, Homeoffice-Technik – alles auch zur privaten Nutzung
Way of Work: Modernstes Office mit Relax-Area, Work-Café, ergonomischen Arbeitsplätzen, Firmenparkplatz und hochwertiger Kantine
…und natürlich bieten wir die üblichen Standards wie 30 Tage Urlaub, BAV, Corporate Benefits, E-Bike-Leasing uvm. Title: Machine Learning Engineer, ADAS
Description:
About Us

Founded in 2017, Wayve is the leading developer of Embodied AI technology. Our advanced AI software and foundation models enable vehicles to perceive, understand, and navigate any complex environment, enhancing the usability and safety of automated driving systems.

Our vision is to create autonomy that propels the world forward. Our intelligent, mapless, and hardware-agnostic AI products are designed for automakers, accelerating the transition from assisted to automated driving.

In our fast-paced environment big problems ignite us—we embrace uncertainty, leaning into complex challenges to unlock groundbreaking solutions. We aim high and stay humble in our pursuit of excellence, constantly learning and evolving as we pave the way for a smarter, safer future.

At Wayve, your contributions matter. We value diversity, embrace new perspectives, and foster an inclusive work environment; we back each other to deliver impact.

Make Wayve the experience that defines your career!

**

🛠️ About our Engineering Teams**

Wayve’s ADAS engineering teams build the perception and intelligence that power driver assistance in real-world driving. We work end-to-end: from creating high-quality training data, to developing and evaluating CV/3D perception models, to iterating quickly based on performance gaps. The team mixes “online” (on-car, latency/compute constrained) and “offline” (heavier, large-scale data generation) work, with a strong focus on measurable impact and shipping.

🧠 Your day-to-day

You’ll train, debug, and improve computer vision and 3D perception models, and iterate based on clear evaluation signals. You’ll work across the full ML lifecycle (data → training → evaluation → iteration), partnering with the team to decide what to tackle next based on where the system is underperforming. A meaningful portion of the role involves building scalable data pipelines (including auto-labelling / pseudo-labelling) to accelerate model development.

🧩 What You’ll Be Working On

You’ll help deliver core ADAS perception capabilities such as detection, classification, and instance segmentation, with domain focus across lanes, objects, traffic signs, and traffic lights. You’ll contribute to offline pipelines like tracking + 3D reconstruction that let us back-propagate “known good” labels through time and generate large labelled datasets. Depending on your strengths, you may lean more into online models that must run fast in-car, or offline models that improve data quality and coverage at scale.

🙌 You Should Apply If

You’ve built and shipped CV-focused deep learning systems and can demonstrate strong applied ML engineering (not research-only). You have experience with 3D perception concepts or pipelines (e.g., LiDAR, multi-view geometry, tracking, 3D reconstruction) and you’re comfortable owning work end-to-end, including evaluation and dataset generation. You enjoy pragmatic problem-solving, working under real product constraints, and you’re excited to improve real-world driving performance through better perception.

🌱 Not ticking every box? That’s totally okay! If you’re passionate about autonomy and keen to learn, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every requirement.

More About Wayve

🚀 Wayve is building the leading AI platform for autonomous driving. We are pioneering an end to end AI approach that enables vehicles to learn directly from real world experience, developing the ability to adapt, generalise and improve at scale. Instead of relying on hand coded rules or pre mapped environments, our AI Driver learns to drive by understanding the world around it. The result is technology that navigates complex urban environments with intelligence, precision and natural flow, unlocking meaningful advances in both safety and efficiency. We believe autonomy represents a once in a generation transformation in how people and goods move, comparable to the shift from horses to cars, and from human driven vehicles to intelligent machines.

Our ambition is to make autonomy universal. Wayve’s mapless and hardware agnostic AI platform integrates with global OEM partners, enabling continuous software evolution and unlocking advanced levels of automation from L2 plus through to L4 as our core AI model scales. In a race increasingly defined by intelligence and real world learning, Wayve is taking a distinct approach, building a generalisable driving intelligence that can power any vehicle, anywhere. By combining embodied AI with scalable deployment, we are creating technology that can be shaped to each OEM brand and driver experience, accelerating the transition to a safer, more intelligent future of mobility.

How we work 💻- Locations & Flexible Working:

Our main hubs are in London, Sunnyvale, Yokohama, Herzliya, Vancouver and Leonberg. We operate a hybrid working model that combines in-person collaboration in our dedicated office spaces with focused time working remotely. This gives our teams the connection and energy of working together, alongside the flexibility to do their best work in a way that fits their lives.

🔍 The Interview Process

Our process is clear and respectful of your time:


Initial call / recruiter screen (30 mins)
Competency Interviews (Programming and System Design 2 hours total)
Deep-dive technical interviews (domain-specific interview: 1 hours total)
Final interview: mission & values alignment (1 hour).


We’ll always explain the format and work around your availability.

What’s in it for you (Location dependant):

💰 Salaries benchmarked against the market annually

📈 Meaningful equity, sharing in the ownership and long term success of Wayve

✈️ Relocation support and visa sponsorship where applicable

✅ Hybrid working, core hours and the chance to work hands on in vehicle workshops and labs

📚 Learning and development budgets with support for training, conferences and growth

🩺 Comprehensive benefits including health insurance, dental, enhanced maternity and paternity leave, retirement or pension where applicable, access to therapists, wellbeing partnerships, team socials and more

Wayve is committed to creating an inclusive interview experience. If you require any accommodations or adjustments to participate fully in our interview process, please let us know.

We understand that everyone has a unique set of skills and experiences and that not everyone will meet all of the requirements listed above. If you’re passionate about self-driving cars and think you have what it takes to make a positive impact on the world, we encourage you to apply.

At Wayve we're committed to creating a diverse, fair and respectful culture that is inclusive of everyone based on their unique skills and perspectives, and regardless of sex, race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding) or any other basis as protected by applicable law.

For more information visit Careers at Wayve.

To learn more about what drives us, visit Values at Wayve

For US candidates only, please visit E-Verify Notice and Participation and Right to Work

DISCLAIMER: We will not ask about marriage or pregnancy, care responsibilities or disabilities in any of our job adverts or interviews. However, we do look to capture information about care responsibilities, and disabilities among other diversity information as part of an optional DEI Monitoring form to help us identify areas of improvement in our hiring process and ensure that the process is inclusive and non-discriminatory.
