Title: Senior Data Scientist
Place: Herlev, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About The Job

You will join a growing AI team of 11 talented colleagues with a shared mission: transforming data and AI into measurable business value for Flatpay and our merchants.

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will design, build, and productionize GenAI-powered products and pipelines that support business-critical decisions across the organization. A key area of focus will be customer-facing conversational AI, where creativity, strong product thinking, and a user-centred mindset are essential for developing cutting-edge solutions.

In addition, you will work on AI-driven document, image, and audio processing systems that power critical business processes and analytics. You will collaborate closely with the broader AI team while taking strong ownership of complex projects and driving them from ideation to scalable production systems.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex, ambiguous problems with modern AI technologies and who has the technical judgment to turn ideas into robust, maintainable, and high-impact products. You will work across the entire AI product lifecycle — from data engineering and model development to deployment and user-facing applications — while helping set a high standard for applied machine learning, LLMs, and generative AI at Flatpay.

We are looking for someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment, takes end-to-end ownership, and is comfortable engaging directly with stakeholders to identify opportunities, shape solutions, align technical direction with business needs, and continuously drive improvement. As a senior member of the team, you will also be expected to contribute to technical direction, raise the quality bar, and support the growth of those around you.

What You'll Do


Design, build, and maintain production-grade GenAI applications and pipelines using Python, MLflow, Databricks, and leading AI platforms such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.
Develop AI-powered solutions including customer-facing conversational AI, LLM-driven data enrichment and analytics workflows, and audio, image, and multimodal processing systems for both internal and customer-facing products.
Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested production Python code, including automated tests, monitoring, guardrails, validation frameworks, and fail-safe mechanisms to ensure reliability, security, and robustness in AI systems.
Build, orchestrate, and maintain data and AI workflows in Databricks, including pipelines, jobs, workflows, model serving endpoints, and custom applications deployed on the Databricks platform.
Leverage cloud platforms such as GCP, AWS, and Azure to provision, deploy, and scale AI services, including LLMs.
Integrate, optimize, and manage AI workflows that leverage external platforms and services, including tools such as ElevenLabs, Metabase, and other third-party providers.
Collaborate closely with data engineers to ensure AI products are built on reliable, scalable, and well-governed data foundations.
Drive complex projects from proof of concept through production deployment, monitoring, ongoing optimization, and business adoption.
Make technical design decisions, evaluate trade-offs, and help define best practices for building reliable, secure, and scalable AI systems.
Mentor and support other team members through code reviews, technical sparring, and sharing practical knowledge across AI engineering, machine learning, and production GenAI systems.


Who You Are


You have 5+ years of industry experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, AI Engineering, or a similar technical role, with a strong track record of delivering production AI or ML systems.
You have hands-on experience building and maintaining customer-facing GenAI applications and pipelines in production and understand the importance of automated testing, monitoring, guardrails, and reliability in production AI systems.
You have experience using LLMs from multiple state-of-the-art providers of frontier and open-source models, and you have a deep understanding of the strengths and trade-offs of different frontier and open-source models.
You have strong experience building applications that leverage retrieval-augmented generation, tool calling, agents, or other modern LLM architectures.
You are highly comfortable developing production solutions in Python and working with libraries such as Transformers and the SDKs of leading LLM providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and others.
You are comfortable working in Databricks or similar cloud-based data and AI platforms, including building pipelines, workflows, model serving endpoints, and production deployments.
You have strong PySpark, Pandas, and SQL skills and are comfortable working with large, structured datasets.
You are pragmatic and able to turn ambiguous business problems into concrete technical solutions, while helping stakeholders understand trade-offs and opportunities.
You are comfortable taking ownership of high-impact projects, working independently, and collaborating directly with stakeholders across the business.
You contribute positively to team standards, technical direction, and knowledge sharing, and you enjoy helping others become better at building high-quality AI systems.


Why Flatpay?


Impact: Ship AI and data solutions that create real impact in one of Europe’s fastest-growing companies.
Meaningful products: Build models that help merchants and colleagues make better decisions, act on insights, and improve workflows.
Learning and growth: Develop across forecasting, production ML, data engineering, and AI in a supportive team.
Flat structure: Flat structure and a strong action-over-process culture
Competitive salary
Vacation: 6 weeks paid
Relaxed atmosphere with optional social events such as minigolf evenings, hackathons, and company parties in exciting off-site locations.
Co-workers with a passion for data and AI, and a passion for sharing knowledge daily.
Pension and health insurance, as well as almost unlimited company merch.


About Us

Welcome to Flatpay!

Flatpay is not just another payment solution; we are dedicated to innovating the market for payment solutions and financial services in the SME segment with our customer-centric and transparent services.

Today, we are one of the fastest-growing fintech companies out of the Nordics - and Denmark's fastest unicorn!

Flatpay is more than just work. We believe in fostering a vibrant and fun work culture where our ambitious colleagues can thrive. As part of our team, you will be one of +2.000 passionate colleagues dedicated to making a difference in the world of payments.

What sets Flatpay apart is our dedication. We continuously strive to stay ahead of the curve, adapting our solutions to meet our client's evolving needs.

Let's shape the future of merchants together!

Title: Lead Data Scientist / Analytics Engineer to TxB Analytics in Nordea
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job ID: 5146

The banking industry is undergoing a period of dynamic change and Transaction Banking (TxB) – the Payments, Cards and Trade Solution area – is at the heart of the digital, instant and open revolution. We are looking for a Lead Data Scientist / Analytics Engineer with high stakeholder management skills to create actionable customer insights, identify new revenue opportunities, improve execution on strategies utilising data and further develop or analytical platform.

About Our Team

Welcome to the TxB Analytics team; the data science team that enables TxB and our customers to become more data driven. We add value by creating actionable customer insights to optimise existing business while at the same time exploring new revenue opportunities and improving efficiency. To be successful we need a tech savvy and analytical mind with great business understanding. We aim to be courageous and explorative in our approach which means you’ll have many opportunities to learn and grow as you build your career with us. Will you help us take the next step with our use of data?

Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.

Main responsibilities in this role:


Work closely with our most important stakeholders, understand their needs, capture requirements and conduct workshops with them.
Functional leadership of the area software engineering and ML/AI to strengthen the team in the area.
Utilise data analytics to build analytics products so our stakeholders can make important strategic decisions, grow income, improve operational efficiency and develop Nordea’s product offering.
Identify business opportunities (e.g. sales leads, merchant intelligence opportunities, etc) utilising internal and external data.
Apply data science techniques such as statistical modelling, machine learning and Artificial Intelligence.
Be responsible for the development of our technical analytical platform to make it fit for purpose for Data Science.
Support and collaborate closely with other team members.


Who You Are

Your background and skills include:


You have experience working as a Data Scientist or similar where you have experiences with analytical and quantitative problem solving using advanced statistical techniques, machine learning methods and AI.
You have a business consulting mindset and are proactive working with stakeholders to understand their needs and how analytics can help them.
You have good end-to-end software engineering skills.
You have deep knowledge in SQL and programming, e.g. Python.
Solid experience in Airflow, dbt and Snowflake (or similar tools).
You are familiar with Power BI (or similar business intelligence tools).
You have data/analytics engineering experience.
You are business minded, analytical and have excellent communication skills in English (both in text and spoken).
You are a social, curious and a pragmatic team player.
Experience in Requirements Management and workshop facilitation would an advantage.
Experience in Transaction Banking or Payments/Cards would be an advantage.
You have a MSc/BSc/BA degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, physics or related quantitative degree.


What We Offer

People come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.

A culture that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.

Hybrid working model – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.

Diversity and inclusion are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.

If this sounds like you, get in touch!

Next steps

Submit your application no later than 07/08/2026. For more information, you’re welcome to contact Mikael Simonsson at mikael.simonsson@nordea.com.

We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.

We build relationships. We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.

We learn and develop. We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.

We lead change. We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.

We are Nordea. We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.

Only for candidates in Sweden: For union information, please contact Finansförbundet at finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACO at SacoNordea@nordea.com.

Only for candidates in Finland: A security clearance will be performed for the person selected for this position

Title: Data scientist til asset management og digital transformation
Place: Hedehusene, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du arbejde med dataanalyse, modellering og visualisering og samtidig være bindeled mellem forretning, IT og vores eksterne leverandører? Så er du måske vores nye kollega.




Hos Vejdirektoratet arbejder vi hver dag for at skabe sammenhæng for de mange trafikanter, der benytter de danske statsveje. I afdelingen Befæstelser søger vi en kollega, der kan omsætte data til viden og være med til at styrke vores brug af data og samarbejdet med IT.




Dine opgaver

Du kommer til at arbejde med vejdata og bidrage til bedre beslutningsgrundlag for drift og vedligehold af vejnettet. Din rolle vil spænde bredt fra analyse og modellering til at skabe overblik og sikre, at behov bliver forstået og omsat i samarbejde med vores IT-afdeling og konsulenter. Vi arbejder med at strukturere og anvende data mere systematisk og har bl.a. løsninger i Azure.




Dine primære arbejdsopgaver bliver bl.a. at:

indhente, strukturere og analysere data om vejtilstand
arbejde med modellering og finde mønstre i data
understøtte økonomisk prioritering af vedligehold
udvikle visualiseringer og formidle resultater (fx i Power BI)
bidrage til bedre og mere effektive dataprocesser
sparre med IT og konsulenter om behov og scope for data, databaser og arkitektur
deltage i internationalt samarbejde med andre vejadministrationer




Din profil

Du har en uddannelse som f.eks. data scientist eller ingeniør, gerne med erfaring i eller uddannelse inden for databehandling eller vejteknik, herunder asset management.




Derudover ser vi gerne, at du:

kan arbejde i Python eller et andet objektorienteret sprog
har erfaring med dataanalyse og modellering fx Azur eller Databricks,
har erfaring med visualisering fx Power BI eller lign.
har forståelse for databaser og dataarkitektur
trives i en rolle, hvor du arbejder i spændet mellem IT og forretning
er nysgerrig på værktøjer og nye måder at arbejde på




Det er en fordel, hvis har praktisk erfaring med tilstandsmålinger, vejteknik eller pavement management systemer, men uanset baggrund sørger vi for at byde dig varmt velkommen til stillingen og give en grundig introduktion til arbejdsopgaverne.




Velkommen til afdelingen Befæstelser

Afdelingen har fokus på digitalisering og teknologi og består primært af ingeniører, der arbejder med vejes funktionelle og strukturelle opbygning og nedbrydning. Vi er en fagligt stærk afdeling med engagerede kolleger og højt til loftet.




Mange af os ved meget om veje, det behøver du ikke gøre, før du begynder. Til gengæld får du en central rolle i at arbejde med vores data i praksis og bidrage til, at samarbejdet mellem fagområdet, IT og leverandører fungerer. Vi er 20 ansatte og ser frem til at byde en ny kollega velkommen.




Dine løn- og ansættelsesvilkår

Du vil blive ansat og aflønnet efter gældende statslig overenskomst med mulighed for forhandling af tillæg alt efter dine kvalifikationer. Du ansættes som udgangspunkt efter én af følgende overenskomster:




Som akademiker ansættes du efter overenskomsten mellem Finansministeriet og AC. Du indplaceres på AC-skalaens basisløntrin efter anciennitet.




Som it-medarbejder ansættes du efter gældende fællesoverenskomst mellem Finansministeriet og Offentligt Ansattes Organisationer (OAO) og organisationsaftale for it-medarbejdere (PROSA) i statens tjeneste. Som udgangspunkt er stillingen placeret i den relevante løngruppe.




Du kan se basisløntrinnene i Statens lønoversigt: loenoversigten-1april-2026.pdf




Du ansættes med stillingsbetegnelsen: Ingeniør.




Forventet ansættelsesdato er d. 1. oktober 2026.




Kontakt Vejdirektoratets HR på rekruttering@vd.dk, hvis du har spørgsmål til lønindplacering. Vi gør opmærksom på, at vi kun oplyser startløn/basisløn for stillingen, og at eventuelle tillæg først forhandles i forbindelse med et konkret løntilbud.




Du bliver en del af en organisation, hvor fleksibilitet, kompetenceudvikling og arbejdsliv i balance står højt på dagsordenen. Der vil være mulighed for ugentlige hjemmearbejdsdage.




Dit ansættelsesområde vil være By-, land- og transportministeriet med de til enhver tid tilhørende institutioner. Tjenestested er Vejdirektoratet i afdelingen Befæstelser, hvor du ansættes i Drift- og Teknikdivisionen på adressen Guldalderen 12, 2640 Hedehusene (Fløng).




Læs mere

Vil du vide mere om Vejdirektoratet, og hvad det vil sige at arbejde hos os kan du læse mere om vores opgaver og medarbejdere på www.vejdirektoratet.dk.




Hvis du vil vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte afdelingsleder Ghita Berg på telefon 7244 7141 eller 2290 7024.




Er du interesseret?

Send din ansøgning snarest muligt dog ikke senere end søndag den 2. august 2026. Vi forventer at afholde samtaler onsdag den 12. august 2026.




Søg stillingen ved at trykke på linket ”Søg stillingen” eller via vores hjemmeside www.vejdirektoratet.dk. Vedhæft ansøgning, cv, eksamenspapirer og andre relevante dokumenter.

Vi tror på, at et inkluderende og mangfoldigt arbejdsmiljø styrker fællesskabet og den måde, vi løser opgaverne på. Vi prioriterer en medarbejdersammensætning, der afspejler samfundet og giver lige muligheder for alle. Derfor opfordrer vi alle kvalificerede til at søge stillinger uanset alder, køn, seksuel orientering, religion, politisk anskuelse, handicap, politisk anskuelse eller etnisk tilhørsforhold.

Afdelingen har fokus på digitalisering og teknologi og består primært af ingeniører, der arbejder med vejes funktionelle og strukturelle opbygning og nedbrydning. Vi er en fagligt stærk afdeling med engagerede kolleger og højt til loftet.


Mange af os ved meget om veje, det behøver du ikke gøre, før du begynder. Til gengæld får du en central rolle i at arbejde med vores data i praksis og bidrage til, at samarbejdet mellem fagområdet, IT og leverandører fungerer. Vi er 20 ansatte og ser frem til at byde en ny kollega velkommen.


Dine løn- og ansættelsesvilkår

Du vil blive ansat og aflønnet efter gældende statslig overenskomst med mulighed for forhandling af tillæg alt efter dine kvalifikationer. Du ansættes som udgangspunkt efter én af følgende overenskomster:


Som akademiker ansættes du efter overenskomsten mellem Finansministeriet og AC. Du indplaceres på AC-skalaens basisløntrin efter anciennitet.


Som it-medarbejder ansættes du efter gældende fællesoverenskomst mellem Finansministeriet og Offentligt Ansattes Organisationer (OAO) og organisationsaftale for it-medarbejdere (PROSA) i statens tjeneste. Som udgangspunkt er stillingen placeret i den relevante løngruppe.


Du kan se basisløntrinnene i Statens lønoversigt: loenoversigten-1april-2026.pdf


Du ansættes med stillingsbetegnelsen: Ingeniør.


Forventet ansættelsesdato er d. 1. oktober 2026.


Kontakt Vejdirektoratets HR på rekruttering@vd.dk, hvis du har spørgsmål til lønindplacering. Vi gør opmærksom på, at vi kun oplyser startløn/basisløn for stillingen, og at eventuelle tillæg først forhandles i forbindelse med et konkret løntilbud.


Du bliver en del af en organisation, hvor fleksibilitet, kompetenceudvikling og arbejdsliv i balance står højt på dagsordenen. Der vil være mulighed for ugentlige hjemmearbejdsdage.


Dit ansættelsesområde vil være By-, land- og transportministeriet med de til enhver tid tilhørende institutioner. Tjenestested er Vejdirektoratet i afdelingen Befæstelser, hvor du ansættes i Drift- og Teknikdivisionen på adressen Guldalderen 12, 2640 Hedehusene (Fløng).


Læs mere

Vil du vide mere om Vejdirektoratet, og hvad det vil sige at arbejde hos os kan du læse mere om vores opgaver og medarbejdere på www.vejdirektoratet.dk.


Hvis du vil vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte afdelingsleder Ghita Berg på telefon 7244 7141 eller 2290 7024.


Er du interesseret?

Send din ansøgning snarest muligt dog ikke senere end søndag den 2. august 2026. Vi forventer at afholde samtaler onsdag den 12. august 2026.


Søg stillingen ved at trykke på linket ”Søg stillingen” eller via vores hjemmeside www.vejdirektoratet.dk. Vedhæft ansøgning, cv, eksamenspapirer og andre relevante dokumenter.

Vi tror på, at et inkluderende og mangfoldigt arbejdsmiljø styrker fællesskabet og den måde, vi løser opgaverne på. Vi prioriterer en medarbejdersammensætning, der afspejler samfundet og giver lige muligheder for alle. Derfor opfordrer vi alle kvalificerede til at søge stillinger uanset alder, køn, seksuel orientering, religion, politisk anskuelse, handicap, politisk anskuelse eller etnisk tilhørsforhold.

Title: AI Software Developer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
The Team & The Mission

3Shape Audio is on a journey in transforming the hearing aid industry in working digitally to improve the production of custom fit hearing aids for the convenience of the patients. The journey started around 25 years ago. Now we are on a mission to disrupt the market by automating the CAD modelling of the hearing aids using AI that will bring the production even closer to the patients.




We are expanding our engineering organization working on AI solutions that power our next-generation audio products. You will join a highly skilled, cross-functional, international team building robust desktop and web applications used daily by professionals around the world.

You will be based in our Copenhagen office and collaborate closely with colleagues in other countries, working in an agile setup focused on high quality, predictable delivery, and continuous improvement.




Your role & responsibilities

Develop and maintain AI and classical algorithms on 3D meshes using Python and C#
Develop AI methods for optimizing the 3D design of custom fit hearing aids – for comfort, look and functionality.
Work closely with audiologists to understand and define the problem
Evaluate and document the performance of algorithms
Participate in defining, developing and testing of MLOps procedures (data management, deployment of algorithms, etc.) for the team and wider AI organization
Participate in knowledge sharing and cross-team collaboration in the wider AI organization




What You Bring

You’ll be a great fit if you have most of the following:

Master’s degree or a PhD in Data Science, Computer Science, Mathematics, Bioinformatics, Physics, Engineering or a related field
Proven experience with Machine Learning (e.g. deep neural networks, supervised and unsupervised learning), preferably within image processing or processing of 3D data
Solid software development skills, preferably in Python and/or C#
Mindset for quality, flexibility, creativity and emerging technologies
Have a mindset of continuous improvement and quality within software development




Why Join 3Shape

Being part of 3Shape means:

Meaningful impact – Your work helps change the future of dentistry and audio technologies
Strong culture – Professional, friendly, and supportive environment with talented colleagues
Continuous growth – Opportunities to grow your skills, take ownership, and influence architecture and design
Healthy work-life balance – Flexible remote work options and a sustainable pace
Stability & care – A company that continues to invest in Ukraine and stand by its employees




About 3Shape

3Shape develops 3D scanners and software solutions that enable dental and hearing professionals to treat more people, more effectively. Our products are market-leading innovative solutions that make a real difference in the lives of both patients and professionals around the world.




3Shape is headquartered in Copenhagen, with development teams in Denmark, Ukraine, North Macedonia and a production site in Poland. We are a truly global company with presence in Europe, Asia and the Americas. Founded in 2000, today we serve customers in over 100 countries, and our growing talent pool of 2000+ employees spans 35+ nationalities.




If you are looking for stability, meaningful work, and confidence in your future, this is the right place for you.




Interested?

If this sounds like a strong match for your skills and ambitions, send us your CV and let’s talk. Applicants will be reviewed continuously; however, we plan on conducting interviews in August.

Join us and help shape the future of audio healthcare solutions at scale.




We encourage all relevant applicants to apply. We are committed to celebrating human diversity, and we trust that the best way to reach outstanding business results, is by welcoming diverse people into our community.

Title: Senior/Lead Data Scientist
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Build end-to-end AI/ML solutions that shape commercial decisions at Novo Nordisk – from forecasting and causal inference to GenAI assistants and agentic workflows. If you love taking models all the way to production and working shoulder-to-shoulder with the people who use them, read on.

Your new role

You'll join the Commercial Data Science & AI team to develop and scale advanced analytics, machine learning and AI across our commercial organisation. As a Senior/Lead Data Scientist, you'll build end-to-end AI/ML solutions – from forecasting and econometrics to GenAI systems (RAG, assistants, agentic workflows) – and take them all the way to production. You'll embed directly with domain experts as both advisor and hands-on builder, working closely with commercial stakeholders and AI Engineers on platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake and Azure.

Your Responsibilities Will Include


Translating commercial business questions into data science, machine learning and model-production solutions
Developing and deploying statistical, econometric and machine-learning models for commercial use cases – forecasting, segmentation, targeting, propensity, experimentation, causal inference and commercial optimisation
Building and shipping GenAI applications end-to-end – RAG solutions, GenAI assistants and agentic or workflow-based automation
Evaluating model performance with appropriate statistical, ML and business metrics, and explaining assumptions, limitations, uncertainty and recommendations to non-technical stakeholders
Taking your work to production: version control, reusable and tested code, documentation and MLOps best practice – including experiment tracking, model registries and monitoring
Collaborating with AI Engineers and platform teams where models are integrated into AI products or user-facing solutions
Independently scoping complex commercial problems, choosing appropriate methods, and helping set best practice for commercial decision science and productionisation
Leading technical projects and mentoring team members


You are the person who is not both a classic data science expert and a GenAI specialist. The one who brings depth in one area, curiosity for the other, and a willingness to build across both.

Your new department

In Enterprise IT & Quality, you will be part of a business area that drives impact across the full Novo Nordisk value chain. We strive to stay close to our stakeholders in the business to ensure we deliver on their needs, helping reach millions of people living with serious chronic diseases. Our focus is on being at the forefront within our three areas; ensuring the highest quality for patients, being a trusted tech leader and an environmentally responsible business.

You'll sit within Enterprise AI, in a small, end-to-end Commercial Data Science & AI team where scientists and engineers work side by side – you own the modelling, and you're never far from the engineering help that gets it live. We're cloud-native (Databricks, Snowflake, Azure) and value agility, diverse thinking and continuous learning in a culture built on feedback, honesty and fun.

Your Skills & Qualifications

We're looking for people with deep expertise in one or more of: statistics, econometrics, forecasting, causal inference, segmentation or applied/commercial ML; and/or model production and MLOps; and/or GenAI engineering – LLM applications, RAG and agents.

You'll Bring


Several years' experience developing, deploying or supporting data science, machine learning, statistical or AI models on real commercial or business data
Strong Python and SQL, with good coding practice (Git, documentation, reusable and tested code), and a strong background in model validation, interpretation, uncertainty and business impact
The ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and a genuine interest in solving commercial problems – not only technical modelling ones
An AI-native way of working, comfortable with AI-assisted development tooling (Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot)
An MSc, PhD or equivalent practical experience in statistics, econometrics, data science, mathematics, computer science, engineering or a related quantitative field


Depending on your strengths, you'll bring depth in several of:


Practical MLOps – CI/CD for models, model registries and experiment tracking (e.g. MLflow), and model monitoring
Hands-on GenAI – LLM applications, RAG (embeddings, vector search, retrieval and evaluation), prompt engineering, and agentic/workflow-based solutions
APIs and backend development, testing and CI/CD
Deploying models with GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps


It would be an advantage if you also bring some of:


A track record of taking work to production – whether classical models or AI applications – and a solid understanding of production concerns: reliability, monitoring, versioning, security, access control and maintainability
LLM evaluation, prompt/version management, guardrails and observability (e.g. LangFuse / LangSmith); monitoring quality, latency, reliability and cost of AI products
Experience from pharma, healthcare, life sciences or commercial analytics and consumer behavior


Working at Novo Nordisk

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

At Novo Nordisk, you’ll join a global healthcare company with a unique culture and strong results. We offer continuous learning, career development, and benefits tailored to your life and career stage.

Salary: For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 651,000.00 to 956,900.00 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position.

The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.

Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.

Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here.

Deadline

12 July 2026. Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Title: Senior Data Scientist - Store Partnering
Place: Albertslund, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Senior Data Scientist – Store Partnering

Coop Danmark, Albertslund (Possibility of remote work)

Do you want to be a key driver in establishing an ambitious new team where decisions are founded in thorough data analysis and where machine learning forecasting methodologies are at the heart of the roadmap? Then this is your opportunity to make your mark on the future of the supply chain at Coop!

Your new team

In the role of Senior Data Scientist you will become a central part of the new Store Partnering team. The team will be heavily focused on spearheading a new approach to data centricity in Coop, and as the team is newly formed, you will be highly able to influence ways of working.

About The Position

As a Senior Data Scientist in Coop’s new Store Partnering team, you will have a central responsibility for establishing and developing the data-driven approach to supply chain processes and decisions. Your focus will be on driving the digital agenda forward in Coop’s supply chain, with the aim of improving the quality and efficiency of planning processes. Focus will be on leveraging the vast data landscape available to a large retail organization - turning data into valuable forecasting and planning action. You will work to make technology, data science and AI a pivotal component in how we do planning to unlock efficiencies in planning processes and improvements to consumer availability for millions of consumers every day.

Your Primary Responsibilities


Tools: Developing promotion of quantity estimation methodologies and related tools. Develop the analytics toolbox that makes campaign and store planning tasks faster to execute.
Data driven culture and problem solving: Drive the agenda of putting analysis, math and statistics behind every decision by supporting the store and demand planning team with advanced analysis for operational decisions and development initiatives.
Measurement & Learning: Help establish the KPI and reporting framework, such that continuous development enables and learnings from campaigns and operations can be reused.
RELEX AI: Driving the ML/AI agenda in Relex and operationalizing relevant large data and AI agent use cases. Includes design, diagnostics, and roadmap development.
Guidance for analysts and specialists: Coaching and sparring with the cohort of analysts and specialists across supply chain planning.


About You

You have solid experience with complex analysis, and you thrive on translating data into concrete improvements. You are analytically strong, structured, and have a natural ability to collaborate across disciplines. You enjoy continuously developing ways of working and are energized about the opportunity to be part of shaping up a new team with a big organizational focus. You are driven by creating structure and momentum in complex deliverables and thrive at the intersection of business, IT, and operations. You can communicate complex statistical and technical concepts and findings for the broader organization.

We imagine that you have several of the following competencies:


4–6 years of experience with applied ML in production environment, preferably within a supply chain or retail context.
Master or PHD within mathematical fields
Strong acumen in data science programming language like Python, R or similar data science programming language and experience with MLOps – model versioning, backtesting, and retraining pipelines
Experienced in feature engineering on demand data, including causal factors such as pricing, promotions, events, and weather
Able to translate complex models into business value and communicate technical results to non-technical stakeholders
Has experience with Relex or similar advanced planning systems


Coop Offers

An exciting role in a highly ambitious environment where you will have the opportunity to make a real difference. You will become part of a dedicated team with a lot of hunger and high professional expertise and strong collaboration, where you help shape the digital agenda in Coop’s supply chain.


Influence on projects that impact our 900 stores and 38,000 colleagues
An attractive compensation package including pension, health insurance, and employee benefits
Discounts in Coop's stores, summer cottage rentals, staff clubs, and purchasing schemes
Flexible working hours and the option of one work-from-home day per week


Your base will be at Coop's head office in Albertslund, close to the teams you collaborate with on a daily basis.

Interested?

Are you ready to help shape the future of data-driven decision-making at one of Scandinavia's largest retailers? Then send your CV via our online application form no later than 03-08-2026. We conduct interviews on an ongoing basis, so don't hesitate to apply.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact Supply Chain Manager Bo Bøgvad by email at bo.boegvad@coop.dk

You can read more about Coop as a workplace at www.coop.dk.

We look forward to hearing from you!

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact Supply Chain Manager Bo Bøgvad by email at bo.boegvad@coop.dk

You can read more about Coop as a workplace at www.coop.dk.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Senior Model Developer
Place: Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us in this role where you’ll develop and improve core market models that support short- and mid-term analysis. You’ll combine strong Python development with an interest in energy markets to deliver scalable, reliable models used daily by trading and commercial teams.

Welcome to Forecasting & Market Analysis

You’ll be part of Forecasting & Market Analysis where you together with your colleagues will develop models and forecasts supporting trading and portfolio decisions across power and gas. You’ll work closely with analysts, traders, and platform teams to ensure high-quality data-driven insights. As a team, we collaborate across disciplines, share knowledge, and continuously improve how we model and understand energy markets.

You’ll play an important role in:


Developing our new gas supply and demand to enable our gas trading and portfolio management activities
developing and improving gas and power market models for supply-demand and price formation.
maintaining and operating power production forecast models and related tooling.
integrating key drivers such as weather, fuel prices, outages, and interconnector flows into models.
ensuring model outputs reflect market behaviour across short- and mid-term horizons.
improving model governance, monitoring performance, and keeping assumptions and data up to date.
collaborating with traders, analysts, and developers to deliver actionable insights and recommendations.


To succeed in the role, you:


have a background in quantitative development, engineering, economics, or similar, or equivalent experience.
write scalable, well-tested Python code and apply modern development practices.
are familiar with version control, CI/CD, and collaborative software development.
work confidently with data, including time series analysis, SQL, and data visualisation tools.
are motivated to build expertise in energy markets and translate it into models.
communicate clearly and work effectively both independently and as part of a team.


Maybe you’ve read the above and can see you have some transferable skills, even though they don’t quite match all the points. If you think you can bring something to the team, we still encourage you to apply.

Shape the future with us

Send your application to us as soon as possible. We’ll be conducting interviews on a continuous basis and reserve the right to take down the advert when we’ve found the right candidate.

As an applicant or employee, you may request reasonable work and position accommodation or adjustments via accommodation@orsted.com.

Please note that for your application to be taken into consideration, you must submit your application via our online career pages and answer the screening questions relevant for your country. We don't take applications or inquiries from external recruiters or agencies into account for this position.

Position salary range:

For this position, the expected base pay range is kr51,440.00- kr77,160.00 for the advertised location. The offered base salary will be determined within this range based on relevant skills, experience, and expected role fulfilment within the stated pay range to ensure a fair and competitive offer. Where applicable, you may also be eligible for variable payment and a comprehensive benefits package.

Our benefits package:

For this position, employees who meet eligibility requirements have access to: employer‑paid pension (11% contribution), full insurance coverage (accident, life, critical illness, and health insurance); five weeks of paid holiday plus 10 additional paid days off per holiday year; a 3% holiday allowance; access to Ørsted Life & Fitness Vitality including fitness classes, physiotherapy, massage, and wellbeing programmes; a multimedia package (mobile phone, PC, internet); staff club membership with sport and cultural activities; and access to the company canteen (monthly lunch scheme or daily tickets).

Pre-employment background check:

Certain positions may require a background check as part of the hiring process. If applicable, you will be informed during the recruitment process. Background checks are conducted by an external provider in accordance with local legal requirements.

Title: Data Scientists/udviklere til avanceret modellering af ejendomsvurderinger
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du spille en central rolle i implementeringen og videreudviklingen af verdens mest model- og datadrevne ejendomsvurderingssystem? Er du stærk til statistiske analyser og til at arbejde med store mængder data? Kan du slet ikke stoppe før hele puslespillet er lagt? Så kan du være den udvikler, vi søger.

Danmark har fået verdens mest model- og datadrevne ejendomsvurderingssystem, så danskerne kan få mere retvisende og ensartede ejendomsvurderinger. Danmark har ca. 1,7 mio. ejerboliger og ca. ½ mio. erhvervsejendomme. Langt hovedparten af disse ejendomme vil fremover skulle have en modelbaseret ejendomsvurdering uden yderligere sagsbehandling. Derfor søger vi en eller flere udviklere, der kan være med til at sikre kvaliteten af de kvantitative vurderingsmodeller, som er helt afgørende for fremtidens ejendomsvurderinger.

Vurderingsmæssig sammenhæng mellem modeller og processer

Som udvikler i kontoret Modeludvikling og Økonomisk analyse bidrager du til videreudviklingen af første generation af vurderingsmodellerne. Det kræver bl.a., at erfaringerne fra implementeringen af første generation af modellerne bliver indarbejdet i de kommende generationer. Du får derfor en central rolle i løsningen af tværfaglige opgaver, hvor du sikrer, at der er sammenhæng mellem datagrundlag, vurderingsmodellerne og de manuelle processer, der er grundlaget for vurdering af ejendomme i fremtiden.

Du bidrager til implementering af og videreudvikling af vurderingsmodellerne med statistiske og økonomiske analyser og deltager i test af modellerne. Derudover får du ansvaret for statistiske analyser og kvalitetssikring af de løbende vurderinger, herunder godkendelse af vurderingsmodeller og forslag til forbedringer.

I det daglige arbejde får du et tæt udviklingssamarbejde med kolleger fra vurderingsområdet i Udviklings- og Forenklingsstyrelsen samt den øvrige Vurderingsstyrelse.

Struktureret, en skarp kommunikator og nysgerrig på den gode løsning

Vi forestiller os, at du som minimum har en kandidatgrad med solid samfundsøkonomisk, statistisk eller naturvidenskabelig baggrund. Du er analytisk stærk, har en god forståelse for data og har arbejdet med kvantitative og statistiske analyser. Erfaring med data science fra tidligere beskæftigelse er en fordel, men ikke et krav. Erfaring med programmering, modeludvikling og dataanalyse i R, eller tilsvarende software, samt generel viden om ejendomsvurderingsområdet, vil også være en fordel, men ikke et krav. Nyuddannede tages også i betragtning.

For at få succes i jobbet er du herudover:


god til at samarbejde og skabe relationer på tværs af fagligheder
løsningsorienteret med fokus på at sikre fremdrift og skabe resultater
nysgerrig og idérig
struktureret på et metodisk højt niveau med fokus på at levere resultater af høj kvalitet
en skarp kommunikator i både skrift og tale.


Få ansvar og medbestemmelse

Du bliver en del af kontoret Modeludvikling og Økonomisk analyse, hvor vi på nuværende tidspunkt er 22 medarbejdere, der har samfundsøkonomisk eller naturvidenskabelig baggrund.

”Det skal være sjovt at gå på arbejde, og jeg giver ansvar, plads og medbestemmelse” fortæller kontorchefen Søren Hauskov Pilgaard og fortsætter ”Du kan se frem til at arbejde i et dynamisk miljø, hvor vi lægger vægt på et godt sammenhold med plads til humor og en uformel omgangstone, samtidig med, at vi får nye idéer, tager ansvar og har ambitioner om hele tiden at forbedre os.”

Kontakt og ansættelsesvilkår

Ansøgning med CV og eksamenspapirer bedes sendt via linket ”Søg stillingen” senest 12. juli 2026. Vi holder løbende samtaler, så send din ansøgning hurtigst muligt. Opstart 1. september 2026 eller efter aftale.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte kontorchef for Søren Pilgaard på tlf. 2856 8281.

Der kan indgå personprofilanalyse og logisk test i rekrutteringsprocessen.

Du bliver ansat på overenskomstvilkår i henhold til enhver tid gældende overenskomst for akademikere i staten (AC). Afhængig af dine kvalifikationer kan du blive ansat som fuldmægtig, special- eller chefkonsulent. Ansættelse vil være Skatte- og Vækstministeriet med tilhørende institutioner.

Arbejdsstedet er Lindevangs Allé 8-12, 2000 Frederiksberg.

Forud for ansættelse indhentes referencer og straffeattest.

Læs mere om ansættelse i Vurderingsstyrelsen her.

Om Vurderingsstyrelsen

’Vi sikrer, at boligejerne får en mere retvisende og gennemskuelig ejendomsvurdering’

Vurderingsstyrelsen varetager alle forhold om ejendomsvurdering og beskatning af ejendomme. Styrelsens opgave er at udarbejde mere retvisende og gennemskuelige offentlige vurderinger af grunde og ejendomme og sikre korrekt betaling af skatten på baggrund af disse. Fremtidens vurderinger bliver baseret på nye og detaljerede data. Det skal sikre, at borgere og virksomheder kan have tillid til, at de bliver beskattet på et korrekt grundlag. Vores værdier er ansvar, udsyn, faglighed og samarbejde.

Vi er en arbejdsplads med mange forskellige fagligheder. De er alle i spil i dagligdagen, hvor vi lægger vægt på et godt arbejdsmiljø og en stærk samarbejdskultur, hvor alle trives, tager ansvar og bidrager til den fælles opgave. Vi har et godt kollegaskab med en uformel omgangstone og vi tror på, at en fornuftig balance mellem arbejdsliv og privatliv giver motiverede og tilfredse medarbejdere.

Vurderingsstyrelsen har ca. 1400 ansatte og er en arbejdsplads i vækst, og vi har derfor løbende brug for nye, engagerede kolleger.

Læs mere om Vurderingsstyrelsen på www.vurdst.dk .

Vurderingsstyrelsen opfordrer alle kvalificerede uanset alder, køn, seksuel orientering, religion eller etnisk tilhørsforhold til at søge stillingen.
Show more

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Veo is a global leader in AI-based sports camera technology. Our innovative, fully automatic camera solution enables sports teams to record matches and training sessions without a camera operator. We’re democratizing the world of sports by granting video analysis for teams on all levels—a privilege that used to be only for the few. More than 50,000 clubs in 90+ countries record their games every week.

Growing as fast as we do in Veo means that every day is different, exciting, and challenging, both on the front line and in the back office.

But that’s not the most remarkable thing about us.

The coolest thing is our people. We’ve attracted some of the brightest minds in the industry. They are the reason we can create a great product and do it while enjoying ourselves.

As a Machine Learning Engineer at Veo you will get to work on challenging problems and directly contribute to the value created by our AI-driven end-user products.

You will become part of our AI team, which comprises 15 researchers and engineers responsible for all stages in the machine learning lifecycles across different projects - from scoping and defining data annotation tasks to modeling, validation, and deployment. You will be free to determine the directions of the project you work on while also getting feedback and being encouraged to spar with the rest of the team to assist each other in improving and succeeding.

We stay current with the latest research and continuously discuss concepts and ideas in recent papers to assess their relevance to our tasks and product-specific challenges.

We are looking to add yet another ambitious, high-performing junior to senior engineer/researcher with proven experience from real-world machine learning projects to the team. Experience with computer vision is advantageous but not a must.

What you will do:



Train state-of-the-art machine learning models
Write efficient inference pipelines for cloud and edge
Define and supervise data annotation tasks
Improve evaluation schemes to increase understanding of model performance and shortcomings
Contribute to long-term planning and prioritization of tasks




What you will need:



MSc or PhD in a relevant field
Hands-on experience with real-world, large-scale machine-learning projects
Great coding skills
Skilled at both autonomous tasks and contributing to group efforts
Ability to examine details closely and critically




Our Copenhagen office mirrors our innovative technology – with an indoor ball court, rooftop terrace, and well-equipped gym. We have created a facility that supports connecting disciplines across the business, facilitates creative thinking, and gives the space to engage with colleagues, our global partners, and the entire Veo community.

Values we hold at Veo:

Dare - We take initiative, stay curious, and challenge the status quo in our work.

Own it - We drive impact by identifying opportunities, taking responsibility, and confidently executing.

Level up - We embrace growth, continuously learning and shaping our skills.

Play as a team - We collaborate across a diverse organization, leveraging shared insights for stronger

outcomes.

Customer Centric - We deliver value by having close partnerships and a deep understanding of our customers (internally and externally)

We value diversity and inclusion and welcome applicants from all backgrounds, even if your experience doesn’t completely match every qualification listed. What matters most is your creativity, potential, and ability to make meaningful contributions.

Title: Advanced Analytics Specialist, Supply Chain Intelligence
Place: Ballerup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about driving high-impact projects in a global supply chain? Do you want to play a key role in delivering transformational initiatives that shape how Ambu scales, operates, and delivers value to patients worldwide?

Ambu is transforming – and this is your opportunity to shape how intelligence drives our supply chain.

We have recently evolved our analytics function into a Supply Chain Intelligence team within our Global Supply Chain organization, and we are seeking an Advanced Analytics Specialist to join us. The team’s ambition is clear: moving from describing what happened to knowing what to do next. You will play a central role in delivering this journey; building predictive and prescriptive models, enabling what-if scenarios and decision support, and strengthening the data foundation that underpins it all; while helping build a data-driven culture across the global supply chain.

At Ambu, we are on an ambitious growth journey - scaling our operations and capabilities to meet the rising global demand for our innovative medical solutions. As part of this journey, you will join a highly motivated team that values initiative, impact, and collaboration; and you will have a front-row seat in maturing how data and intelligence power our global supply chain.

The role

As an Advanced Analytics Specialist, your primary responsibility is to design, build, and deploy analytics solutions that move our global supply chain from descriptive dashboards to predictive and prescriptive intelligence. You will work across our five focus areas: data foundation, reporting & visibility, advanced analytics, decision support, and analytics enablement; with the greatest emphasis on advanced analytics and decision support.

This is a hands-on role where technical depth meets business-centric problem-solving. Working closely with the Advanced Analytics Manager, GSC, you will partner with stakeholders across corporate functions and manufacturing sites – bridging business and IT – to ensure that data becomes a strategic asset embedded in how Ambu’s supply chain makes decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Advanced analytics & decision support (primary focus)


Develop, deploy, and maintain predictive and prescriptive models (e.g., forecasting, anomaly detection, optimization) that address concrete supply chain challenges across planning, operations, and distribution
Build what-if scenario and simulation capabilities that give leadership actionable decision support; not just reporting
Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical problems, and analytical results into clear recommendations for stakeholders and leadership
Automate recurring analyses and workflows to free capacity across the organization for higher-value work
Take ownership of solutions end-to-end: from problem framing and data preparation to model deployment in collaboration with back-end IT, monitoring, and adoption


Data foundation, visibility & analytics enablement


Contribute to advancing our data foundation toward a single source of truth; improving data quality, governance, and data models in collaboration with IT
Develop and enhance Power BI dashboards and standard GSC reporting where they create genuine visibility and value
Support upskilling across the global supply chain through training, documentation, and self-service enablement; helping embed a data-driven culture
Stay curious about new technologies (AI, machine learning, modern analytics tooling) and proactively bring relevant opportunities into the team


Suggested candidate profile

Skills

Skills and experience can be gained in many ways, and we understand that the best fit for the role cannot be described on paper. You are, however, likely to succeed in this position if you have:


2–5 years of experience in analytics, data science, or business intelligence roles. You may have gained your experience in a consultancy, an industrial company, or a data-driven scale-up
Strong hands-on skills in SQL and Python (or R) for data preparation, analysis, and modelling
Solid experience with Power BI – including DAX and data modelling – and an eye for visualizations that drive decisions
Experience building predictive models (e.g., forecasting or machine learning) and bringing at least one into real operational use; you know the difference between a notebook and a solution people rely on
Strong business translation skills; you can turn a vague business question into an analytical approach, and an analytical result into a clear recommendation
A working understanding of supply chain processes (e.g., planning, manufacturing, logistics, or distribution); you don't need to be a supply chain expert, but you can speak the language of operations and understand the physical flows behind the data
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills; you build trust quickly and collaborate effectively across functions and levels
A proactive, structured and independent way of working: you thrive in a maturing environment where you help build the way, not just follow it


It is an advantage if you have experience with the following:


Deeper experience within supply chain planning, S&OP, or distribution analytics
ERP data (e.g., Infor), cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Fabric), or optimization and simulation techniques
Experience in integrating new data (e.g., through API/EDI) into database and semantic models
Experience in training or upskilling non-technical colleagues
Exposure to a GxP-regulated industry


Please note that due to the upcoming summer holiday period, the recruitment process may take slightly longer than usual. We expect to begin the interview process at the beginning of August.

Ambu – a visionary and international workplace where your efforts matter

Ambu is a company that expands rapidly and has ambitious growth targets. We meet these targets through interdisciplinary teamwork between motivated and highly skilled employees. Your contributions and efforts are crucial to our success and by joining us you will get the opportunity to work in an international head office with an informal working environment.

We offer great opportunities for personal development. Furthermore, we offer a wide range of professional, social and financial employee benefits in addition to exciting job challenges and continuous professional and personal development.

Are you passionate about driving high-impact projects in a global supply chain? Do you want to play a key role in delivering transformational initiatives that shape how Ambu scales, operates, and delivers value to patients worldwide?

Ambu is transforming – and this is your opportunity to shape how intelligence drives our supply chain.

We have recently evolved our analytics function into a Supply Chain Intelligence team within our Global Supply Chain organization, and we are seeking an Advanced Analytics Specialist to join us. The team’s ambition is clear: moving from describing what happened to knowing what to do next. You will play a central role in delivering this journey; building predictive and prescriptive models, enabling what-if scenarios and decision support, and strengthening the data foundation that underpins it all; while helping build a data-driven culture across the global supply chain.

At Ambu, we are on an ambitious growth journey - scaling our operations and capabilities to meet the rising global demand for our innovative medical solutions. As part of this journey, you will join a highly motivated team that values initiative, impact, and collaboration; and you will have a front-row seat in maturing how data and intelligence power our global supply chain.

The role

As an Advanced Analytics Specialist, your primary responsibility is to design, build, and deploy analytics solutions that move our global supply chain from descriptive dashboards to predictive and prescriptive intelligence. You will work across our five focus areas: data foundation, reporting & visibility, advanced analytics, decision support, and analytics enablement; with the greatest emphasis on advanced analytics and decision support.

This is a hands-on role where technical depth meets business-centric problem-solving. Working closely with the Advanced Analytics Manager, GSC, you will partner with stakeholders across corporate functions and manufacturing sites – bridging business and IT – to ensure that data becomes a strategic asset embedded in how Ambu’s supply chain makes decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Advanced analytics & decision support (primary focus)

  • Develop, deploy, and maintain predictive and prescriptive models (e.g., forecasting, anomaly detection, optimization) that address concrete supply chain challenges across planning, operations, and distribution
  • Build what-if scenario and simulation capabilities that give leadership actionable decision support; not just reporting
  • Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical problems, and analytical results into clear recommendations for stakeholders and leadership
  • Automate recurring analyses and workflows to free capacity across the organization for higher-value work
  • Take ownership of solutions end-to-end: from problem framing and data preparation to model deployment in collaboration with back-end IT, monitoring, and adoption

Data foundation, visibility & analytics enablement

Title: Quantitative analyst for Short-term Power Trading
Description:
Experienced in quantitative modeling for energy or finance? Skilled in programming? Join us as a quantitative analyst for Intraday Analysis and make a real impact.




Here’s what the job entails

In this role, you will help optimise our trading performance by developing quantitative models that impact the decision-making process across our trading teams.




More specifically, your responsibilities include:

developing and enhancing quantitative models to drive trading decisions
taking active part in coding algorithmic trading infrastructure and optimisation engines used for live trading
conducting market research and strategy analysis aimed at improving overall PnL and operational robustness
collaborating with team members to ensure the operational stability of algorithms and tools developed by the team




Meet the team

As our new quantitative analyst, you will join our Intraday Analysis team, where we support the trading and asset optimisation teams by building quantitative models and trading tools. Our primary area of responsibility is to develop strategies, trading tools, and quantitative models that enable decision-making for our proprietary trading and asset business.




We are a team of 6 specialists in energy trading with backgrounds in economics and mathematics. We are a curious, dedicated, and highly analytical team who always strive to improve processes, systems, and strategies to fine-tune the infrastructural engine of our power trading business.




But enough about us… over to you!

As you will be developing models and analyses used for decision-making, we are looking for someone who has a data-driven approach and an eye for market risks while also evaluating other factors in your analyses. Also, as we operate in an ever-changing market, we see you as someone who thrives in a dynamic environment and can quickly adapt to market conditions.




We also imagine that you:

have an educational background in a quantitative field, for example, economics, mathematics, physics, statistics, data science, or engineering
have experience in a similar position, either in the energy industry, the financial markets or similar
have solid coding skills with the ability to write production-level Python code
have great communication skills as you should be able to explain your analyses to stakeholders who do not have the same insights as our team
are proficient in English - both written and verbal




Please note that prior knowledge of the energy sector is not required for this role.




We’ll take good care of you

At Danske Commodities, we take great care of our people. Joining us means you’ll get a lot of great perks – including social events, cultural experiences, tasty food and benefits to better your health, your life and your future.




Here are some of the benefits we offer our employees:

Shape your development tailored growth plans, 100+ DC University courses & Harvard Learning access

100+ yearly events from office parties to DC Sports events, CS:GO team, student network & more

Global career paths as part of our parent company, Equinor’s 20,000-strong international network

Flexible work life adjustable hours, part-time options & up to 2 work from home days/week

Fuel Fridays one paid Friday off every month (July and December excluded)

Top-tier parental leave up to 26 weeks paid, pension during leave & post-leave support

Unlimited paid child's sick days because family comes first

Extra time off 5 additional days on top of your 5 weeks

Senior days extra time off if you’re 55+

Attractive package 10% pension, health insurance, bonus scheme & more

Share savings the option to join Equinor’s attractive share programme

Home setup covered paid internet, phone & IT gear

Free tickets & discounts culture, family fun, hotels, restaurants & more

Organic meals light breakfast, lunch, snacks, dinner & take-away

Barista café high-quality coffee, always on us

On-site wellness state-of-the-art gym, physio, chiro and massage therapy

New HQ in the heart of Aarhus with stunning views, inspiring architecture and close to public transport

…. and much more

Title: AI Engineer Consultant
Description:
Introduction

Vil du hjælpe nogle af Danmarks største virksomheder med at realisere deres AI-potentiale? Vi søger en nysgerrig og ambitiøs AI Engineer til vores konsulentteam, som brænder for at omsætte data og AI til konkrete forretningsresultater. Rollen spænder bredt - fra klassisk data science og machine learning til de nyeste muligheder inden for Generativ AI og AI-agenter.

IBM Client Innovation Center er en del af IBM Consulting og leverer rådgivning, udvikling og implementering af innovative teknologiløsninger til både danske og internationale virksomheder. Vi kombinerer dyb teknisk ekspertise med stærk forretningsforståelse og hjælper vores kunder med at accelerere digital transformation, innovation og værdiskabelse.

Hos os får du mulighed for at arbejde med de nyeste AI-teknologier og være med til at udvikle løsninger, der skaber reel værdi for nogle af Danmarks mest spændende organisationer.

Your Role And Responsibilities

Som AI Engineer hos os bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt miljø, hvor vi hjælper kunderne med at løfte deres tekniske fundament og skabe målbar værdi gennem AI. Du vil arbejde tæt sammen med kunderne om at designe, udvikle og implementere løsninger, der spænder fra dataplatforme og Databricks-pipelines til machine learning-modeller, AI-løsninger og LLM-baserede AI-agenter.

Hos os får du det bedste fra begge verdener. Mange af vores projekter løber over flere år og giver mulighed for at skabe langsigtet værdi for kunden, mens andre er kortere og mere intensive. Det betyder, at du løbende bliver eksponeret for nye brancher, teknologier og forretningsudfordringer - og udvikler dig hurtigt både fagligt og personligt.

Dine Opgaver Vil Blandt Andet Være At


Arbejde med de nyeste teknologier inden for AI, Generativ AI og Agentic AI.
Designe, udvikle og operationalisere machine learning- og AI-løsninger i produktionsmiljøer.
Udvikle intelligente applikationer ved hjælp af LLM’er, RAG, AI-agenter og Copilot-teknologier.
Bygge robuste data- og AI-pipelines på moderne cloud-platforme som Azure og Databricks.
Samarbejde tæt med kunder om at omsætte forretningsmæssige udfordringer til skalerbare tekniske løsninger.
Bidrage til arkitektur, teknologivalg og best practices inden for moderne AI-udvikling.
Holde dig opdateret på nye teknologier og aktivt bidrage til at styrke vores AI-kompetencer og innovationsarbejde.


Required Technical And Professional Expertise

For at få succes i rollen forestiller vi os at du:


Har erfaring inden for data engineering, data science eller AI – typisk 3-4 års erfaring eller er en stærk profil med potentiale til hurtigt at udvikle sig mod seniorniveau.
Har solid forståelse for machine learning, deep learning, AI (herunder Generativ og Agentic AI) samt stærke kompetencer i Python og SQL.
Har kendskab til Databricks og moderne data- og AI-arkitekturer.
Har praktisk erfaring med Azure Data & AI-platformen og trives med at bygge robuste løsninger i pro-code miljøer fremfor low-code/no-code.
Er nysgerrig, læringsorienteret og motiveres af tæt samarbejde med både kunder og kolleger.
Kommunikerer klart og professionelt på både dansk og engelsk.


Vi tilbyder

Hos IBM får du mulighed for at arbejde i frontlinjen af teknologi og innovation. Du bliver en del af et fællesskab, hvor læring, videndeling og personlig udvikling er en naturlig del af hverdagen. Samtidig får du adgang til et stærkt netværk og mulighed for at lære af nogle af branchens mest erfarne specialister og ledere.

Desuden Tilbyder Vi


Et stærkt fagligt miljø, hvor ekspertise går hånd i hånd med et inkluderende og socialt fællesskab.
Adgang til et af branchens stærkeste læringsmiljøer med træning, certificeringer og kontinuerlig kompetenceudvikling gennem IBMs globale læringsplatform for mere end 250.000 IBM-medarbejdere, så du hele tiden kan udvikle dig i takt med nye teknologier, markeder og kundebehov.
Spændende projekter, der giver variation, ansvar og mulighed for at skabe reel værdi fra dag ét.
Dygtige kolleger og en ledelse, der prioriterer både faglig udvikling, trivsel og arbejdsglæde.
Fleksibilitet og frihed under ansvar i et dynamisk og tillidsbaseret arbejdsmiljø.
En arbejdsplads med base på Aarhus Ø samt mulighed for at arbejde tæt sammen med kunder på tværs af brancher og lokationer.


Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, så er du velkommen til at kontakte Data & AI Practice Lead, Anders Schaumann på anders.schauman@ibm.com.

Title: Data Analytics Specialist
Description:
Job Description:

What the job is:

At Satair , data and analytics are among our most powerful levers for driving a culture of continuous improvement across the entire organization. They play a vital role in enhancing operational efficiency, boosting competitiveness, engaging employees, and most importantly, delivering outstanding customer satisfaction.

As a Data Analytics Specialist , you will join our dynamic Data & Infrastructure team , where we are dedicated to empowering the business with self-service tools, robust data foundations, and scalable cloud infrastructure solutions. Our mission also includes upskilling teams across the organization to build long-term capabilities and promote a data-driven mindset.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with functional teams across all areas of the Satair organization. You’ll act as a trusted advisor, partnering with business analysts and domain experts to provide technological insights, define data standards, conduct diagnostics, and apply advanced analytics to optimize processes and maximize value for our customers, as well as making sure the underlying infrastructure and pipelines are running smoothly and as effective as possible.

Our team is truly global, reflecting Satair’s international footprint with colleagues and offices all across the globe. This means you will work in small, agile, cross-functional teams, focused on delivering practical, high-impact solutions side-by-side with the business.

Task and responsibilities:


Improve and maintain our key pipelines running on the Airbus Skywise platform - a customized version of Palantir Foundry. Main tasks will be focusing on optimisation, automation and improving the backend pipelines and flows.
Support the business with in-depth data analysis and experimentation to uncover valuable business insights and support data-driven decision-making.
Engage with functional teams to understand end-to-end processes, identify pain points, and uncover opportunities for improvement through data and emerging technologies.
Translate analysis into actionable outcomes by developing automated tools, visual dashboards, reports, or workflows that enhance productivity and business value.
Design and deliver Minimum Viable Products (MVPs) to quickly test, validate, and address critical business challenges.
Act as a coach and advisor to cross-functional teams, guiding them on data quality improvement plans, and supporting them in developing their own MVPs and analytics use cases.


How will you contribute to the team:

We are looking for a dynamic, business-savvy individual who is passionate about data and technology, with a strong drive to deliver impactful results through collaboration and innovation.


You are a quick learner, passionate about your work and driven to get things done with a collaborative way of working.
You bring a pragmatic, solution-oriented mindset-comfortable with rapid experimentation and iteration when building digital products and tools.
You understand the intersection of data and business processes and are passionate about solving real-world industrial and operational challenges.
Robust coding abilities (Python, SQL, Java) & experience in working with APIs, algorithms, data structures would be a plus.
Experience with DevOps tools / modern cloud stack & practices would be a plus.
Experience working with Data Mesh principles such as decentralized data ownership, domain-aligned data products, and self-serve data infrastructure would be a strong plus.
Prior experience with Patantir Foundry would be a big plu
A university degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field is preferred.
3-5 years of experience in an analytics, data science, or similar role within a complex business environment.
Strong knowledge of English, both orally and in writing


What does your next challenge look like?

At SATAIR, we believe in sustainable aviation that allows people to travel and connect across the globe.

We are on a mission to provide world class material solutions that keep aircraft flying and extend their lifespan.

In Satair, you can challenge the status quo, pioneer new ways of working, and generate real value for airlines and suppliers worldwide.

Being part of the Airbus family gives us the opportunity to live a global mindset, working in international and diverse teams and collaborating across functions, sites and cultures.

If you want to work as one team embracing a can-do attitude in a demanding environment, then we offer a great place to explore, learn, and grow, making an impact on aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company!

About SATAIR

Satair is an Airbus Services company offering first-class material management solutions in the aviation aftermarket industry. We take pride in delivering an excellent customer experience around the clock, driving innovation, and contributing to aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

With our global functional organisation spanning 12 sites and locations worldwide, and a team of over 2,000 people supporting a $3.9 billion turnover, you will be part of a truly global company that continues to grow year after year.

Welcome to the Satair family!

If you want to know more about our business, have a closer look at our website www.SATAIR.com or check out our SATAIR Youtube Channel at www.youtube.com/sataircompany !

What do we offer at SATAIR?

At SATAIR, you will find much more than a job - we offer you the possibility of growing both your personal and professional capacities in a fun, supportive and informal working environment, where you will be encouraged to engage, share knowledge and ideas.

SATAIR is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We love to bring in people with different backgrounds and experiences as we believe it enhances our performance immensely!

Here is a selection of some of the benefits we offer our employees in Copenhagen:

Financial Benefits: Competitive salary, Employee Stock Ownership Program, Success sharing, Health insurance, Group accident insurance, Employee discounts to local businesses

Employee Health and Wellbeing: On-site gym, App-based health & wellbeing platform, Amazing canteen at a subsided price, Massage scheme
