Job Description:

What the job is:

At Satair , data and analytics are among our most powerful levers for driving a culture of continuous improvement across the entire organization. They play a vital role in enhancing operational efficiency, boosting competitiveness, engaging employees, and most importantly, delivering outstanding customer satisfaction.

As a Data Analytics Specialist , you will join our dynamic Data & Infrastructure team , where we are dedicated to empowering the business with self-service tools, robust data foundations, and scalable cloud infrastructure solutions. Our mission also includes upskilling teams across the organization to build long-term capabilities and promote a data-driven mindset.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with functional teams across all areas of the Satair organization. You’ll act as a trusted advisor, partnering with business analysts and domain experts to provide technological insights, define data standards, conduct diagnostics, and apply advanced analytics to optimize processes and maximize value for our customers, as well as making sure the underlying infrastructure and pipelines are running smoothly and as effective as possible.

Our team is truly global, reflecting Satair’s international footprint with colleagues and offices all across the globe. This means you will work in small, agile, cross-functional teams, focused on delivering practical, high-impact solutions side-by-side with the business.

Task and responsibilities:

  • Improve and maintain our key pipelines running on the Airbus Skywise platform - a customized version of Palantir Foundry. Main tasks will be focusing on optimisation, automation and improving the backend pipelines and flows.
  • Support the business with in-depth data analysis and experimentation to uncover valuable business insights and support data-driven decision-making.
  • Engage with functional teams to understand end-to-end processes, identify pain points, and uncover opportunities for improvement through data and emerging technologies.
  • Translate analysis into actionable outcomes by developing automated tools, visual dashboards, reports, or workflows that enhance productivity and business value.
  • Design and deliver Minimum Viable Products (MVPs) to quickly test, validate, and address critical business challenges.
  • Act as a coach and advisor to cross-functional teams, guiding them on data quality improvement plans, and supporting them in developing their own MVPs and analytics use cases.

How will you contribute to the team:

We are looking for a dynamic, business-savvy individual who is passionate about data and technology, with a strong drive to deliver impactful results through collaboration and innovation.

  • You are a quick learner, passionate about your work and driven to get things done with a collaborative way of working.
  • You bring a pragmatic, solution-oriented mindset-comfortable with rapid experimentation and iteration when building digital products and tools.
  • You understand the intersection of data and business processes and are passionate about solving real-world industrial and operational challenges.
  • Robust coding abilities (Python, SQL, Java) & experience in working with APIs, algorithms, data structures would be a plus.
  • Experience with DevOps tools / modern cloud stack & practices would be a plus.
  • Experience working with Data Mesh principles such as decentralized data ownership, domain-aligned data products, and self-serve data infrastructure would be a strong plus.
  • Prior experience with Patantir Foundry would be a big plu
  • A university degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field is preferred.
  • 3-5 years of experience in an analytics, data science, or similar role within a complex business environment.
  • Strong knowledge of English, both orally and in writing

What does your next challenge look like?

At SATAIR, we believe in sustainable aviation that allows people to travel and connect across the globe.

We are on a mission to provide world class material solutions that keep aircraft flying and extend their lifespan.

In Satair, you can challenge the status quo, pioneer new ways of working, and generate real value for airlines and suppliers worldwide.

Being part of the Airbus family gives us the opportunity to live a global mindset, working in international and diverse teams and collaborating across functions, sites and cultures.

If you want to work as one team embracing a can-do attitude in a demanding environment, then we offer a great place to explore, learn, and grow, making an impact on aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company!

About SATAIR

Satair is an Airbus Services company offering first-class material management solutions in the aviation aftermarket industry. We take pride in delivering an excellent customer experience around the clock, driving innovation, and contributing to aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

With our global functional organisation spanning 12 sites and locations worldwide, and a team of over 2,000 people supporting a $3.9 billion turnover, you will be part of a truly global company that continues to grow year after year.

Welcome to the Satair family!

If you want to know more about our business, have a closer look at our website www.SATAIR.com or check out our SATAIR Youtube Channel at www.youtube.com/sataircompany !

What do we offer at SATAIR?

At SATAIR, you will find much more than a job - we offer you the possibility of growing both your personal and professional capacities in a fun, supportive and informal working environment, where you will be encouraged to engage, share knowledge and ideas.

SATAIR is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We love to bring in people with different backgrounds and experiences as we believe it enhances our performance immensely!

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest independent consumer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!




At Trustpilot, we’re looking for a Machine Learning Engineer who’s ready to tackle real-world challenges and help us build robust, scalable systems for our AI models. If you’re excited about machine learning, enjoy solving complex problems, and want to work in a collaborative environment, this could be the perfect role for you.




Your role as a ML Engineer:




As part of the MLOps team, you’ll work closely with data scientists, software engineers, and other stakeholders to bring machine learning models to life—ensuring they’re deployed, maintained, and monitored efficiently in production. You’ll have the opportunity to improve model performance and infrastructure, all while contributing to Trustpilot’s AI-driven solutions.




Model Deployment: Collaborate with data scientists to take machine learning models from development to production, ensuring high performance and scalability.
Build Pipelines: Develop and maintain data and model pipelines, integrating seamlessly with our existing systems to support reliable, efficient workflows.
CI/CD for ML: Design and implement continuous integration and delivery pipelines to streamline the deployment of machine learning models.
Model Monitoring: Help monitor the performance of machine learning models post-deployment, ensuring reliability, scalability, and quality over time.
Collaboration: Work with cross-functional teams to design solutions that meet business needs while adhering to best practices in machine learning and software engineering.
Optimise: Continuously improve our infrastructure, ensuring we remain at the forefront of AI model production and delivery.
Agentic Development: Develop MCP servers and A2A agents through our internal framework for managing multi agent orchestrated deployments.




Ideal Qualifications:




We’re looking for someone who has a solid technical foundation in both machine learning and software engineering. If you don’t meet every single point below, please still consider applying—what matters most to us is your growth mindset, your ability to learn quickly, and how you work collaboratively.




Experience deploying machine learning models in production environments in cloud platforms like GCP, AWS or Azure.
Experience with CI/CD pipelines for machine learning (e.g., Github action, Docker).
Experience with ML platforms/frameworks (eg. VertexAI, Kubeflow, Sagemaker)
Experience with data processing frameworks and tools(eg. Spark, Databricks), particularly Apache Beam/Dataflow is highly desirable.
Knowledge of monitoring and maintaining models in production.
Experience with performance/cost optimization is highly desirable (eg. Latency, throughput)
Proficiency in Python and relevant ML libraries (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn).
Problem-solving skills with the ability to troubleshoot model and pipeline issues.
Strong communication skills, enabling effective collaboration across teams.




What’s in it for you:




A range of flexible working options to dedicate time to what matters to you
Competitive compensation package + bonus
25 days holiday per year, with an extra 5 days holiday allocated after your 1 year anniversary (prorated)
Two (paid) volunteering days a year to spend your time giving back to the causes that matter to you and your community
Rich learning and development opportunities are supported through the Trustpilot Academy and Blinkist
Comprehensive health package, pension, and full access to Headspace, a popular mindfulness app to promote positive mental health
Paid parental leave
Central office with a laid-back vibe and constant buzz of different languages being spoken everywhere you go It’s complete with a coffee bar, canteen, and table tennis and has a wide variety of refreshments available - you can opt-in for breakfast and lunch at an affordable price
Regular opportunities to connect and get to know your fellow Trusties, including company-wide celebrations and events, ERG activities, and team socials
Opportunity to join the Trustpilot Social Club for a small monthly fee and attend regular social and leisure activities like go-karting, cooking classes, and escape rooms
Employee discounts to restaurants and shops




Still not sure?




We want to be a part of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive world of work for all. We’re excited to hear about your experiences along with how you will contribute to our working culture. Even if you don’t feel you meet all the requirements, we'd still really like to hear from you!




About us




Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.




Today, we have more than 300 million reviews and 64 million monthly active users on average across the globe, with 140 billion annual Trustbox impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1,000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York.




We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas.




Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.




Join us at the heart of trust.




Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.




Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.




If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

Title: Quantitative Developer
Place: Aarhus Municipality, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Build and evolve the core framework behind quantitative decision-making.




We are looking for a Quantitative Developer to join our Quantitative Research team at InCommodities Asset Management. In this role, you’ll build and evolve the core simulation framework used for pricing, valuation, and risk analysis across the company.




From day one, you’ll create the abstractions, tooling, and workflows that make quantitative decision-making scalable, reproducible, and trusted - while also supporting the modelling of structured power products in close collaboration with stakeholders. Sounds like your kind of challenge? Then keep reading.




The Journey

At InCommodities Asset Management, we're not just building a company – we're facilitating the future of renewable energy.

Back in 2022, we launched our dedicated Asset Management (AM) team in Aarhus, Denmark, with a clear ambition: to accelerate the green transition by making renewable energy investments both attractive and bankable. Through Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), we help renewable asset owners and producers unlock value, manage risk, and ensure stable, long-term returns.




In 2025, we took the next bold step – becoming an independent company. This move gives us full autonomy and a sharp commercial focus on asset trading and optimization.

Now, we’re a fast-growing team of 35+ ambitious professionals, and we’re just getting started. With big plans to scale across the entire renewable value chain, now is the perfect time to come on board and be part of something meaningful – and truly exciting.




Ready to rethink the future of energy with us?




What you’ll do

You’ll help build the technical foundation that enables quantitative decision-making across the company. Your core responsibility is to design, build, and evolve our simulation and research framework, ensuring that models and flows are scalable and reproducible across the organization.

This involves ongoing improvements in the engineering maturity of the quant platform by setting standards and practices that keep the codebase robust, maintainable, and production ready. Day-to-day, you’ll:




Build and maintain the core backend for simulation, back testing, and forward-looking market and cashflow analysis
Design and implement a lean, modular framework architecture that balances performance, flexibility, and maintainability as usage grows
Create reusable abstractions and tooling that accelerate research and reduce friction between ideas and implementation
Establish and uphold engineering standards across the quant codebase (testing, CI/CD, structure, documentation, versioning)
Ensure correctness and reproducibility through structured workflows, automated testing, and clear assumptions
Drive modelling and forecasting of structured power products in close collaboration with the Portfolio Management team and other stakeholders




What you bring

A master’s or PhD in a quantitative field (e.g. Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or Data Science)
Relevant professional experience from a financial company (e.g. bank, fund/asset-manager, trading company) and/or a strong academic background with hands-on technical projects
Strong skills in Python and experience with SQL
Solid understanding of software engineering fundamentals such as design patterns, API/library design, testing, and CI/CD
Preferably an interest in numerical methods, optimization, dynamic programming, and asset pricing
You’re fluent in English, both written and verbal




How you work

You bring strong commercial drive and are motivated by delivering solid results
You’re structured and systematic with a strong sense of correctness and quality
You are accountable and thrive by taking ownership of several areas of work
You enjoy building robust systems and are comfortable working independently on complex problems that others depend on
You’re people-oriented and collaborate naturally with both quant and commercial stakeholders
You bring a can-do attitude and thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment




What you can expect

A tech-driven scale-up company, which challenges how energy assets are traded and optimized
A great start-up environment with a high degree of autonomy and flexibility to drive real impact
Curious, ambitious and bold people who come together to rethink what’s possible
A unique company culture where we live up to our values: Honesty, Transparency, and Rethink
Flat hierarchy — no dress code, no meaningless bureaucracy




Like what you’re reading? Apply now – we’d love to hear from you!

Title: Founding Data/ML Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Who we’re looking for 👀

We're looking for a data/ML engineer to own the brain of Palette: the signal engine.

The ideal person sits at the intersection of four hats:


Data engineer: owns the pipelines at real volume, and takes data privacy and security seriously
ML engineer: embeddings, clustering, retrieval, vector search, LLMs as system components, evals
Data scientist: spots the use cases and drives what we build next on top of the data
Software engineer: comfortable across the stack, comfortable in TypeScript / Postgres


You won't be equally deep in all four - nobody is. But you're genuinely strong in at least one and eager to grow into the rest. Right skills, but more importantly the right mindset: strong product sense, agency, curiosity. You think in relationships and retrieval, and you care about cost and quality. Bonus points for RAG and graph-shaped data.

At Palette you'll drive our signal engine, end to end. Today it captures events and stores them as signals - enough for the briefs and context pages we ship now. Next is a queryable graph of activities: everything that happens across a company's tools, on one timeline, answerable by who, what, when, and meaning, by both people and AI agents.

You work AI-native - use the tools, move fast, own the output. If the AI writes 80% of the code, great; just reason about it and ship.

The stack 🚀


Desktop: Tauri v2, Rust, TypeScript / React / TanStack
Frontend: Next.js (migrating to TanStack)
Backend: Hono
AI Agents: Mastra, plus the coding-agent harnesses we run on-device
Data & infra: PostgreSQL, Redis, Inngest, Nango, Railway
Plus: Linear, GitHub Actions, WorkOS, Cloudflare, Sentry, Incident.io, Requesty, Swarmia, PostHog, Atlas and more.


We're bullish on TypeScript but we're constantly evaluating our approach and open to be challenged.

What you get 💰


Top-tier salary + warrants
Solid office in Copenhagen
The gear you need
Dental insurance
Lunch, snacks & drinks at the office


How we work 🤝


Copenhagen office. Mostly in person, but around two days from home every week.
We aim to stay a small and lean team.
We're all doers and care about our craftsmanship.
We're constantly exploring what AI-native means to us.
We're obsessed with making something people actually want to use, therefore everyone at the company talks to customers regularly.


Who we are 🎨

For the last decade, knowledge work drifted away from its original promise. It was supposed to be about thinking, creating, deciding. Instead it became status meetings, reporting, alignment rituals, and busywork.

AI changes that. But the speed has created a new problem: the individual got faster than the organization. More and more work happens inside sessions between humans and agents, and the context behind that work disappears before anyone else in the company can see it.

Palette is the shared context layer for teams and AI. We ingest signals from the tools teams already use (Slack, Linear, Notion, GitHub, calendar, email), turn them into a living map of the organization, and serve that context to the agents people work with every day.

Palette Desktop is the app where teams put that context to work. Point it at a folder your team shares on Google Drive or Dropbox, and run agents on Anthropic, OpenAI, Mistral, or a local model inside it. More providers ship in regularly. It opens those agents up to the whole company, including non-engineers, and the team picks the model that fits. We shipped Desktop in May, and it's already where most of our own work happens.

We're 6 people in Copenhagen. Four co-founders, two founding engineers. We work with design partners across leading startups, scaleups, and household-name brands. We recently raised our pre-seed and are now bringing on our first commercial hire.

Please note that during week 29-31 there might be slower response times due to our small team taking some vacation ☀️

HTML:
Who we’re looking for 👀

We're looking for a data/ML engineer to own the brain of Palette: the signal engine.

The ideal person sits at the intersection of four hats:

  • Data engineer: owns the pipelines at real volume, and takes data privacy and security seriously
  • ML engineer: embeddings, clustering, retrieval, vector search, LLMs as system components, evals
  • Data scientist: spots the use cases and drives what we build next on top of the data
  • Software engineer: comfortable across the stack, comfortable in TypeScript / Postgres

You won't be equally deep in all four - nobody is. But you're genuinely strong in at least one and eager to grow into the rest. Right skills, but more importantly the right mindset: strong product sense, agency, curiosity. You think in relationships and retrieval, and you care about cost and quality. Bonus points for RAG and graph-shaped data.

At Palette you'll drive our signal engine, end to end. Today it captures events and stores them as signals - enough for the briefs and context pages we ship now. Next is a queryable graph of activities: everything that happens across a company's tools, on one timeline, answerable by who, what, when, and meaning, by both people and AI agents.

You work AI-native - use the tools, move fast, own the output. If the AI writes 80% of the code, great; just reason about it and ship.

The stack 🚀

  • Desktop: Tauri v2, Rust, TypeScript / React / TanStack
  • Frontend: Next.js (migrating to TanStack)
  • Backend: Hono
  • AI Agents: Mastra, plus the coding-agent harnesses we run on-device
  • Data & infra: PostgreSQL, Redis, Inngest, Nango, Railway
  • Plus: Linear, GitHub Actions, WorkOS, Cloudflare, Sentry, Incident.io, Requesty, Swarmia, PostHog, Atlas and more.

We're bullish on TypeScript but we're constantly evaluating our approach and open to be challenged.

What you get 💰

  • Top-tier salary + warrants
  • Solid office in Copenhagen
  • The gear you need
  • Dental insurance
  • Lunch, snacks & drinks at the office

How we work 🤝

  • Copenhagen office. Mostly in person, but around two days from home every week.
  • We aim to stay a small and lean team.
  • We're all doers and care about our craftsmanship.
  • We're constantly exploring what AI-native means to us.
  • We're obsessed with making something people actually want to use, therefore everyone at the company talks to customers regularly.

Title: Product Manager, Data Insights
Description:
About Us

nShift is the leading global provider of cloud delivery management solutions (SaaS), we enable the frictionless shipment and return of almost one billion shipments across 190 countries each year. We are headquartered in London and Oslo and have over 500 employees across offices in Sweden, Finland, Norway, Denmark, the United Kingdom, Poland, the Netherlands, Belgium, and Romania.

Our software is used by many of the world leading e-commerce, retail, manufacturing, and 3PL shippers due to us having over 1000 carriers integrated into our platform, nearly 3 times more than our competitors!

If you buy goods online, there is a strong chance that nShift has powered that delivery, so come and join us as we shape the future of shipping, one frictionless journey at a time.

About You

You think like a product manager and work like a data scientist. You have shipped ML-powered products to real customers and can point to the decisions you made, not just the ones made around you. Messy data, ambiguous model outputs, and stakeholders who don't yet see why the data matters are familiar territory.

You think in terms of compounding: how every data point collected today improves predictions tomorrow, and how that improvement builds a position competitors cannot close. Data insights is nShift's path from a tool company to an intelligence platform, and that scope motivates you rather than overwhelming you.

You are as comfortable in a Jupyter notebook as in a leadership meeting, and you know when each is needed.

Purpose of role

The mission is to define what nShift builds with that data, validate it before engineering commits, and ship intelligence products that help shippers make better decisions. A shipper asking "how can I improve my delivery experience" should get an answer powered by the full network, not just their own history. Done well, that becomes a compounding intelligence layer: every data point collected today improves suggestions tomorrow, and the improvement creates a position no competitor can close.

The intelligence layer draws from two sources. Structured operational data, such as shipment telemetry and carrier performance, feeds statistical models and predictions. Qualitative data, such as customer support interactions, delivery exception messages, and community feedback, carries signal that numbers alone cannot surface. LLMs make that signal extractable and usable at scale. This role owns both.

The role sits at the intersection of product management and data science. You have the technical depth to build ML proof-of-concepts independently and the commercial judgment to translate those capabilities into products customers pay for. You write the strategy and the first notebook.

What You'll Be Doing

Define and own the data driven product strategy


Own the roadmap for nShift's data intelligence layer: carrier performance prediction, cross-customer benchmarking, ML-driven carrier recommendations, and related capabilities.
Translate nShift's data asset into a sequenced product strategy that delivers near-term value while building toward the compounding intelligence layer vision.
Define the information architecture for the data insights layer: what data is collected, how it is normalized (in partnership with adjacent teams), and how it is exposed to upstream products.
Make hard prioritization calls: what to build first to validate the ML hypothesis, what to defer, and what to kill when the data doesn't support the bet.
Own the qualitative intelligence pipeline alongside the quantitative one: define which unstructured sources carry decision-relevant signal (support tickets, delivery exception messages, customer forum, carrier communications), how LLM-extracted insights are structured for downstream use, and how qualitative and quantitative signals combine into a coherent intelligence product.
Own the positioning and differentiation narrative: why this dataset is uniquely nShift's, why no competitor can replicate it, and how to articulate that defensibility to enterprise buyers.



Build and validate before engineering commits


Run ML proof-of-concepts independently: data exploration, feature engineering, model selection, and initial validation, using Python, notebooks, and the data available in nShift's data layer.
Define the evaluation framework for both ML models and LLM pipelines. For ML features: what accuracy thresholds matter, how to measure model performance in production, how to distinguish signal from noise. For LLM-based features: how to assess output consistency, factual grounding, and quality at scale, where traditional accuracy metrics don't apply.
Design lightweight experiments that answer the key questions before a full engineering team is committed. Does the prediction accuracy justify the product? Does cross-customer data improve the model significantly? Is the data we have sufficient, or do we need to instrument more?
Translate POC findings into clear build, kill, or iterate decisions backed by data.



Partner across data, engineering, and commercial teams


Work closely with engineers to define the data models, pipelines, and normalization standards needed to power ML features. You are a customer and co-designer of the data layer, not a passenger.
Drive integration of data insights capabilities into existing products. Work closly with your PM peers to make this happen.
Engage directly with customers and prospects across the full spectrum, from small shippers to global enterprises, to validate intelligence use cases: what decisions do they make today that better data would improve? What would they pay for? What do they trust?
Build the commercial narrative: ROI frameworks, value quantification, and positioning for the target buyer.



Govern the data responsibly


Define the anonymization, aggregation, and consent frameworks that make cross-customer benchmarking legally and ethically sound, and credible to enterprise customers who will ask hard questions.
Work with Legal and the DPO to ensure data usage within the intelligence layer meets GDPR and contractual obligations. The data asset is only valuable if customers trust nShift to use it appropriately.
Establish transparency standards for ML-powered recommendations: when does a merchant see why a carrier was recommended? What level of explainability builds trust without revealing proprietary network data?



What You'll Bring

Must-have


Hands-on data proficiency across both paradigms: you can build and validate ML proof-of-concepts (data exploration, feature engineering, model training, evaluation) using Python and standard ML tooling (scikit-learn, pandas, XGBoost, or equivalent), and you can design and evaluate LLM-based analysis pipelines (prompt design, retrieval-augmented approaches, output quality evaluation). You are not a data scientist or an ML engineer, but you can work like one when the problem requires it.
Shipped ML-powered products: you have personally driven an ML or data intelligence product from initial hypothesis to production, including defining evaluation criteria, partnering with data engineers and scientists, and making commercial decisions based on model performance data.
PM fundamentals with commercial depth: 3+ years shipping B2B SaaS products. You can build a business case, define pricing, run customer discovery, and hold direct conversations with enterprise buyers about ROI.
Data literacy that earns engineering respect: you understand data models, pipeline design, normalization challenges, and the practical constraints of real production data. You can review a data schema and immediately ask the right questions.
Clear communication across the technical and commercial boundary: you can explain a precision-recall tradeoff to a CPO and a carrier event data use case to a data engineer in the same afternoon, without condescending to either.
Excellent written and verbal English: you will write strategy documents, ML experiment briefs, customer-facing product narratives, and present to leadership, often in the same week.



Nice to have


Experience with time-series or logistics domain data: carrier performance modelling, routing optimization, ETA prediction, or related domains.
Familiarity with data platform concepts: data lake houses, feature stores, model registries, observability for ML in production.
Background in cross-customer benchmarking or network intelligence products, where the value comes from aggregation rather than individual customer data.
Experience with GDPR-compliant data product design in a multi-tenant B2B SaaS context.
Nordic or European logistics market experience: carrier landscape, regulatory environment, freight economics.
Track record of building the first data product at a company, defining standards rather than inheriting them.



Please ensure you upload your CV in English

At nShift we believe in embracing diversity in all forms and fostering an inclusive environment for everyone which we believe is essential for our continued success. We're an equal-opportunity employer which means that all applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity, or disability status.

Title: Lead AI Developer
Description:
Hos Envidan udvikler vi softwareløsninger, der gør en forskel for vandforsyning, spildevand og klimatilpasning. Nu tager vi næste skridt og integrerer AI direkte i vores produkter – og derfor søger vi en dygtig person, der kan lede den rejse.

Om rollen

Du bliver vores primære drivkraft på AI og dataanalyse. Du arbejder tæt sammen med vores softwareudviklere, produktteam, data arkitekt og rådgivere om at identificere, designe og implementere AI-funktionalitet, der skaber reel forskel for vores kunder i forsyningssektoren.

Du er ikke typen, der venter på at få opgaver stukket i hånden. Du opsøger aktivt nye muligheder – i dialog med kunder, kollegaer og potentielle eksterne partnere indenfor AI – og du forstår vigtigheden af at sætte AI i produktion på en ansvarlig og bæredygtig måde.

Det er en rolle med stor frihed og bred indflydelse – du er med fra idé til deployment og drift.

Dine ansvarsområder


Proaktivt opsøge og kvalificere relevante AI use cases i tæt dialog med kunder og resten af forretningen
Integrere LLM-baserede løsninger i vores eksisterende softwareplatform, fx intelligente søgefunktioner, automatiske opsummeringer og smarte assistenter til brugerne
Opbygge og forvalte en solid AI governance-ramme for løsninger, der sættes i produktion – herunder ansvarlig AI, datakvalitet og monitorering
Samarbejde tæt med vores data arkitekt om datamodeller, infrastruktur og arkitektur der understøtter AI-løsningerne
Udvikle og vedligeholde AI-pipelines og dataflows i et produktionsmiljø
Samarbejde med produktteamet om brugervenlig AI-integration der føles naturlig, ikke påklistret
Vurdere og implementere eksterne analysemoduler fra partnere, og sikre at de integreres på en ansvarlig og bæredygtig måde i vores platform
Bidrage til vores dataanalysekapacitet – herunder datavisualisering og simple forecast modeller, eksplorative analyser og sparringspartner.


Hvem er du?

Vi forestiller os, at du har en uddannelse indenfor datalogi, software engineering, matematik/statistik eller data science, men har du den rette erfaring og et stærkt teknisk fundament, er din formelle baggrund dog ikke afgørende.

Du har 3–5 års erfaring inden for data science, ML‑engineering eller AI‑produktudvikling og har:


Solid erfaring med LLMs og generativ AI – herunder prompt engineering, RAG, fine-tuning eller API- integration (fx OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral)
Praktisk erfaring med at integrere AI i eksisterende softwaresystemer og produktionsmiljøer og herunder gerne erfaring med softwareudvikling
Kendskab til eller erfaring med AI governance, ansvarlig AI og best practices for AI i produktion
Stærke Python-færdigheder og kendskab til relevante frameworks (LangChain, FastAPI, Pandas, m.fl.)
Erfaring med cloud (gerne Azure) og gerne erfaring med Infrastructure as Code; du er tryg ved at deployere og drifte løsninger i et cloud-miljø
Interesse for eller erfaring fra forsynings-, industri- eller infrastruktursektoren er et plus


Som person trives du i et miljø hvor du tænker både strategisk og skriver kode. Du er proaktiv og opsøgende af natur – du finder selv mulighederne og bringer dem i spil. Som ansvarlig for AI i Envidan Software, evner du at kommunikere tekniske løsninger klart til både kunder og ikke-tekniske kollegaer.

Hvad tilbyder vi?


En nøglerolle i et team på 28 engagerede kollegaer, hvor vi er i gang med at re-designe vores eksisterende applikationer – og nu også integrere AI som centralt element
En arbejdskultur præget af samarbejde, tillid og faglig sparring – vi hjælper hinanden og deler viden, både i dagligdagen og gennem fælles learning sessions
Tæt samarbejde med erfarne softwareudviklere og direkte adgang til produktbeslutninger
Mulighed for at sætte dit præg på produkter, der bruges i kritisk infrastruktur


Vi har interesse i hinanden og vægter et godt socialt miljø højt – udlevet gennem teamarrangementer, faglige klubber og en aktiv personaleforening på tværs af hele organisationen



Stillingen er fuldtid. Vi tror på, at de bedste løsninger opstår når mennesker mødes – og vi lægger vægt på at skabe en stærk sammenhængskraft i teamet. Derfor ser vi gerne, at du er på kontoret i Silkeborg 2–3 dage om ugen. De resterende dage er der fuld fleksibilitet til at arbejde hjemmefra.

Har du spørgsmål til rollen er du velkommen til at kontakte Afdelingschef Thorben Føns på +45 4117 7627 eller thf@envidan.dk. Henvendelser fra rekrutteringsbureauer frabedes.

Ansøgning

Vil du med ombord, så send din ansøgning snarest muligt. Vi behandler ansøgninger fortroligt og løbende, og vi lukker stillingsopslaget, når vi har fundet den rette kandidat.

Om os

Envidan er Nordens ledende vand- og spildevandsspecialist med tre selvstændige forretningsområder: rådgivning, entreprise og software.

Vi hjælper forsyninger, kommuner og industri inden for vandets kredsløb. Og uanset om vi rådgiver om klimatilpasning, leverer færdige anlæg eller udvikler innovative softwareløsninger, er vandfaglighed og digital tænkning altid omdrejningspunktet.

Fra vores kontorer i Norge, Sverige og Danmark bidrager +500 medarbejdere til at løse nogle af vandsektorens største udfordringer: Bæredygtig vandhåndtering, klimatilpasning og sikring af rent drikkevand.

På den måde er vores forretning født med et bæredygtigt DNA. Men vores ambitioner rækker videre. Via vores stærke, tekniske faglighed kan vi anvise praktiske, bæredygtige løsninger på vores kunders konkrete problemer.

Envidan – en del af Rambøll

Hos Envidan bliver du en del af Nordens stærkeste hold af specialister inden for vandets kredsløb. I partnerskab med forsyninger, kommuner og industrier yder vi rådgivning, leverer anlæg og skaber software. Disse tre forretningsområder samlet gør os stærkere, fordi vores kunder får bedre software af en leverandør, der forstår sektoren. Og bedre rådgivning af en leverandør, der også forstår at bygge anlæg.

Du får sektorens skarpeste hoveder som kolleger. Det kan vi tilbyde, fordi vi hvert år allokerer 20 procent af vores overskud i forskning og udvikling.

  • Praktisk erfaring med at integrere AI i eksisterende softwaresystemer og produktionsmiljøer og herunder gerne erfaring med softwareudvikling
  • Kendskab til eller erfaring med AI governance, ansvarlig AI og best practices for AI i produktion
  • Stærke Python-færdigheder og kendskab til relevante frameworks (LangChain, FastAPI, Pandas, m.fl.)
  • Erfaring med cloud (gerne Azure) og gerne erfaring med Infrastructure as Code; du er tryg ved at deployere og drifte løsninger i et cloud-miljø
  • Interesse for eller erfaring fra forsynings-, industri- eller infrastruktursektoren er et plus

Som person trives du i et miljø hvor du tænker både strategisk og skriver kode. Du er proaktiv og opsøgende af natur – du finder selv mulighederne og bringer dem i spil. Som ansvarlig for AI i Envidan Software, evner du at kommunikere tekniske løsninger klart til både kunder og ikke-tekniske kollegaer.

Hvad tilbyder vi?

  • En nøglerolle i et team på 28 engagerede kollegaer, hvor vi er i gang med at re-designe vores eksisterende applikationer – og nu også integrere AI som centralt element
  • En arbejdskultur præget af samarbejde, tillid og faglig sparring – vi hjælper hinanden og deler viden, både i dagligdagen og gennem fælles learning sessions
  • Tæt samarbejde med erfarne softwareudviklere og direkte adgang til produktbeslutninger
  • Mulighed for at sætte dit præg på produkter, der bruges i kritisk infrastruktur

Vi har interesse i hinanden og vægter et godt socialt miljø højt – udlevet gennem teamarrangementer, faglige klubber og en aktiv personaleforening på tværs af hele organisationen



Stillingen er fuldtid. Vi tror på, at de bedste løsninger opstår når mennesker mødes – og vi lægger vægt på at skabe en stærk sammenhængskraft i teamet. Derfor ser vi gerne, at du er på kontoret i Silkeborg 2–3 dage om ugen. De resterende dage er der fuld fleksibilitet til at arbejde hjemmefra.

Har du spørgsmål til rollen er du velkommen til at kontakte Afdelingschef Thorben Føns på +45 4117 7627 eller thf@envidan.dk. Henvendelser fra rekrutteringsbureauer frabedes.

Ansøgning

Vil du med ombord, så send din ansøgning snarest muligt. Vi behandler ansøgninger fortroligt og løbende, og vi lukker stillingsopslaget, når vi har fundet den rette kandidat.

Om os

Envidan er Nordens ledende vand- og spildevandsspecialist med tre selvstændige forretningsområder: rådgivning, entreprise og software.

Vi hjælper forsyninger, kommuner og industri inden for vandets kredsløb. Og uanset om vi rådgiver om klimatilpasning, leverer færdige anlæg eller udvikler innovative softwareløsninger, er vandfaglighed og digital tænkning altid omdrejningspunktet.

Fra vores kontorer i Norge, Sverige og Danmark bidrager +500 medarbejdere til at løse nogle af vandsektorens største udfordringer: Bæredygtig vandhåndtering, klimatilpasning og sikring af rent drikkevand.

På den måde er vores forretning født med et bæredygtigt DNA. Men vores ambitioner rækker videre. Via vores stærke, tekniske faglighed kan vi anvise praktiske, bæredygtige løsninger på vores kunders konkrete problemer.

Envidan – en del af Rambøll

Hos Envidan bliver du en del af Nordens stærkeste hold af specialister inden for vandets kredsløb. I partnerskab med forsyninger, kommuner og industrier yder vi rådgivning, leverer anlæg og skaber software. Disse tre forretningsområder samlet gør os stærkere, fordi vores kunder får bedre software af en leverandør, der forstår sektoren. Og bedre rådgivning af en leverandør, der også forstår at bygge anlæg.

Du får sektorens skarpeste hoveder som kolleger. Det kan vi tilbyde, fordi vi hvert år allokerer 20 procent af vores overskud i forskning og udvikling.

Title: Ansvarlig for data science og analyse (Data scientist)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Lige nu leder vi efter dig, der kan omsætte kvantitative data til meningsfulde indsigter, som understøtter Clevers beslutninger og udvikling af løsninger.

Din rolle

Du vil være med til at drive den grønne omstilling til gavn for hele samfundet. Her spiller virksomheder en vigtig rolle gennem elektrificering af deres bilflåder. For at kunne understøtte denne udvikling er det vigtigt, at vi driver omstillingen baseret på datadrevne beslutninger funderet i kundernes adfærd. Netop dét har vi brug for dig til at hjælpe med. 

Du vil være ansvarlig for at omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Hvad fyldes din dag med?

Rollen er hverken en ren Power BI-rolle eller en rolle, hvor du sidder og bygger tunge ML-modeller. Det er en rolle for dig, der vil være tæt på forretningen og være med til at løfte vores data modenheden i Erhverv, så vores beslutninger bliver skarpere, hurtigere og mindre afhængige af enkeltpersoner. I din hverdag vil du omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata, og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på, hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Konkret betyder det at du:


Driver overgangen fra manuelle Excel-processer til robuste, skalerbare og datadrevne beslutningsgrundlag – f.eks. inden for forecasting, segmentindsigter og prissætning.
Samarbejder med vores Data Enablement-team om at udvikle dataløsninger og omsætte forretningens behov til konkrete data- og analyseleverancer.
Udvikler og videreudvikler Power BI-appen “Clever Erhverv” samt andre rapporterings- og analyseværktøjer, så de bidrager til bedre beslutninger for forretningen.
Sparrer og rådgiver om data for vores produkt-, salgs- og supportteams.
Aarbejder sammen med vores kvalitative indsigtsrolle for at skabe et stærkt beslutningsgrundlag på tværs af kvantitative og kvalitative indsigter.



Dit team

Du bliver en del af teamet Forretningsudvikling for Erhverv. Vi er et udviklingsteam på ni personer med kompetencer inden for økonomiske og kvalitative indsigter, produktudvikling samt salgs- og erhvervsstrategi. Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med den kvalitative indsigtsrolle, produktudviklerne og resten af erhvervsforretningen.

Du vil have berøringsflader med mange forskellige interessenter på tværs af erhvervsforretningen og navigere i et udviklende og tempofyldt område, hvor dine kompetencer vil være efterspurgte. Samtidig bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt fællesskab med Clevers øvrige specialister inden for kvalitative indsigter, hvor vi sparrer og vidensdeler.

Hvem er du?

Når vi forestiller os vores kommende kollega, ser vi dig, der har:


Fokus på beslutninger frem for rapporter og motiveres af at hjælpe forretningen med at forudse udviklingen og handle på den – ikke blot at visualisere historiske data.
Tekniske dybde for at indgå i en kvalificeret dialog med vores Data Enablement-team.
Kendskab til statistiske og datavidenskabelige metoder, kan arbejde i SQL og Databricks og kan vurdere kvaliteten af dataløsninger, selvom du ikke selv bygger dem.
Erfaring med Power BI og Excel (DAX er en fordel), og ser værktøjerne som middel til at skabe bedre beslutninger.
Stærke formidlingsevner til at oversætte mellem data og forretning og skabe struktur og klarhed for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
Lyst til at drive udvikling og være med til at løfte, hvordan vi arbejder datadrevet i Erhverv.



Vi er ekstra begejstrede, hvis du lytter aktivt, arbejder analytisk og kan skabe struktur og klarhed i komplekse dialoger.

Medledelseskompetencer


Du holder dit ego i skak og ved, at succes handler om at løfte i flok mod fælles mål  
Du tager naturligt ansvar for både dig selv og dine kollegaer, med omsorg og nærvær  
Du er en holdspiller, der ser arbejde som et samarbejde, ikke en konkurrence  



Har du sat mentale flueben hele vejen igennem?

Send gerne din ansøgning afsted allerede i dag – for vi kan godt finde på at lukke opslaget ned før tid, hvis vi møder det rigtige match. Vi håber, at vi gennem din ansøgning kan lære dig at kende og se, hvad du kan bidrage med. Hvis du bruger AI-værktøjer til inspiration, er det fint, men husk at personliggøre den for at skille dig ud.

Vi holder en lang velfortjent sommerferie væk fra computeren og er tilbage i uge 33, hvor du kan forvente at høre fra os. Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål i mellem tiden, så kontakt os på rekruttering@clever.dk .

Du kan læse mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på: www.clever.dk/velkommen eller vores Instagramkonto @liveticlever.

Er du nysgerrig på vores rekrutteringsproces kan du læse mere om den her: Rekrutteringsprocessen i Clever | Clever .

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to join our Software and ML Engineering team at Gubra. In this role, you will build and scale machine learning infrastructure and MLOps capabilities for scientific automation.




Your Role

You will collaborate closely with software engineers, other ML engineers, data scientists, and scientific domain experts within peptide discovery to accelerate our drug discovery initiatives.




Your primary responsibilities will include:

Collaborate with domain experts to develop ML models for generative peptide design and peptide property prediction.
Develop and maintain reusable ML pipelines, tools, and infrastructure that accelerate model development and deployment within peptide discovery. This includes automating model training, CI/CD, and serving/monitoring of models in production.
Establish flexible pipelines, leveraging cloud or hybrid infrastructure to support large-scale model training and high-throughput inference.
Partner with data scientists and scientific stakeholders to identify high-impact opportunities for ML and ensure our solutions deliver value to both research teams and operational workflows.
Promote high standards in ML engineering including version control, testing, code reviews, and model governance. Help establish common MLOps best practices like reproducible research, model and data versioning, and effective monitoring. Mentor junior engineers and data scientists, fostering an environment of knowledge sharing and continuous improvement.







What success looks like

As an ML Engineer, you are a key enabler of Gubra’s digital strategy. Success in this role means:

Drug Discovery teams can rapidly prototype, train, and deploy new models with minimal friction, creating faster discovery cycles and reducing the time from idea to insight.
Our scientific groups rely on robust, automated ML pipelines that consistently deliver reproducible results and adapt to new experimental data.
The team and organization have adopted modern MLOps practices, benefiting from the compounding impact of shared ML infrastructure, and are recognized for integrating advanced AI into scientific research effectively.







Your Profile

We welcome candidates with diverse backgrounds and experiences. We promise that you will learn from us, and we are looking forward to learning from you.




Your qualifications should include:

A M.Sc. or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, with 5+ years of hands-on experience in machine learning and software engineering, including building or operating production ML systems.
Proficiency in Python and experience with modern ML frameworks. Familiarity with CI/CD (e.g., GitHub Actions) for ML pipelines and with cloud or hybrid infrastructure for model deployment.
Strong foundation in machine learning theory and hands-on experience with state-of-the-art models, including deep understanding of model architectures and advanced fine-tuning techniques.
Excellent communication and teamwork skills. Ability to work closely with scientific domain experts and mentor colleagues in ML engineering and coding best practices.
Exposure to life sciences or biotechnology is a strong plus (e.g., understanding of peptides, biochemistry, drug discovery processes), but not required. We value curiosity and the ability to quickly learn about new scientific domains as needed.







About the Software and ML Engineering team

Our team focuses on automating and scaling scientific processes and research studies at Gubra. You will join skilled ML engineers and software developers at Gubra HQ in Hørsholm, on the edge of Greater Copenhagen.




You will be part of shaping the foundation of ML-driven drug discovery and scientific innovation at Gubra. You’ll play a crucial role in bridging cutting-edge ML with our mission to improve health and scale our impact on science and society. If you’re passionate about building robust ML systems and applying them to real-world scientific challenges, we would love to hear from you.







Contact and application

Please apply no later than August 9, 2026 by uploading your cover letter, resume (in English), and relevant diplomas on our website. Interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis.




If you have any questions about the position, you are very welcome to contact Manager, Anders Nielsen at ani@gubra.dk.




We are looking forward to receiving your application.







About Gubra

Gubra is a disease-agnostic techbio company specialized in peptide-based drug discovery and development as well as preclinical contract research services striving for excellence at all levels. We insist on doing things efficiently – and often differently - to reach the results we aim for. Our vision is to become leaders in the path towards a more sustainable and healthier world. We do that by facilitating the discovery of new medicine, and by acting and inspiring others to fight the ongoing climate and biodiversity crises.




Gubra’s activities are focused on the early stages of drug development and are organised in two main highly synergistic business areas: Biotech and CRO Services. We generate our revenue by performing research for life science companies as well as by partnering projects from our discovery and development pipeline.




Our therapeutic focus is within metabolic and fibrotic diseases, and we specialize in in vivo pharmacology, ex vivo assays, drug profiling, histology, stereology and whole brain and organ imaging. In addition, we offer a full palette of advanced transcriptomics. Our ML/AI-driven peptide drug discovery platform streaMLine enables us to rapidly develop a peptide hit into a non-clinical candidate ready for development. Through a constant focus on high quality, scientific excellence, speed, and solid teamwork we have established ourselves as a highly professional and competent partner in the market.




People are our greatest asset, and our team consists of around 300 employees of which many are located in Hørsholm, Denmark. The mix of people from different cultures and educational backgrounds combined with our entrepreneurial mindset have greatly impacted our working environment, which is characterized by entrepreneurial drive, scientific curiosity, and teamwork – we join forces!

Title: Product Manager, Data Insights
Description:
About Us

nShift is the leading global provider of cloud delivery management solutions (SaaS), we enable the frictionless shipment and return of almost one billion shipments across 190 countries each year. We are headquartered in London and Oslo and have over 500 employees across offices in Sweden, Finland, Norway, Denmark, the United Kingdom, Poland, the Netherlands, Belgium, and Romania.

Our software is used by many of the world leading e-commerce, retail, manufacturing, and 3PL shippers due to us having over 1000 carriers integrated into our platform, nearly 3 times more than our competitors!

If you buy goods online, there is a strong chance that nShift has powered that delivery, so come and join us as we shape the future of shipping, one frictionless journey at a time.

About You

You think like a product manager and work like a data scientist. You have shipped ML-powered products to real customers and can point to the decisions you made, not just the ones made around you. Messy data, ambiguous model outputs, and stakeholders who don't yet see why the data matters are familiar territory.

You think in terms of compounding: how every data point collected today improves predictions tomorrow, and how that improvement builds a position competitors cannot close. Data insights is nShift's path from a tool company to an intelligence platform, and that scope motivates you rather than overwhelming you.

You are as comfortable in a Jupyter notebook as in a leadership meeting, and you know when each is needed.

Purpose of role

The mission is to define what nShift builds with that data, validate it before engineering commits, and ship intelligence products that help shippers make better decisions. A shipper asking "how can I improve my delivery experience" should get an answer powered by the full network, not just their own history. Done well, that becomes a compounding intelligence layer: every data point collected today improves suggestions tomorrow, and the improvement creates a position no competitor can close.

The intelligence layer draws from two sources. Structured operational data, such as shipment telemetry and carrier performance, feeds statistical models and predictions. Qualitative data, such as customer support interactions, delivery exception messages, and community feedback, carries signal that numbers alone cannot surface. LLMs make that signal extractable and usable at scale. This role owns both.

The role sits at the intersection of product management and data science. You have the technical depth to build ML proof-of-concepts independently and the commercial judgment to translate those capabilities into products customers pay for. You write the strategy and the first notebook.

What You'll Be Doing

Define and own the data driven product strategy


Own the roadmap for nShift's data intelligence layer: carrier performance prediction, cross-customer benchmarking, ML-driven carrier recommendations, and related capabilities.
Translate nShift's data asset into a sequenced product strategy that delivers near-term value while building toward the compounding intelligence layer vision.
Define the information architecture for the data insights layer: what data is collected, how it is normalized (in partnership with adjacent teams), and how it is exposed to upstream products.
Make hard prioritization calls: what to build first to validate the ML hypothesis, what to defer, and what to kill when the data doesn't support the bet.
Own the qualitative intelligence pipeline alongside the quantitative one: define which unstructured sources carry decision-relevant signal (support tickets, delivery exception messages, customer forum, carrier communications), how LLM-extracted insights are structured for downstream use, and how qualitative and quantitative signals combine into a coherent intelligence product.
Own the positioning and differentiation narrative: why this dataset is uniquely nShift's, why no competitor can replicate it, and how to articulate that defensibility to enterprise buyers.



Build and validate before engineering commits


Run ML proof-of-concepts independently: data exploration, feature engineering, model selection, and initial validation, using Python, notebooks, and the data available in nShift's data layer.
Define the evaluation framework for both ML models and LLM pipelines. For ML features: what accuracy thresholds matter, how to measure model performance in production, how to distinguish signal from noise. For LLM-based features: how to assess output consistency, factual grounding, and quality at scale, where traditional accuracy metrics don't apply.
Design lightweight experiments that answer the key questions before a full engineering team is committed. Does the prediction accuracy justify the product? Does cross-customer data improve the model significantly? Is the data we have sufficient, or do we need to instrument more?
Translate POC findings into clear build, kill, or iterate decisions backed by data.



Partner across data, engineering, and commercial teams


Work closely with engineers to define the data models, pipelines, and normalization standards needed to power ML features. You are a customer and co-designer of the data layer, not a passenger.
Drive integration of data insights capabilities into existing products. Work closly with your PM peers to make this happen.
Engage directly with customers and prospects across the full spectrum, from small shippers to global enterprises, to validate intelligence use cases: what decisions do they make today that better data would improve? What would they pay for? What do they trust?
Build the commercial narrative: ROI frameworks, value quantification, and positioning for the target buyer.



Govern the data responsibly


Define the anonymization, aggregation, and consent frameworks that make cross-customer benchmarking legally and ethically sound, and credible to enterprise customers who will ask hard questions.
Work with Legal and the DPO to ensure data usage within the intelligence layer meets GDPR and contractual obligations. The data asset is only valuable if customers trust nShift to use it appropriately.
Establish transparency standards for ML-powered recommendations: when does a merchant see why a carrier was recommended? What level of explainability builds trust without revealing proprietary network data?



What You'll Bring

Must-have


Hands-on data proficiency across both paradigms: you can build and validate ML proof-of-concepts (data exploration, feature engineering, model training, evaluation) using Python and standard ML tooling (scikit-learn, pandas, XGBoost, or equivalent), and you can design and evaluate LLM-based analysis pipelines (prompt design, retrieval-augmented approaches, output quality evaluation). You are not a data scientist or an ML engineer, but you can work like one when the problem requires it.
Shipped ML-powered products: you have personally driven an ML or data intelligence product from initial hypothesis to production, including defining evaluation criteria, partnering with data engineers and scientists, and making commercial decisions based on model performance data.
PM fundamentals with commercial depth: 3+ years shipping B2B SaaS products. You can build a business case, define pricing, run customer discovery, and hold direct conversations with enterprise buyers about ROI.
Data literacy that earns engineering respect: you understand data models, pipeline design, normalization challenges, and the practical constraints of real production data. You can review a data schema and immediately ask the right questions.
Clear communication across the technical and commercial boundary: you can explain a precision-recall tradeoff to a CPO and a carrier event data use case to a data engineer in the same afternoon, without condescending to either.
Excellent written and verbal English: you will write strategy documents, ML experiment briefs, customer-facing product narratives, and present to leadership, often in the same week.



Nice to have


Experience with time-series or logistics domain data: carrier performance modelling, routing optimization, ETA prediction, or related domains.
Familiarity with data platform concepts: data lake houses, feature stores, model registries, observability for ML in production.
Background in cross-customer benchmarking or network intelligence products, where the value comes from aggregation rather than individual customer data.
Experience with GDPR-compliant data product design in a multi-tenant B2B SaaS context.
Nordic or European logistics market experience: carrier landscape, regulatory environment, freight economics.
Track record of building the first data product at a company, defining standards rather than inheriting them.

We're an equal-opportunity employer which means that all applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity, or disability status.

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Place: Aarhus Municipality, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Vil du udvikle og lede leverancen af avancerede AI- og machine learning-løsninger, der bliver sat i produktion og skaber reel værdi hos kunden?




Vi søger en hands-on Senior Data Scientist med erfaring fra konsulentbranchen. Du har stærk teknisk dybde inden for machine learning, generativ AI eller begge områder og kan tage ansvar for komplekse tekniske leverancer hos kunden.




Du samarbejder direkte med kunden, afklarer tekniske behov og skaber fremdrift i leverancen. Det afgørende er, at du både kan bygge løsningerne selv, sætte teknisk retning og løfte kvaliteten omkring dig.




Du bliver en del af et større fagligt miljø i vores AI, Apps & Automation-capability. Her arbejder du tæt sammen med data scientists samt software- og automationsspecialister og samarbejder på tværs af capabilities med data engineers, arkitekter, forretningskonsulenter m.fl.




Om rollen

Som Senior Data Scientist leder du det tekniske arbejde og er samtidig selv en aktiv del af udførelsen. Du arbejder hands-on med kode, modeller, eksperimenter, løsningsdesign og produktionsmodning.

Du tager ansvar for de svære tekniske valg og hjælper teamet med at fastholde kvalitet og fremdrift.




Du kommer blandt andet til at:

Lede tekniske AI- og machine learning-projekter fra problemforståelse til produktionsklar løsning
Omsætte uklare forretningsbehov til konkrete tekniske problemstillinger og løsningsdesign
Sætte teknisk retning og træffe valg om modeller, metoder, arkitektur og platforme
Drive tekniske afklaringer med relevante stakeholders hos kunden
Sikre sammenhæng mellem teknisk kvalitet, anvendelighed, governance og forretningsværdi
Bidrage til scoping, estimering og kvalificering af nye kundeopgaver
Mentorere kolleger og udvikle genanvendelige metoder, komponenter og accelerators




Afhængigt af din specialisering kan du eksempelvis arbejde med forecasting, optimering, recommendation engines og MLOps eller med RAG, dokumentintelligens, copilots, agentic AI og intelligente workflows.




Sådan skaber du succes i rollen

Du omsætter komplekse AI- og machine learning-problemstillinger til løsninger med tydelig forretningsværdi
Du skaber struktur og fremdrift, også når rammerne eller løsningen ikke er givet på forhånd
Du bygger løsninger, der kan implementeres, anvendes, monitoreres og videreudvikles
Du skaber tillid gennem faglig tyngde, tydelig kommunikation og kvalificerede tekniske anbefalinger
Du løfter kvaliteten omkring dig gennem sparring, mentoring og genanvendelige metoder




Din profil

Konsulenterfaring er et krav.

Du har erfaring fra et konsulenthus, hvor du har arbejdet direkte med kunder og taget ansvar for komplekse data science-, machine learning- eller AI-leverancer.




Vi lægger vægt på, at du:

Har haft en ledende teknisk rolle eller taget tydeligt ansvar i komplekse kundeprojekter
Har udviklet og implementeret produktionsorienterede løsninger inden for machine learning og/eller generativ AI
Har stærke kompetencer i Python, kan arbejde sikkert med SQL og har forståelse for moderne software engineering
Har erfaring med Azure, Databricks, Snowflake, Microsoft Fabric eller tilsvarende cloudplatforme
Forstår deployment, test, CI/CD, monitorering, governance og driftsoverdragelse
Kan omsætte forretningsbehov til tekniske løsninger og kommunikere valg, risici og trade-offs til kunden
Har erfaring med at sparre med kolleger og gerne bidraget til scoping, estimering eller genanvendelige løsninger




Du behøver ikke være ekspert i både machine learning og generativ AI. Det afgørende er, at du har dokumenteret dybde inden for mindst ét område og kan samarbejde troværdigt med andre specialister om den samlede løsning.




Det tilbyder vi

Hos Inspari bliver du en del af et ambitiøst konsulenthus med stærk specialisering inden for data og AI og komplekse opgaver hos enterprise-kunder.

Du får blandt andet:

Ansvar for komplekse AI- og machine learning-leverancer med synlig kundeværdi
Mulighed for at sætte teknisk retning og påvirke, hvordan vi udvikler vores AI-løsninger
Et stærkt fagligt miljø på tværs af data science, software, automation og Data Platform
Gode muligheder for faglig udvikling og videre specialisering
Fleksible arbejdsforhold og mulighed for hybridarbejde
En kultur præget af høj faglighed, ansvar, samarbejde og Heartwork




Lokation

Du vil være tilknyttet vores kontor i Lystrup ved Aarhus eller Kgs. Lyngby. Arbejdet foregår i en hybrid model mellem kontoret, kunderne og hjemmearbejdspladsen.




Rejseaktivitet

Som konsulent må du forvente en vis rejseaktivitet. Omfanget afhænger af projekterne og planlægges løbende i samarbejde med dig.




Ansøg

Hvis rollen matcher din erfaring, faglige profil og det ansvar, du ønsker fremover, glæder vi os til at modtage dit CV og din ansøgning.

Vi afholder samtaler løbende og ansætter, når vi har fundet det rette match. Forventet opstart er den 1. oktober 2026.




Har du spørgsmål til rollen eller processen, kan du kontakte Talent Acquisition Director Steffen Krogsgaard på krog@inspari.dk.

Bemærk, at Steffen holder ferie i uge 29-32. I denne periode kan der derfor være længere svartid på henvendelser. Samtaler kan efter behov varetages af andre fra teamet.

Title: AI Engineer
Place: Hedehusene, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Req Number: 120122

Time Type: Full Time

At DSV, we keep global supply chains moving – and increasingly, this includes how we use AI to support critical operations, decisions, and customer experiences across the world.

We are looking for an AI Engineer who is motivated to turn emerging AI capabilities into reliable, high-impact solutions. You are curious, solution-oriented, and driven to push boundaries, drive continuous improvement, and build production-grade language systems powered by large language models.

Your Responsibilities And Impact

You join our AI Language Systems team that develops high-impact AI products and capabilities used across the organization. We work in agile squads aligned with different products and business domains, collaborating closely with product, research, and operational teams.

We support critical business operations by experimenting with, evaluating, and applying advanced AI, LLM, and NLP methods to solve real problems and move the business forward. Our work goes beyond research prototypes and notebook-based experimentation. We design, build, deploy, and operate reliable, production-grade AI solutions that perform at scale and deliver measurable business value.

As an AI Engineer, you help shape how advanced AI and large language models are applied in real business contexts. The role combines applied AI research and software engineering. You will independently deliver AI and GenAI features of moderate complexity, owning scoped work from requirements clarification through experimentation, implementation, deployment, and initial production support.

You will work on RAG pipelines, agentic search, prompt optimization, model evaluation, fine-tuning workflows, and emerging integration patterns such as Model Context Protocol and agent-to-agent communication.

Key Responsibilities



Independently deliver scoped AI, GenAI, NLP, and RAG features of moderate complexity
Develop and enhance RAG pipelines, including document parsing and ingestion, chunking and metadata strategies, query transformation, retrieval and ranking, response generation and grounding
Build GenAI features using LLM APIs, structured prompting, and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LangGraph, DSPy, etc.)
Evaluate AI system performance using practical methods such as retrieval metrics, response quality assessment, hallucination analysis, latency measurement, cost analysis, and failure-case testing
Understand engineering trade-offs across model quality, latency, cost, reliability, maintainability, and implementation complexity
Own features end-to-end – from clarification and experimentation to deployment and initial support
Translate requirements into user stories and provide implementation plans, as well as own features end-to-end throughout the software development lifecycle - from clarification and experimentation to deployment and initial support
Identify risks, dependencies, and data limitations early and propose workable solutions
Challenge unclear requirements and contribute with pragmatic, value-driven alternatives
Stay close to new developments in LLMs, RAG, prompt optimization, model evaluation, fine-tuning, and applied AI frameworks, and assess where they create real business impact



What You Bring

Ideally, you have strong hands-on AI engineering experience, understand the fundamentals of modern AI language systems, and can translate product and technical requirements into reliable production implementations with limited guidance.

To succeed and thrive in the role, you bring:



A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, AI, Machine Learning, or similar- or equivalent professional experience
3+ years of professional AI engineering/applied data science experience, including hands-on experience with AI, NLP, machine learning, deep learning, or language-model-based applications
Strong Python skills and experience building clean, maintainable, and production-ready software
Hands-on experience with GenAI or LLM-based solutions or open-source models
Solid understanding of software engineering practices (testing, CI/CD, version control, etc.)
Experience with model evaluation, monitoring, or experiment tracking tools (i.e., MLflow or similar)
Ability to work in cross-functional, agile teams and communicate clearly in English



Nice to have:



Experience with cloud platforms such as Google Cloud or similar
Familiarity with RAG architectures, embeddings, vector databases, and retrieval techniques
Exposure to fine-tuning or model optimization approaches
Experience with Kedro for building modular, reproducible data and ML pipelines, and KServe for scalable, production-grade model deployment and inference on Kubernetes
Knowledge of agentic workflows, tool-calling systems, agentic search, MCP, or A2A integration patterns



What This Role Offers



Ownership of real AI solutions used at scale in a global organisation
The opportunity to turn cutting-edge technology into tangible business impact
Collaboration with international teams across products, engineering, and operations
A dynamic environment focused on learning, improvement, and shared success
Opportunities to grow your skills and shape how AI is applied across DSV



Why DSV
