Job Req Number: 120122

Time Type: Full Time

At DSV, we keep global supply chains moving – and increasingly, this includes how we use AI to support critical operations, decisions, and customer experiences across the world.

We are looking for an AI Engineer who is motivated to turn emerging AI capabilities into reliable, high-impact solutions. You are curious, solution-oriented, and driven to push boundaries, drive continuous improvement, and build production-grade language systems powered by large language models.

Your Responsibilities And Impact

You join our AI Language Systems team that develops high-impact AI products and capabilities used across the organization. We work in agile squads aligned with different products and business domains, collaborating closely with product, research, and operational teams.

We support critical business operations by experimenting with, evaluating, and applying advanced AI, LLM, and NLP methods to solve real problems and move the business forward. Our work goes beyond research prototypes and notebook-based experimentation. We design, build, deploy, and operate reliable, production-grade AI solutions that perform at scale and deliver measurable business value.

As an AI Engineer, you help shape how advanced AI and large language models are applied in real business contexts. The role combines applied AI research and software engineering. You will independently deliver AI and GenAI features of moderate complexity, owning scoped work from requirements clarification through experimentation, implementation, deployment, and initial production support.

You will work on RAG pipelines, agentic search, prompt optimization, model evaluation, fine-tuning workflows, and emerging integration patterns such as Model Context Protocol and agent-to-agent communication.

Key Responsibilities


  • Independently deliver scoped AI, GenAI, NLP, and RAG features of moderate complexity
  • Develop and enhance RAG pipelines, including document parsing and ingestion, chunking and metadata strategies, query transformation, retrieval and ranking, response generation and grounding
  • Build GenAI features using LLM APIs, structured prompting, and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LangGraph, DSPy, etc.)
  • Evaluate AI system performance using practical methods such as retrieval metrics, response quality assessment, hallucination analysis, latency measurement, cost analysis, and failure-case testing
  • Understand engineering trade-offs across model quality, latency, cost, reliability, maintainability, and implementation complexity
  • Own features end-to-end – from clarification and experimentation to deployment and initial support
  • Translate requirements into user stories and provide implementation plans, as well as own features end-to-end throughout the software development lifecycle - from clarification and experimentation to deployment and initial support
  • Identify risks, dependencies, and data limitations early and propose workable solutions
  • Challenge unclear requirements and contribute with pragmatic, value-driven alternatives
  • Stay close to new developments in LLMs, RAG, prompt optimization, model evaluation, fine-tuning, and applied AI frameworks, and assess where they create real business impact


What You Bring

Ideally, you have strong hands-on AI engineering experience, understand the fundamentals of modern AI language systems, and can translate product and technical requirements into reliable production implementations with limited guidance.

To succeed and thrive in the role, you bring:


  • A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, AI, Machine Learning, or similar- or equivalent professional experience
  • 3+ years of professional AI engineering/applied data science experience, including hands-on experience with AI, NLP, machine learning, deep learning, or language-model-based applications
  • Strong Python skills and experience building clean, maintainable, and production-ready software
  • Hands-on experience with GenAI or LLM-based solutions or open-source models
  • Solid understanding of software engineering practices (testing, CI/CD, version control, etc.)
  • Experience with model evaluation, monitoring, or experiment tracking tools (i.e., MLflow or similar)
  • Ability to work in cross-functional, agile teams and communicate clearly in English


Nice to have:


  • Experience with cloud platforms such as Google Cloud or similar
  • Familiarity with RAG architectures, embeddings, vector databases, and retrieval techniques
  • Exposure to fine-tuning or model optimization approaches
  • Experience with Kedro for building modular, reproducible data and ML pipelines, and KServe for scalable, production-grade model deployment and inference on Kubernetes
  • Knowledge of agentic workflows, tool-calling systems, agentic search, MCP, or A2A integration patterns


What This Role Offers


  • Ownership of real AI solutions used at scale in a global organisation
  • The opportunity to turn cutting-edge technology into tangible business impact
  • Collaboration with international teams across products, engineering, and operations
  • A dynamic environment focused on learning, improvement, and shared success
  • Opportunities to grow your skills and shape how AI is applied across DSV


Why DSV

You will join a global organisation with more than 150,000 colleagues across 90+ countries, working together to keep supply chains moving worldwide.

At DSV, movement creates opportunity – and your work helps transform how technology supports that movement every day. We foster a culture of curiosity, ownership, continuous improvement, and shared success, with a strong focus on the growth of both our products and our people.

Title: Lead Analytics Platform Developer
Place: Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us in this role where you’ll be the Business Application Responsible for the Analytics Workspace, a JupyterHub-based platform built on cloud Kubernetes and used across Trading & Revenue. You’ll co-develop the platform with Commercial Digital Technology, guide and test deliveries, and make sure the platform evolves in line with user needs across analytics, algo development, and model development.

Welcome to Analytics

You’ll be part of Data Science & Platforms, one of three teams in Trading & Revenue’s Analytics function, where you, together with your colleagues, will deliver the platforms and products behind trading and revenue decisions. You’ll work closely with quantitative developers, analysts, model developers, and Commercial Digital Technology to shape a reliable, scalable platform that supports both current users and new analytics capabilities. As a team, we value flexibility, experimentation, knowledge sharing, and collaboration. We care about building an inclusive environment where different perspectives help us create better solutions.

You’ll play an important role in:


owning the Analytics Workspace on the Trading & Revenue side, ensuring it remains reliable, relevant, and well aligned with business needs
co-developing platform capabilities with Commercial Digital Technology through coding, prototyping, reviewing pull requests, and defining acceptance tests
enabling strong platform foundations for analytics, algo development, and machine learning use cases, including compute, environments, data access, deployment, logging, and monitoring
supporting users through onboarding, day-to-day platform support, and close collaboration with stakeholders across Trading & Revenue
maintaining best practices, tools, templates, and ways of working for analytics engineering and the wider analytics community
driving the platform roadmap, including improvements, migrations, and adjacent capabilities such as machine learning, streaming and AI tooling.


To succeed in the role, you:


have strong Python development skills and apply sound engineering practices across testing, packaging, and CI/CD
bring hands-on experience with JupyterHub-based platforms, Kubernetes, Airflow and cloud infrastructure in Azure, AWS, or similar environments
work confidently across DevOps or MLOps topics and can contribute to platform design, delivery, and operational excellence
collaborate well with internal users and external engineering partners, and communicate clearly with both technical and business stakeholders
take ownership, build trust, and stay close to user needs while helping specialist teams contribute and co-own solutions
are motivated to grow your understanding of Trading & Revenue analytics, and you bring curiosity, structure, and a practical mindset to complex platform challenges.


Maybe you’ve read the above and can see you have some transferable skills, even though they don’t quite match all the points. If you think you can bring something to the team, we still encourage you to apply.

Shape the future with us

Send your application to us as soon as possible. We’ll be conducting interviews on a continuous basis and reserve the right to take down the advert when we’ve found the right candidate.

As an applicant or employee, you may request reasonable work and position accommodation or adjustments via accommodation@orsted.com.

Please note that for your application to be taken into consideration, you must submit your application via our online career pages and answer the screening questions relevant for your country. We don't take applications or inquiries from external recruiters or agencies into account for this position.

Position salary range:

For this position, the expected base pay range is kr58,000.00- kr87,000.00 for the advertised location. The offered base salary will be determined within this range based on relevant skills, experience, and expected role fulfilment within the stated pay range to ensure a fair and competitive offer. Where applicable, you may also be eligible for variable payment and a comprehensive benefits package.

Our benefits package:

For this position, employees who meet eligibility requirements have access to: employer‑paid pension (11% contribution), full insurance coverage (accident, life, critical illness, and health insurance); five weeks of paid holiday plus 10 additional paid days off per holiday year; a 3% holiday allowance; access to Ørsted Life & Fitness Vitality including fitness classes, physiotherapy, massage, and wellbeing programmes; a multimedia package (mobile phone, PC, internet); staff club membership with sport and cultural activities; and access to the company canteen (monthly lunch scheme or daily tickets).

Pre-employment background check:

Certain positions may require a background check as part of the hiring process. If applicable, you will be informed during the recruitment process. Background checks are conducted by an external provider in accordance with local legal requirements.

Title: DATA SCIENCE CONSULTANT COPENHAGEN
Description:
We are currently recruiting for our Summer 2026 intake, with an expected start date in September 2026.




Management Solutions is an international consulting firm whose core mission is to deliver business, risk, financial, organisational and process-related advisory services, targeting both functional aspects and the implementation of related technologies. We currently have a multidisciplinary team (functional, mathematical, technical and systems integration) of 4,000 professionals.

We operate through 52 offices (24 in Europe, 23 in the Americas, 3 in Asia, 1 in Africa and 1 in Oceania) from where we regularly serve clients that operate in more than 50 countries across five major geographical areas (Europe, Americas, Asia, Africa and Oceania).




Role:




Statistical data processing (Data Mining)
Artificial Intelligence (AI), including generative AI
Predictive modeling using Machine Learning and Data Science techniques
Time-series modeling (ARIMA and panel data models) and simulation models (Monte Carlo)
Development, validation and audit of financial, risk, marketing and business-oriented models
R&D projects




Requirements:




Recent graduates or final year students.
Should desirably have knowledge of Artificial Intelligence modeling techniques (logit, GLM, time series, random forests, clustering, XGBoost), statistical programming languages (SAS, R, Python, Matlab), big data tools and platforms (Hadoop, Hive, etc.) and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Solid academic record.
Strong computer skills.
Postgraduate studies and/or specialized courses are an asset, especially in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Quantitative Finance or similar.
Knowledge of other languages is desirable.
Get-up-and-go attitude, maturity, responsibility and strong work ethic.
Strong ability to learn quickly.
Able to integrate easily into multidisciplinary teams.




What we offer:




We offer you the possibility to join a firm that provides all you need to develop your talent to the fullest:




Working in the highest-profile consulting projects in the industry,
for the largest companies, leaders of their respective markets,
alongside top industry management as they face challenges at the national and global level,
as part of an extraordinary team of professionals whose values and corporate culture are a benchmark for the industry




Training:

Ongoing training plan, with approximately 10% of business turnover spent on training.
Specialist knowledge courses, external expert courses, professional skills courses and language courses.
Last year our staff as a whole received over 400,000 hours of training spanning more than 160 courses.




Career plan:

Clearly defined career plan.
Internal promotion based solely on merit.
Partnership-based management model offers all professionals the opportunity to become part of the Firm’s group of partners




Complementary experiences:




University: we maintain a close relationship with the world’s most prestigious universities.
Social Action: we organize more than 30 community support activities.
Sports Club: internal and external tournaments.




How to apply:




If you are interested in our offer, please complete our application form online at https://www.managementsolutions.com/en/professional-careers-send-us-your-cv

Title: Junior Analytics Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you have a passion for Sports & Outdoor?

Do you thrive at the intersection of data, automation, and AI?

Do you love finding ways to make complex processes simpler through data?

Apply now and become part of our great team at DANISH ENDURANCE®!

We are looking for a talented and ambitious Junior Analytics Engineer to help drive our automation and AI journey - and to contribute to the continuous development and growth of our brand of endurance and outdoor gear.

Who is DANISH ENDURANCE®?

DANISH ENDURANCE® is a global endurance & outdoor brand within e-commerce. With a focus on Danish design and sustainable manufacturing in Europe and Asia, we are a young high-growth company, with a flat structure and a friendly, dynamic working environment. We are a truly international team consisting of 70 professionals with 25 different nationalities based in Copenhagen at Holmen and Gramrode near Horsens.

We have a scalable and unique business model, our ambitions are rocket-high, and we strongly believe that everything is possible. That is why we need you!

What can you expect from the job?

As Our Junior Analytics Engineer, You Will


Build and maintain ELT pipelines, and model the ingested data to business needs in dbt
Drive process optimization and automation initiatives
Help take automations and agentic workflows into production - and grow into the architecture and hosting behind them
Build data products people use - dashboards, automated reports, and ad-hoc analyses
Ensure data quality and maintain documentation
Participate in ML/AI projects and help to execute on our AI roadmap


What do we expect from you?


Curious - you want to understand how things work end to end, not just make them run
Reliable and solution-oriented - you take ownership and see things through
Eager to learn and quick to adapt in a fast-moving environment
Design, implement, and maintain automation pipelines that reduce manual work across the business
Develop (agentic) solutions using modern AI tools and frameworks
Transform complex business requirements into efficient, scalable technical solutions
Proactively identify opportunities for process improvement and see them through to delivery
Collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams


Qualifications

You don’t need to tick every box — but solid hands-on experience with several of the following is what we’re after:


Degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field
Working knowledge of SQL
Familiarity with data modelling and a cloud data warehouse (we use BigQuery)
Experience with agentic development/AI automations
Experience with dbt and version control (Git)
Experience with data analysis and visualization tools
Experience with Python
Business process optimization experience
Bonus: exposure to deploying solutions in production - hosting, architecture, CI/CD
Passion for sports is a bonus


Additional Facts

Weekly working hours: Full time

Location: Copenhagen Holmen, at Talent Garden Rainmaking

Deadline: We will review applications on an ongoing basis, the sooner you apply, the better

Start date: As soon as possible

How To Apply

Please apply via the Hub. Please include both your CV and motivational letter in one document, as you can only uploade one document to your application.

Read more about DANISH ENDURANCE® at www.danishendurance.com

KEEP YOUR PERSONAL DATA SAFE

Danish Endurance takes responsibility for the protection of your personal data. Accordingly, upon receiving your job application and additional material you choose to submit, we keep it in a safe and secure environment and ensure confidentiality.

You also have a role to play in keeping your personal data safe. We request you only submit personal data about yourself in your job application, CV or other relevant material you choose to provide to us that is relevant for the specific position you are applying for.

(Please consider whether there is personal information you are about to submit, such as health information, that is not necessary for our recruitment process or the position you are applying for. Please also be aware of certificates or other documents that are not necessary for the vacancy.

Title: Data Engineer - On-site Copenhagen
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Data Engineer




AI is changing how software gets built.




The data underneath it matters more than ever.




At Heyra, we build AI systems, workflows and data platforms that are meant to live in production from day one. We work closely with companies that are moving quickly and need infrastructure that can keep up with the pace of change.




We’re looking for a Data Engineer who enjoys building things properly. Someone who can move through complex systems, shape clean architecture and create the foundations modern AI workflows depend on.




You’ll work closely with engineers, AI specialists, strategists and designers in a company where ideas move quickly and the distance from thinking to building is intentionally small.




What you'll work on

Building modern data platforms and pipelines
Creating semantic foundations for AI systems
Developing AI-powered workflows and agentic systems
Helping clients move AI into real operational environments
Working closely with both technical teams and decision makers
Shaping how modern AI infrastructure is built in practice




You probably have experience with

dbt, SnowFlake, Databricks, Fabric, BigQuery or similar ecosystems
SQL, Python and APIs
Data modelling and warehousing
GenAI, ML or MLOps workflows
Complex systems and problem solving

Curiosity, taste and builder mentality matter just as much to us as specific tools or years of experience.




Why Heyra

We believe consulting needs to be rewritten. We are building a consultancy designed for the agentic era. We design, build and operate AI products that change how enterprises work, from problem to prototype to production.

Heyra brings together people from engineering, strategy, product, startup and creative environments. We keep teams small, collaboration close and momentum high. We care deeply about craft, speed and building things that work in the real world.

People here care about what they build. Good ideas move quickly. New technology gets explored early. There is room to shape both the company and the way we work.

We are around 45 people today, and we are only just getting started.

If this sounds like your kind of work, send us a short note about yourself together with your CV. We care much more about curiosity, energy and the way you think than polished applications.




Benefits

Healthcare insurance
Pension plan
Parental leave
Social gatherings
New tech gear
Central office
Free lunch, coffee, snacks, and Friday beers
Skill development
Paid holiday
Remote work allowed
Pet friendly

We’re looking for a Data Engineer who enjoys building things properly. Someone who can move through complex systems, shape clean architecture and create the foundations modern AI workflows depend on.


You’ll work closely with engineers, AI specialists, strategists and designers in a company where ideas move quickly and the distance from thinking to building is intentionally small.


What you'll work on

  • Building modern data platforms and pipelines
  • Creating semantic foundations for AI systems
  • Developing AI-powered workflows and agentic systems
  • Helping clients move AI into real operational environments
  • Working closely with both technical teams and decision makers
  • Shaping how modern AI infrastructure is built in practice


You probably have experience with

  • dbt, SnowFlake, Databricks, Fabric, BigQuery or similar ecosystems
  • SQL, Python and APIs
  • Data modelling and warehousing
  • GenAI, ML or MLOps workflows
  • Complex systems and problem solving

Curiosity, taste and builder mentality matter just as much to us as specific tools or years of experience.


Why Heyra

We believe consulting needs to be rewritten. We are building a consultancy designed for the agentic era. We design, build and operate AI products that change how enterprises work, from problem to prototype to production.

Heyra brings together people from engineering, strategy, product, startup and creative environments. We keep teams small, collaboration close and momentum high. We care deeply about craft, speed and building things that work in the real world.

People here care about what they build. Good ideas move quickly. New technology gets explored early. There is room to shape both the company and the way we work.

We are around 45 people today, and we are only just getting started.

If this sounds like your kind of work, send us a short note about yourself together with your CV. We care much more about curiosity, energy and the way you think than polished applications.


Benefits

  • Healthcare insurance
  • Pension plan
  • Parental leave
  • Social gatherings
  • New tech gear
  • Central office
  • Free lunch, coffee, snacks, and Friday beers
  • Skill development
  • Paid holiday
  • Remote work allowed
  • Pet friendly

Title: AI Udvikler
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Bliv AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT og vær med til at udvikle fremtidens AI-løsninger til kommunerne!




Har du lyst til at udvikle ansvarlige, sikre og værdiskabende AI-løsninger – og arbejde med de nyeste værktøjer i et stærkt fagligt miljø? Så har vi netop den rette mulighed for dig.




Et stærkt it-fællesskab

I KOMBIT har de danske kommuner et fælles omdrejningspunkt med stærke og specialiserede kompetencer på digitaliseringsområdet. Vi er et kommunalt ejet it-fællesskab, der er ansvarlig for at indkøbe, forvalte, drifte og videreudvikle tidssvarende og stabile it-løsninger og -infrastruktur til gavn for borgere og virksomheder. Sammen med kommunerne og it-leverandørerne arbejder vi for, at borgere og virksomheder i Danmark får en bedre service og billigere og mere sammenhængende it-løsninger. KOMBITs ambition er at sikre fortsat kommunal digital bevægelse - i dag og i fremtiden.




Vi har et stærkt fokus på at levere ansvarlige og sikre AI-løsninger, der kan komme i produktion og skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.




Dine primære ansvarsområder

Du bliver tilknyttet specialet Design & Arkitektur og en del af KOMBITs fokuserede AI core team. Her arbejder vi sammen med bl.a. KL, kommunerne, leverandører samt interne og eksterne eksperter om at udvikle, modne og udbrede AI-løsninger i den kommunale sektor.

Vi søger udviklere, der vil være med til at bygge konkrete AI-løsninger fra idé til produktion. Det kan være, at du har en baggrund inden for data science, erfaring som full stack-udvikler eller stærke kompetencer inden for DevOps, CI/CD og moderne udviklingsmiljøer. Det vigtigste er, at du har lyst til at arbejde praktisk og nysgerrigt med AI-teknologi, softwareudvikling og løsninger, der kan skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.




Som AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT kommer du bl.a. til at arbejde med udvikling, test, integration, drift og videreudvikling af AI-løsninger. Vi anvender AI aktivt i alle faser af udviklingen – fra analyse, design og kodning til test, dokumentation, kvalitetssikring og drift. Du vil derfor også få mulighed for at arbejde med AI-agenter og andre nye værktøjer, der kan styrke både udviklingsprocessen og de løsninger, vi leverer.




Du kan eksempelvis blive involveret i arbejdet med vores AI Assistent, som hjælper kommunale sagsbehandlere med at anvende fagsystemer og søge information i lovgivningen, og som også anvendes internt i KOMBIT. Derudover kan du bidrage til udvikling af nye AI-produkter, fælles komponenter og platforme, screening af nye use cases, dataanalyse, evaluering, overvågning og sikker drift af AI-løsninger.




Vi er på udkig efter flere nye kollegaer til teamet, så uanset om du primært ser dig selv som data scientist, full stack-udvikler, DevOps-profil eller AI-nysgerrig softwareudvikler, hører vi meget gerne fra dig.




Fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv

Vi tilbyder dig et job, hvor omdrejningspunktet er samarbejde på tværs, og hvor du vil arbejde med nogle af Danmarks største it-løsninger. Samtidig bliver du en del af en ambitiøs virksomhed, der står i udviklingens tegn, både hvad angår de mange eksisterende løsninger samt forventningen om en fremtid med endnu bredere portefølje inden for digitalisering og it-dreven innovation i kommunerne.




I KOMBIT er det vigtigt, at alle medarbejdere finder en god balance mellem arbejde og privatliv. Vi skaber motiverende rammer, der giver medarbejderne frihed til at planlægge og løse deres opgaver ud fra individuelle behov. Af samme grund vil du hurtigt opleve, at der er højt til loftet og rig mulighed for at sætte dit præg i en hver fyldt med spændende opgaver. Vi har som organisation fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv i balance – vi kalder det LiFE!

Lyder det som noget for dig?




Så tag første skridt mod en spændende karriere i frontlinjen for digital udvikling, og send os dit CV senest d. 2. august 2026. Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at sende en mail til Chef for Design & Arkitektur, Lars Vraa (LVR@kombit.dk).




Vi screener relevante kandidater, hvor vi spørger ind til motivationen for at søge stillingen samt forventninger til vilkår for at sikre, at vi finder den bedst egnede kandidat til stillingen.




Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

Title: Product Analytics Engineer
Place: Aarhus Municipality, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
The role

We are looking for a Product Analytics Engineer to join our product team in Aarhus.

This is not a role where you only build pipelines. You own Candeno's data and analytics layer within your domain: the data warehouse, the BI and dashboards, and the instrumentation that turns product usage and AI interactions into reliable data. Part of the work is figuring out the right question before you build the answer. The rest is building it well.




We work AI-augmented. Modern AI tools are a natural part of how we work, so more of the routine building is handled for you. That makes the harder, more valuable work the judgement: what to measure, how to model it, and whether the numbers can be trusted. We use AI to move faster, and we believe people are what make sure we build the right things and trust the right data.




Your work will reach hundreds of thousands of people across the Nordics: job seekers navigating labour market reforms, employment professionals adapting to new digitalisation strategies, and the institutions managing that change. The dashboards and data you build inform decisions inside Candeno and in the hands of our customers, so keeping them trustworthy matters.




What you will work on

Candeno serves municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions across Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. The platform has three sides: candidates navigating the labour market, advisors guiding them through digitalisation and compliance workflows, and companies recruiting talent. One platform, many contexts. Our products increasingly run on data and AI, and this role builds the foundation underneath them.




Discovery (analysis and design)

•    Work with product people and customers to understand what a dashboard or a data model actually needs to answer, before you build it. AI helps us move fast, so the harder work is knowing what is worth measuring.

•    Model the data. Decide access patterns and weigh the tradeoffs between flexibility and control, with architecture direction from our Lead Architect.




Delivery (build and operate)

•    Build and maintain the data warehouse, data pipelines, and ETL or ELT flows between Candeno's platform and the warehouse.

•    Build and maintain internal and customer-facing dashboards, so the right data reaches the right audience in a form that drives decisions.

•    Instrument our AI chats, agents, and product usage so requests and usage signals become structured, first-class data, and work with engineers to keep it accessible for AI and ML use.

•    Write clean, maintainable code, using AI tools where they help and applying judgement to what they produce.




Quality and craft

•    Keep data trustworthy. Care about data quality and make it something the team can rely on.

•    Meet regulatory requirements in a data-sensitive sector, with guidance from our legal advisor.

•    Share what you know and help raise data competence across the team. This is knowledge-sharing, not people management.




Who we are looking for

You are a data and analytics engineer who is as comfortable with the question as with the query. Your foundation is solid delivery in data and BI, and your edge is knowing what is worth measuring before you build it.




You will need:

•    A few years building and maintaining data warehouses and BI or dashboards, ideally in SaaS or a product company. Full build experience matters more than years.

•    Solid data modelling skills, and comfort navigating the tradeoffs between flexibility and control.

•    Experience building data pipelines and ETL or ELT flows. Modern transformation tooling such as dbt is a plus.

•    An understanding of what AI and ML need from data, in structure and quality. You can grow into this.

•    A considered approach to AI in your workflow. You use modern tools well, and you check what they produce critically.

•    Clear communication. You can explain a data tradeoff to a product manager, an engineer, and a customer, and make it feel simple.

•    Comfort working with customers directly.




The kind of person who does well here:

•    Curious about the question, not only the query.

•    Ownership mindset. You follow a problem all the way through.

•    Comfortable making decisions without a perfect foundation, and honest about the tradeoffs when you do.

•    Direct. You share work early, give feedback clearly, and push back when something is not right.




What the first six months look like

After one month

You understand Candeno's platform, our BI setup and data flows, and the team. You have taken ownership of your first scoped tasks and you are active in how the team works.




After three months

You are building key parts of the warehouse and BI independently and contributing to product and AI instrumentation. You are starting to engage with customer analytics needs, with support.




After six months

You reliably own and deliver within the data and analytics domain. The dashboards and instrumentation you build are in real use, and you are a dependable go-to for product analytics questions, working within architecture direction from the Lead Architect.




Working at Candeno

We are a scale-up. That means real ownership, real impact, and a product that is still being shaped by the people who work on it.

We are based in Aarhus, and this is an on-site role. The team is small, cross-functional, and works closely together. You will have real ownership of your work and close proximity to product, engineering, and our customers.

We are direct and focused on doing the right thing for users. We share work early and expect you to do the same.




What we offer

•    Pension scheme and health insurance

•    5 extra days off after 2 years of seniority (10 in total)

•    Daily lunch from Meyers Madhus

•    A shared milestone programme that celebrates successes big and small

•    An active social life: team events, clubs, and an engaged social committee




The process

•    A short screening call

•    A first interview, a deeper conversation about your experience and the role

•    A case interview, a practical brief sent in advance

No cover letter. You will answer three short questions when you apply.




About Candeno

Candeno is a Nordic employment technology company based in Aarhus. We build the platform that connects candidates, advisors, and companies across municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions in Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. Our work sits at the intersection of public sector compliance and modern product thinking, and we take both seriously.

•    An active social life: team events, clubs, and an engaged social committee

About Candeno

Candeno is a Nordic employment technology company based in Aarhus. Title: AI Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
WHY SPEKTR

At spektr, we are on a mission to automate the world’s most complex compliance workflows through AI. Founded by the team behind HelloFlow (acquired by Trulioo), spektr is building the infrastructure and AI agents to run compliance on scale.

Most large financial companies rely on thousands of employees to keep up with manually handling compliance. With spektr’s AI agents, we have the opportunity to change that and completly redefine how compliance operates.

This is a rare opportunity to join spektr at the moment of take-off. We have a proven product, already used by some of the largest and fastest-moving financial companies like Santander, Pleo, Phantom, and many more. Backed by Northzone, Seedcamp and PSV, we are scaling incredibly fast and changing the way compliance works in real time. The work is ambitious, the bar is high, and the opportunity for personal and professional growth is huge.

THE OPPORTUNITY

We are looking for an AI Engineer who is, first and foremost, a product builder. In this role, you won't just be running experiments in a notebook; you will own the end-to-end AI strategy and its direct impact on our customers.

In this role, you will own everything related to AI, LLMs, and intelligent automation.

This is a high-impact role in a fast-growing company. We are looking for someone who thrives in a dynamic environment, loves solving complex compliance puzzles, and is eager to shape the next generation of compliance technology.

What You Will Be Doing

AI Strategy & Ownership: Own the end-to-end AI roadmap—from selecting the right models (SOTA commercial vs. fine-tuned open source) to deciding how they are integrated into the product core.

Agentic Feature Design: Design and deploy LLM-powered features including autonomous agents, advanced RAG pipelines, and intelligent document understanding.

Product-Led AI: Collaborate directly with founders to translate complex compliance "puzzles" into scalable AI solutions. You own the "Why" behind every model choice.

Continuous Innovation: Stay at the bleeding edge of AI/ML research, proactively identifying new papers or releases that can give spektr a competitive advantage.

Engineering for Reliability: Turn non-deterministic AI outputs into deterministic product value. You ensure our agents are accurate, explainable, and hallucination-free.

What We Are Looking For


The Product Mindset: You are a product person at heart. You care about whether the AI actually solves the user's problem, not just if the loss curve is going down.
High-Agency Builder: You prefer building systems over following manuals. You identify bottlenecks in our AI workflows and fix them before they become tickets.
AI/ML Expertise: 2+ years of experience in ML, AI, or Data/Backend roles with a deep understanding of modern LLM stacks.
Technical Mastery: Strong skills in TypeScript (our core) and/or Python, with hands-on experience in frameworks like LangChain or equivalent orchestration layers.
RAG & Vector Mastery: You’ve built production-grade RAG pipelines and understand the nuances of embedding, retrieval strategy, and re-ranking.
Evaluation Obsession: You understand that "it works on my machine" doesn't apply to LLMs. You have experience setting up evaluation frameworks to measure and improve model performance.
Cloud & Scale: Experience deploying AI services in AWS or containerized environments, ensuring they are scalable and cost-effective.
Communication: You can explain the trade-offs of a specific model architecture to a founder and a technical bottleneck to a peer with equal clarity.


OUR TECH INTERESTS


Models: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and Open Source (LLaMA, Mistral).
Stack: TypeScript, Python, LangChain, etc
Infra: AWS, Kubernetes, CI/CD for LLMs.

  • AI/ML Expertise: 2+ years of experience in ML, AI, or Data/Backend roles with a deep understanding of modern LLM stacks.
  • Technical Mastery: Strong skills in TypeScript (our core) and/or Python, with hands-on experience in frameworks like LangChain or equivalent orchestration layers.
  • RAG & Vector Mastery: You’ve built production-grade RAG pipelines and understand the nuances of embedding, retrieval strategy, and re-ranking.
  • Evaluation Obsession: You understand that "it works on my machine" doesn't apply to LLMs. You have experience setting up evaluation frameworks to measure and improve model performance.
  • Cloud & Scale: Experience deploying AI services in AWS or containerized environments, ensuring they are scalable and cost-effective.
  • Communication: You can explain the trade-offs of a specific model architecture to a founder and a technical bottleneck to a peer with equal clarity.

OUR TECH INTERESTS

  • Models: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and Open Source (LLaMA, Mistral).
  • Stack: TypeScript, Python, LangChain, etc
  • Infra: AWS, Kubernetes, CI/CD for LLMs.

Title: Dataanalytiker med stærke Python-kompetencer til Team Risikoanalyser
Company: Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø
Description:
Er du drevet af at omsætte komplekse datasæt til præcise risikovurderinger? Vil du være med til at sikre den grønne omstilling gennem avancerede statistiske modeller? Så er det dig, vi leder efter.

I Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø står vi over for en omfattende opgave: At sikre et rent vandmiljø og gennemføre en historisk omlægning af landbrugsarealer til natur og skov. En central del af denne opgave er at sikre en effektiv og korrekt implementering af den grønne trepart, og derfor ønsker vi nu at øge vores analysekapacitet.

Vi søger derfor en analytisk stærk profil til enheden Kvalitet & Risikoanalyse, som kan bringe avancerede programmeringsteknikker og statistisk metodik i spil for at udvikle fremtidens analyse- og kontrolmodeller.

Din rolle: Fra rådata til intelligent risikoanalyse
Som vores nye dataanalytiker bliver du som noget af det første ansvarlig for at bidrage til løsningen af en specifik tværgående analyseopgave. Gennem vores onboarding-program bliver du introduceret til dine primære ansvarsområder, som vil omfatte:

Avanceret databehandling og automatisering: Du vil bruge Python til at automatisere komplekse arbejdsgange, håndtere store datamængder og transformere rådata til brugbare analyseresultater.
Statistisk modellering og analyse: Du vil udføre dybdegående analyser, der understøtter både den daglige kontrol og den overordnede politikudvikling i styrelsen.
Dokumentation og formidling: Du omsætter komplekse tekniske resultater til letforståelig dokumentation og informationsmateriale til både interne interessenter og eksterne partnere, herunder EU-kommissionen.


Din profil: Analytisk dybde og teknisk ekspertise
Vi forestiller os, at du har en relevant kandidatuddannelse inden for f.eks. statistik, data science, økonomi eller lignende kvantitative discipliner. Derudover lægger vi vægt på, at du har:

Stærke Python-kompetencer: Du er fortrolig med at bruge Python til datamanipulation, statistisk modellering og automatisering af analyseprocesser.
Metodisk fundering: Du har en dyb forståelse for statistisk analyse og erfaring med udvikling af prædiktive modeller.
Data-drevet mindset: Du er vant til at arbejde systematisk og struktureret med store, komplekse datasæt og kan navigere i både ustrukturerede og strukturerede data.
Programmeringskompetencer: Udover Python har du erfaring med SQL og herudover gerne kendskab til SAS Enterprise Guide.
Kommunikativ styrke: Du kan formidle tekniske konklusioner klart og præcist, både skriftligt og mundtligt, til kolleger med forskellige faglige baggrunde.


Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med projektledelse og kendskab til forvaltning af EU-regler på miljø- og landbrugsområdet.

Vi tilbyder
Du bliver en del af Team Risikoanalyser i enheden Kvalitet & Risikoanalyse, som består af ca. 10 medarbejdere med forskellig baggrund. Vi arbejder i et miljø, hvor vidensdeling og gensidig sparring er i højsædet, og hvor du indgår i et tæt samarbejde med andre enheder i styrelsen. Som en del af Team Risikoanalyser vil du erfare, at du er med i opgaveløsningen fra start til slut, og det betyder, at du bidrager til hele processen og ikke kun en isoleret delmængde.

Vi tilbyder en fast stilling med stor faglig indflydelse, hvor dine analyser direkte bidrager til den grønne omstilling af Danmark.

Ansættelsesvilkår
Du vil blive ansat som fuldmægtig efter gældende overenskomst for akademikere i staten.

Ansættelsesområdet er Ministeriet for Natur og Dyrevelfærd med tilhørende institutioner, og tjenestestedet er Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø, i Nyropsgade 30. 1780 København V.

Stillingen ønskes besat snarest muligt.

Kontakt
Vil du vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte kontorchef Henrik Berg på telefon 25 16 82 14.

Du kan læse mere om styrelsen på www.sgav.dk

Ansøgning
Søg stillingen ved at sende en ansøgning inkl. CV, eksamensbevis og evt. referencer online i vores rekrutteringssystem (tryk på ”Søg stilling”) senest mandag den 27. juli 2026.

Vi forventer at afholde ansættelsessamtaler i uge 32.

Skills
Risk Management
Data Analytics
Python (Programming Language)
Statistical Data Analysis
Programming
SQL
Administration
EU Law
Communication
Project Management

Title: Centre of Excellence Lead — Data Science & Machine Learning
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Too Good To Go, we have an ambitious goal: to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together.

40% of all food produced in the world is wasted. And that has a huge impact on the health of our planet, with 10% of greenhouse gas emissions coming from food waste.

We're more than an app: we are a certified B Corporation with a mission to empower everyone to take action against food waste, so alongside our marketplace app, we create educational tools, explore new business solutions — such as our Retail Technologies offering — and influence legislation to help reduce food waste.

We're growing fast: Our community of 120 million registered users and 180,000 active partners across 20 countries have together already prevented 500+ million meals from going to waste

Your mission

We are looking for a Centre of Excellence Lead to build and grow the data science and machine learning capability at the heart of our Data and Analytics organisation.

The Centre of Excellence (CoE) brings together our Data Scientists and ML Engineers into a single, centralised team that partners with Product and business domains to solve our most important problems. As its Lead, you will own the team's technical direction, raise the bar on delivery and standards, and develop the people in it while staying close enough to the work to coach hands-on.

As a marketplace, many of our most critical challenges revolve around supply-demand dynamics, user behaviour, and marketplace efficiency. Your team works on problems such as personalisation, pricing, optimisation, and forecasting, helping us move from reactive analysis to proactive, model-driven systems.

Your role


Lead and grow the CoE team of Data Scientists and ML Engineers — owning performance management, coaching, hiring, and the development of each individual
Set and raise technical standards across the team, from problem framing and methodology through to experimentation, productionisation, and impact measurement
Stay hands-on as a player-coach — close enough to the technical work to guide design decisions, review approaches, and help unblock the team
Strengthen the DS↔ML handoff, ensuring models move from prototype to production reliably and that the two disciplines collaborate closely from the start of each initiative
Partner with Product and business leaders to identify high-impact opportunities where data science and ML can drive step-change improvements, and translate them into a focused team roadmap
Represent the CoE to Product, Engineering, and leadership stakeholders, making the case for where and how advanced methods create the most value
Partner with the People team to define the capability framework for the data science and ML domain
Champion the responsible use of AI within the team's ways of working, advocating for best practices and integrating new tools where they add genuine value


Requirements


Solid experience leading technical teams of a similar size (around 5 people), including performance management and developing engineers or scientists
A strong technical foundation in data science or machine learning — you have done the technical work yourself and can credibly coach it
Comfortable across both disciplines: you don't need to have managed both Data Scientists and ML Engineers, but you should have enough fluency in each to set direction and earn the team's trust
Track record of delivering ML or DS work that reached production and created measurable business or product impact
Strong grounding in experimentation (A/B testing), measurement, and impact evaluation
Experience with applied problems such as personalisation/recommendation, optimisation, pricing, or forecasting
A pragmatic, current view on AI its maturity, where it adds value, and how to integrate it responsibly into a team's workflow
Strong stakeholder and communication skills — able to influence Product, Engineering, and business leaders
Comfort operating in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a high degree of ownership
Fluent in English, with excellent written and verbal communication skills


Our values


We win together: Food waste is a big beast to fight. We believe in a #oneteam.
We raise the bar: We always push for more. We work smart, smash barriers and elevate one another.
We keep it simple: Our ambitions are bold but our solutions are simple.
We build a legacy: We're proud of the change we're driving.
We care: We always look out for each other. Caring is also about the way we do business. We do the right thing.


What we have to offer


An opportunity to work in a global social-impact company and certified B Corporation, where you can see a real and tangible impact in your role.
The chance to build and lead a centralised data science and ML capability from an early stage, shaping how advanced methods drive decisions across the company.
Work-life balance is important to us! Focus on the job to be done, not the hours spent — there is no need for overtime.
We trust our employees and encourage an autonomous environment with several opportunities to contribute, develop, and take ownership.
To be part of an international company, with over 1,200+ colleagues across 20 countries on the same important mission.
An informal environment, working alongside 170+ passionate people within Tech & Product.
We celebrate our differences and our successes and have a strong values-driven team culture.
We work in a hybrid set-up (4 days per week in our Copenhagen office) and value in-office work weekly.
We may be able to support you with a working visa in Copenhagen, please be prepared to discuss this topic further at your first-stage interview.


How to apply


We take recruitment very seriously, so please carefully read everything we have written above. Please also check our website and international media to get a good overview of Too Good To Go.
Submit your CV in English.
Please note that we only accept applications coming through our platform. No CV will be accepted by email or LinkedIn direct messaging.


A Movement for Everyone

We want to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together. With that mission, it’s only natural that we want to build a diverse and inclusive team of highly capable individuals who are passionate about doing things in a better way. We strongly believe we all excel and are more creative when we’re allowed to be ourselves, and we’re committed to a culture where all of us belong.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. If you need reasonable accommodation at any point in the application or interview process, please let us know.

We want to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together. Title: AI Capability Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Copenhagen, Denmark

About The Job

Are you interested in helping shape and strengthen AI capabilities within a global financial organisation?

As an AI Capability Engineer, you will work with Saxo’s Engagement Brain — an AI driven engine for personalised, scalable client engagement. You will turn data and AI into tangible tools that our Relationship Managers and Client Service teams will rely on. In this role, you will make an impact and contribute to one of Saxo’s most significant client-facing transformations.

This position is located at Saxo’s Headquarters in Hellerup and is within the Client Experience team. This role reports directly to the Executive Director of Client Experience.

As an AI Capability Engineer, you will collaborate with Relationship Managers, Client Service Leads, and Journey Owners, to translate user feedback into product improvements. Saxo's central Data Platform team will support you on performance, governance, and shared infrastructure. You will own your capabilities and how they land in the business.

The ambition is for you to progressively deliver the wider engagement brain: from Relationship Manager tools today, Client Service tools next, to AI-driven engagement reaching clients across every Saxo channel. You will not just build the next capability; you will also help shape the pattern that Saxo’s client-facing AI grows from.

Responsibilities


Own the rollout and implementation of the first AI sales-tech capability from pilot to a hardened, centrally run product for all Saxo Relationship Managers
Build upon the same foundation to deliver additional AI capabilities for Relationship Managers, Client Service, Journey Owners, and eventually clients directly
Design and maintain the data foundation, owning the curated data model and semantic layer that AI capabilities read from
Operate AI capabilities in production with a focus on cost, quality, and dependability across all users and clients
Convert user feedback into lasting system improvements, embedding expert judgement into repeatable system rules
Ensure all output remains defensible by working with Commercial Finance, Risk, Compliance, and Legal, staying on the right side of Saxo’s execution-only advice line
Stay closely connected with users to maintain fast, high-quality feedback loops as the team and scope grow


Your profile

You have solid experience working with AI and data and understand how they can support business success. You have strong communication skills and can clearly explain technical information to commercial colleagues. You care about grounded, dependable AI products over flashy demos.


7-10 years of experience, with a minimum of 3 years’ experience within AI/ML applied to production business systems
Experience building and running data pipelines, automation, and AI capabilities to production standard
Experience with Python and modern data platforms (dbt, Snowflake, Databricks or equivalent)
Business and data fluency in trading or comparable financial services
Financial data literacy — portfolios, positions, transactions, KYC, market data
Prompt engineering at production scale — versioning, evaluation, output filtering (not experimentation)
Data privacy engineering with AI-specific considerations (GDPR)
Observability for AI systems — drift detection, cost monitoring, evaluation pipelines


Soft Skills


Direct communication – You can explain what you built, what it does, and what its limits are to various stakeholders without overselling the solution.
Comfortable working with ambiguity
Curious about the business
Judgment on when to ship vs. iterate
Ability to work with a variety of stakeholders, including CxO, Risk, and Compliance leaders, and to communicate challenges in their language
Willingness to challenge stakeholders when needed


It Will Be An Advantage If You Have


Prior experience with LLM applications in a regulated environment
Experience with agent frameworks (MCP, tool-use, retrieval-augmented generation)


Please note that due to the summer holiday period, responses will not be sent during July. Interviews are expected to be held in August.

We get curious people invested in the world

When you work at Saxo, you become a Saxonian and part of a purpose-driven organisation, where good ideas are always taken seriously, and where you can make a true impact. We are invested in your development, and you can expect a robust career from day one when you join Saxo – no matter which role you take on.

You will join 2,500 other ambitious colleagues across 11 countries and become part of an international organisation. Working in Saxo, you will get to meet colleagues from many different cultures and backgrounds, and you should know that we value diversity and inclusion and see it as a genuine source of strength to drive growth, foster innovation and position us for long-term success.

We encourage an open feedback culture and supportive team environments enabling employees to grow and fulfil their career aspirations.

When you bring passion, curiosity, drive and team spirit, your learning journey will be dynamic and your career opportunities in Saxo will be immense.

At Saxo we don’t just offer a job – we offer an opportunity to invest in your future!

How To Apply

Click here to create an account and upload your resume and a short motivation. We look forward to getting to know you better!

  • Curious about the business
  • Judgment on when to ship vs. iterate
  • Ability to work with a variety of stakeholders, including CxO, Risk, and Compliance leaders, and to communicate challenges in their language
  • Willingness to challenge stakeholders when needed

It Will Be An Advantage If You Have

  • Prior experience with LLM applications in a regulated environment
  • Experience with agent frameworks (MCP, tool-use, retrieval-augmented generation)

Please note that due to the summer holiday period, responses will not be sent during July. Interviews are expected to be held in August.

We get curious people invested in the world

When you work at Saxo, you become a Saxonian and part of a purpose-driven organisation, where good ideas are always taken seriously, and where you can make a true impact. We are invested in your development, and you can expect a robust career from day one when you join Saxo – no matter which role you take on.

You will join 2,500 other ambitious colleagues across 11 countries and become part of an international organisation. Working in Saxo, you will get to meet colleagues from many different cultures and backgrounds, and you should know that we value diversity and inclusion and see it as a genuine source of strength to drive growth, foster innovation and position us for long-term success.

We encourage an open feedback culture and supportive team environments enabling employees to grow and fulfil their career aspirations.

When you bring passion, curiosity, drive and team spirit, your learning journey will be dynamic and your career opportunities in Saxo will be immense.

At Saxo we don’t just offer a job – we offer an opportunity to invest in your future!

How To Apply

Click here to create an account and upload your resume and a short motivation. We look forward to getting to know you better!

Title: Scientific Software Developer — Data Reduction Team
Place: Kongens Lyngby, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Scientific Software Developer — Data Reduction Team




The European Spallation Source (ESS) is building the world’s most powerful neutron source, enabling research in materials science, energy, health, and the environment. ESS is expanding the Data Reduction Team at the Data Management and Scientific Computing Centre (DMSC), located just north of Copenhagen in Denmark. We are seeking outstanding candidates for one scientific software developer position.




About the team




We are six developers responsible for the software that turns raw detector data into scientifically usable datasets. This process, known as data reduction, produces structured datasets for downstream analysis software. We develop Scipp (scipp.github.io) and related libraries, working primarily in Python. With ten new ESS instruments coming online next year, our software will play an important role in supporting their scientific commissioning and operations. As a small team, we work on problems that are both technically challenging and central to the success of the facility.




About the role




The work spans library development, scientific workflow design, performance engineering, and user interfaces. You will work closely with neutron scientists, distil requirements from them, determine what belongs in the core library versus a downstream package, and frequently decide what not to build. There is room to specialize over time, but in your first year you should expect to touch various parts of the codebase.




Our team uses AI coding tools heavily but tries to be smart about it. To us, using AI is about amplifying human thought, not outsourcing it. We see these tools as aids for exploration, iteration, and productivity — not replacements for engineering judgement.




About you




We care more about how you think than which tools you have used. We do not have a fixed technology checklist. What matters:




You have shipped software that real users depend on.
You think critically about code quality and welcome constructive discussion and alternative ideas
You can engage with scientific problems on their own terms — a physics background is welcome but not required.
You can take ownership of an area, work with scientists to gather requirements, and make decisions with incomplete information.
You are comfortable working in English in an international team.




Experience with the scientific Python ecosystem, performance engineering, or work at neutron/photon sources is beneficial but not required. Familiarity with technologies and practices such as Jupyter, C++, CI/CD, integration testing, or systems architecture would also be valuable.




What we offer

DMSC is located on the DTU campus in Kongens Lyngby, north of Copenhagen, while the ESS neutron facility is based in Lund, Sweden. Together, they form a unique cross-border scientific environment in the Øresund region.




You will join a dynamic and highly collaborative international workplace with colleagues representing nearly 60 nationalities across ESS and DMSC. We offer opportunities for professional development, and close interaction between computational science, instrumentation, and large-scale experiments.




Benefits include 25 + 5 annual leave days in addition to public and company holidays, an attractive pension scheme, preventative healthcare benefits, and relocation support for international hires where applicable.




This is a full-time, permanent position based in Kongens Lyngby, Denmark, and we need someone who can start as soon as practically possible.




Learn more about living and working in Scandinavia: https://ess.eu/living-working-scandinavia.

For further information




For information about the position, contact the Group Leader for Research Software Engineering (RSE), Fredrik Bolmsten, at fredrik.bolmsten@ess.eu




For recruitment-related questions, contact Recruitment Officer Janne Erwolter at janne.erwolter@ess.eu.




How to apply

Please submit your application, with cover letter and CV, no later than 26 August 2026.

As part of your cover letter, please pick one of these two questions and answer it in your application:




Tell us about a technical decision you made that you would make differently today, and what changed your mind.
Tell us about a piece of software you admire, not because of what it does, but because of how it is built, and what you learned from looking at it closely.




We’re genuinely interested in your response; it helps us understand your technical judgement and curiosity.




We look forward to receiving your application.

Title: Digital Solution Engineer – Offshore Wind Industry
Place: Vejle, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about developing, maintaining, and analysing data? Do you want to work in the offshore wind industry where your skills and expertise will help secure a sustainable future based on renewable energy? Then keep reading – Cadeler is looking for a Digital Solution Engineer with your skills and interests!

Cadeler is a global partner in the offshore wind industry, specializing in wind farm construction and maintenance. We are part of an industry that is now, more than ever, both relevant and play a critical role in the energy transition with a strong long-term outlook. As our industry continues to develop, so do we! We are now looking for a Digital Solution Engineer to help optimise our operational performance within jacking and monopile installation.

What will you do?

As our new Digital Solution Engineer, you will act as a bridge between offshore operational teams and onshore engineering by translating operational data into actionable insights. Furthermore, you will work hand-in-hand with the BI team regarding the data platform, data validation, and the development of dashboards.

Your Main Tasks Include


Analyse operational data from vessel systems, jacking logs, geotechnical data, and installation records.
Develop dashboards and visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Python-based dashboards, or similar) based on Cadeler’s data platform.
Identify trends, deviations, and performance improvement opportunities based on installation and jacking data. Support root cause analysis of operational events using data-driven methodologies.
Identify, propose, and refine engineering performance metrics in collaboration with the Operational Excellence and BI teams
Identify opportunities for performance optimization, cost reduction, and risk mitigation using data analytics.
Engage with clients when data-driven reporting or performance analysis is required


To succeed in this role

Being part of the Cadeler community means you will have a natural interest and care for the environment and our shared mission to speed up the green energy transition. Your environmental considerations and the safety of our people and the planet will be a key priority in your daily work.

We are looking for someone with a strong analytical and structured mindset, who possesses strong communication skills and is driven by curiosity and motivation to improve operations through digital solutions.

We think you will be a good match if you:


2–5 years of relevant experience
Academic degree in engineering (civil, mechanical, geotechnical, marine) or data science with strong technical/engineering exposure.
Experience with data analysis tools such as Python, Power BI, SQL, or similar.
Hands-on experience working with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake or equivalent technologies)
Experience in offshore wind, marine operations, heavy lift, or jack-up vessels is an advantage but not mandatory.
Experience working with large datasets and operational performance analysis.


Come work with us!

By becoming a Cadeler employee, you will be part of a rapidly growing company with a diverse and energetic team. We offer an exciting position focused on delivering excellence in the face of interesting new challenges, within a positive and rewarding work environment in an international company with great development possibilities.

Cadeler is headquartered in Copenhagen, but we also have offices in Vejle, UK, US, and Taiwan, as well as colleagues working offshore aboard our fleet of Wind Farm Installation Vessels (WFIV).

Meet some of our colleagues here: https://www.cadeler.com/career

Apply

Use the APPLY button to apply for this position. For data protection purposes, applications are only accepted via the link and are assessed continuously until we find the right candidate, so please don’t hesitate to send in your application as soon as possible and no later than the 10th of August. You can expect to hear back from us after that date, due to vacation.

If you have any questions regarding the position, please contact Geotechnical & Jacking Engineering Team Lead, Can Mollaibrahimoglu, on (+45) 2979 9623, and for practical matters please contact peopleandculture@cadeler.com.

Title: Data Science - Manager - Retail
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

As a Manager for Data Science consulting, you operate at the intersection of business strategy, data, and emerging AI technologies. You work closely with clients to shape their data and AI agendas—translating business challenges into actionable opportunities, advising on the right approaches, and guiding initiatives from idea through to value realization.

You bring a strong technical foundation, but your primary focus is on understanding client needs, framing problems, and designing impactful solutions. You are a trusted advisor who can connect strategy, data, technology, and organizational change—while staying hands-on enough to explore data, prototype solutions, and leverage modern AI tools.

Qualifications


Strong experience in data, AI, or analytics roles, with exposure to both technical implementation and business-facing work
Proven ability to engage clients, structure problems, and translate business needs into data/AI solutions
Proven experience in developing and deploying AI models at scale, including natural language processing (NLP), computer vision, and reinforcement learning.
Expertise in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and scikit-learn, as well as experience with cloud-based AI platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).
Strong understanding of data engineering concepts, including data pipelines and big data technologies such as Spark, Hadoop, SQL, and NoSQL.
Experience working with modern AI ecosystems (e.g., cloud platforms, LLMs, AI tools, or data platforms)
Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at multiple levels
Comfortable working in ambiguous environments, exploring new problem spaces, and expanding into adjacent domains
Demonstrated curiosity and eagerness to learn and apply new technologies, especially within the rapidly evolving AI landscape
Experience in consulting, digital transformation, or cross-functional environments is a strong plus


Additional Information

Set Yourself Apart With


Experience defining AI or data strategies for organizations
Hands-on experience with modern AI tools (e.g., generative AI, copilots, automation platforms)
Exposure to data architecture, data engineering, or product development
Track record of driving AI adoption or innovation initiatives within organizations
Advanced degree in a relevant field (e.g., data science, engineering, business, or similar)


What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for someone who is:


Equally comfortable in the boardroom and with a dataset
A translator between business ambition and technical reality
Curious, adaptable, and excited about the fast-moving AI landscape
Motivated to go beyond pure data science into broader problem-solving and innovation


Company Description

Publicis Sapient is a digital transformation partner helping established organizations get to their future, digitally enabled state, both in the way they work and the way they serve their customers. We help unlock value through a start-up mindset and modern methods, fusing strategy, consulting, and customer experience with agile engineering and problem-solving creativity. United by our core values and our purpose of helping people thrive in the brave pursuit of the next, our 20,000+ people in 53 offices around the world combine experience across technology, data sciences, consulting, and customer obsession to accelerate our clients’ businesses through designing the products and services their customers truly value.

Title: AI Technology Specialist (Senior Associate/VP) – Private Debt – Copenhagen
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We are looking for an AI Technology Specialist to join our Private Debt team in Copenhagen. This is a hands-on role for someone passionate about applying agentic AI, machine learning, automation, and modern software development to real-world decision-making processes.




You will work directly with investment professionals to design, build, and deploy AI-powered agents and applications that improve how investments are sourced, analysed, and monitored. Rather than operating as a traditional data science function, you will be embedded within the team, gaining a deep understanding of workflows and translating business challenges into scalable technology solutions.




Your responsibilities will include developing AI agents, LLM-based workflows, data pipelines, and analytical tools that automate repetitive tasks, extract insights from large volumes of structured and unstructured data, and support decision-making across the investment lifecycle. Use cases may include document intelligence, information extraction, workflow automation, predictive analytics, risk monitoring, and knowledge management.




You will work with a high degree of autonomy, owning the full lifecycle of AI initiatives - from identifying opportunities and designing solutions to development, deployment, and continuous improvement.




Responsibilities

Develop and implement AI and automation tools across the investment lifecycle, including origination, screening, underwriting support, Investment Committee material generation, and portfolio monitoring
Design AI-driven solutions to improve speed, accuracy, and scalability of investment workflows
Build or support tools for automated analysis of investment materials, financial statements, and investment memoranda
Support the drafting and structuring of Investment Committee materials using AI-assisted workflows
Enhance portfolio monitoring capabilities through automated data extraction, anomaly detection, and early-warning systems
Work closely with investment professionals to embed AI tools into day-to-day decision-making processes
Evaluate and implement external AI solutions and build internal prototypes where needed
Ensure solutions are robust, explainable, and aligned with investment risk and governance standards




Qualifications

Great technical background in AI/ML, data science, software engineering, or applied automation
Hands-on experience with LLMs, NLP, MLOps or applied machine learning in real-world workflows
Comfortable working with structured and unstructured financial data
Ability to translate investment and credit processes into scalable technical solutions
Current technology stack is based on Microsoft Azure, SQL Server Management Studio, Python, PyCharm or Visual Studio Code, Docker, GitHub, GitHub Copilot, and Copilot
Master's degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, Mathematics, Physics, Engineering or a related quantitative field, with great academic performance
Interest in financial markets and investments is a positive, but experience from the financial services industry is not a requirement
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English




About us

Capital Four is a leading credit asset management firm rooted in Scandinavian culture, investing in great companies and exceptional people to deliver attractive value. Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, with offices in New York, London, Frankfurt, Amsterdam and Stockholm, the team of 170 professionals manage €24bn on behalf of a global client base. The firm offers investment solutions within Global High Yield, Leveraged Loans, Structured Credit, Multi-Asset Credit and Private Debt. The team currently manages approximately €5bn across its Nordic and Northern European focused Private Debt mandates. For more details, please visit www.capital-four.com.

 

Contacts & application

Please apply by uploading your cover letter, CV (no photo) and grade transcript through our website: www.capital-four.com/career. Please submit your application in English and as soon as possible as interviews are conducted on a rolling basis.




All applications must be submitted through our website. We do not accept applications sent by e-mail or social media.




For any questions related to the job offering please do not hesitate to contact Martin Jacobsen (martin.jacobsen@capital-four.com) or Per Stoltze Kristensen (per.kristensen@capital-four.com).

We look forward to receiving your application.
